9.7 
CONVEYOR PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS DESIGN 
9.7.1 
General 
9.7.1.1 
Definitions: 
 
1. 
Mains: compressed air piping greater than 4 inches (100 mm) in diameter 
from the compressor or from other mains. 
 
2. 
Headers: - compressed air piping less than 4 inches (100 mm) in diameter 
tapped from a main, complete with a shut-off ball valve at the tap. 
 
3. 
Branch Headers: compressed air piping less than 2 inches (50 mm) in 
diameter tapped from a header, complete with a shut-off ball valve at the top 
e.g., a feed to an area of tooling or transfers or an air loop for tool rails. 
 
4. 
Drops: compressed air piping less than 2 inches (50 mm) in diameter 
tapped from a header or a branch header serving a specific group of 
devices, complete with shut-off ball valve at the top. 
 
5. 
JIC: Joint Industry Council 
9.7.1.2 
This Section is to be used in conjunction with the following GMS specifications.  
This Section shall supersede any conflicts between this Section and those 
specifications listed below. 
 
1. 
w-NA0 Pneumatic Standard. 
9.7.1.3 
This Section is to be used in conjunction with the following FCS sections.  This 
Section shall supersede any conflicts between this Section and those 
specifications listed below. 
 
1. 
Section 15010 Piping Abbreviations and Standards 
 
2. 
Section 15995 Piping Testing and Acceptance 
 
3. 
Section 22 05 00 Common Works Results for Plumbing 
 
4. 
Section 22 05 29 Plumbing Piping and Equipment 
 
5. 
Section 22 05 53 Identification for Plumbing Piping and Equipment 
 
6. 
Section 22 15 13 Piping - Compressed Air 
 
7. 
Section 40 46 00 Process Corrosion Protection 

 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
Page 113 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.7.1.4 
This Section is to be used in conjunction with the following GVOSS sections.  
This Section shall supersede any conflicts between this Section and those 
sections listed below. 
 
1. 
Section 2.1.7 Approved Sources of Pneumatic Equipment 
 
2. 
Section 2.3.7 General Mechanical/Fluid Considerations 
 
3. 
Section 2.3.7.12 Cylinders and Section 2.3.7.11 Valves 
 
4. 
Section 2.3.7 Pneumatic Design Guidelines 
9.7.1.5 
All piping, fittings and hangers furnished by the Contractor shall be thoroughly 
cleaned of oil, scale, rust and dirt. 
9.7.1.6 
All pneumatic devices that control the direction of air shall be labeled as to that 
function using lamacoid plates having black letters on white background. These 
plates shall be attached using drive screws. No glue of other adhesive shall be 
used. Lettering shall be visible from 10 feet (3048 mm). 
9.7.1.7 
Piping greater than 4 inches (100 mm) shall be painted and banded according to 
Section 22 05 53 Identification for Plumbing Piping and Equipment. 
9.7.1.8 
The assembly plant is equipped with compressed air at a pressure of 30 to 120 
psig. All air operated equipment shall be designed to operate at 65 psig or 4.5 
Bar. 
9.7.1.9 
All new air actuated High Lift applications shall include a pilot operated check 
valve between the flow control and the air cylinder on the load side of the circuit. 
All new counterweighted High Lift applications shall utilize dual pilot operated 
check valves. The check valve shall include remote push button trapped air 
pressure relief. Refer to GVOSS section 2.1.7 for approved pilot operated check 
valve sources. 
9.7.2 
Drawings 
9.7.2.1 
The pressure settings for all pneumatic equipment shall be indicated on the 
schematic drawings. 
9.7.2.2 
The following information shall be provided on all pneumatic circuit diagrams 
using standard JIC symbols: 
 
1. 
Flow path 
 
2. 
Line sizes and types 
 
3. 
Component Manufacturer’s model numbers 
 
4. 
Pressure 

 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
Page 114 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
 
5. 
Sequence of operation 
 
6. 
Cycle timing 
 
7. 
Valve board and valve stand layout showing: 
A. 
Valve mounting position 
B. 
Valve designations 
C. 
Valve stand location 
9.7.3 
Pipe 
9.7.3.1 
All pipe shall conform to ASTM Specification A-53. All pipe shall be Schedule 40 
black steel. 
9.7.3.2 
Minimum pipe size for standard conveyor installation shall be 3/4 inch (19 mm). 
Pipe sizes smaller than 3/4 inch (19 mm) shall be used only with written 
permission resulting from approval of a standard deviation request. 
9.7.3.3 
The branch header from the main plant header shall be sized to the demand of 
the air device(s) being served with a maximum of a 2 psig pressure drop for the 
pipe being run to the distribution manifold or distribution piping. 
9.7.3.4 
Pipe distributing air to multiple cylinders or other multiple devices shall be sized 
to operate at the maximum demand of the total cylinders and devices. 
9.7.3.5 
If the Contractor installs piping smaller than specified or as approved, he shall 
replace that piping without additional charge to the Owner and in time to 
complete the change before production launch. 
9.7.3.6 
Connection Point: 
 
1. 
Air piping shall be connected to the nearest main air supply at the truss. 
 
2. 
When tapping into an air main, the Contractor shall provide a 2 inch (50 
mm), minimum, connection with a full-size ball valve at the connection. 
 
3. 
When tapping into any air header, the Contractor shall provide a minimum 
of a full-size ball valve on all branch drops. 
 
4. 
All taps from mains and/or headers shall be made from the top of the mains 
and headers at 90 degrees. 
9.7.3.7 
Pipe Runs: 
 
1. 
All pipe runs shall be located as to not interfere with any machine 
adjustment points, controls or maintenance functions. 
 

 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
Page 115 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
2. 
All vertical pipe drops shall be run down the building column unless 
previously approved by the Project Engineer or the Field Engineer. 
 
3. 
All pipe runs on the first floor across aisles or walkways shall be trenched 
into the floor. 
 
4. 
Formed pipe ways are not permitted across aisles. Fill and patch all holes or 
trenches cut in the floor slab for pipe installations. Do not bury piping under 
the slab. All pipe runs in the floor shall be encased in concrete. 
 
5. 
All piping run on the second floor shall be installed according to the current 
practices of the Plant being serviced. 
 
6. 
All piping along or under conveyors shall be run on Unistrut mounted to the 
floor. All piping between conveyors or walkways shall be plated over. 
9.7.3.8 
Pipe Joints: 
 
1. 
All pipe ends shall be reamed to full size. All pipe threads shall be clean cut 
and tapered. All cuttings, scale and foreign material shall be removed from 
the inside of the pipe before assembly. 
 
2. 
Before final connections are made to the equipment, the pipe lines shall be 
cleaned by blowing with compressed air. Suitable protection, such as a 
burlap bag, shall be used to cover the opening to catch dirt and slag. 
 
3. 
All joints shall be made by carefully applying pipe joint compound to the 
male thread only. No sealing material shall be applied to the inside of the 
pipe or fittings and no silicon type grease shall be used in any device or 
equipment provided. 
9.7.3.9 
Pipe Support: 
 
1. 
All piping shall be properly supported by adjustable hangers or special pipe 
supports. 
 
2. 
Pipe Hangers and supports shall be installed in compliance with FCS 
Section 22 05 29 Plumbing Piping and Equipment. 
9.7.3.10 
Drip Legs 
 
1. 
The air piping system installed by this Contractor shall be designed to 
eliminate any moisture or dirt that might be in the compressed air 
distribution system from reaching any device and/or cylinder. Air mains shall 
be properly sloped to permit drainage of condensed moisture to a drip leg. 
 
2. 
Pipe drops shall be equipped with a drip leg for water and dirt at the lowest 

 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
Page 116 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
point. These drip legs shall be equipped with a ball valve for drain and for 
plant use. 
3. 
The drip leg shall be the same size as the pipe drop. 
9.7.4 
Fittings and Unions 
9.7.4.1 
All fittings for the schedule 40 piping shall be threaded, standard weight wrought 
iron, close grained. 
9.7.4.2 
All unions shall be screw type with brass ground joints. 
9.7.4.3 
All Unions shall be installed so that any air operated components can be 
removed without disturbing the balance of the piping. 
9.7.5 
Hoses 
9.7.5.1 
Hoses shall only be used on movable or portable equipment. All air hoses shall 
be as short as possible – i.e., less than 4 feet (1220 mm) – and shall be sized to 
be equal to the valve port size. 
9.7.5.2 
Hoses shall be adequately supported and shall have sufficient slack to avoid 
sharp flexing and straining. 
9.7.6 
Quick Couplings 
9.7.6.1 
Quick couplings shall be industrial grade, quick-connect type, size 3/8" for 5/16" 
inside diameter hose or 1/2" pipe at all hose connections. 
9.7.7 
Solenoid Valve 
9.7.7.1 
All solenoid valve Suppliers shall be as listed in GVOSS Section 2.1.7 Approved 
Sources of Pneumatic Equipment. 
9.7.7.2 
All solenoid valves shall be 250 series automotive sub-based mounted, 3/4 inch 
or greater, and shall be lubed for life. 
9.7.7.3 
All solenoid valves shall be single or double solenoids, two (2) position, five port. 
9.7.7.4 
All solenoid valves shall be equipped with a five pin Brad Harrison (or Owner 
approved equal) male connector wired per the standard application design 
drawing(s).   
9.7.7.5 
All solenoid valves for all existing paint areas that use lubricated solenoids and 
cylinders shall have the pilot air exhausted to the exhaust port by internal porting. 
No external tubing will be accepted. 
9.7.7.6 
Dual pressure valves shall be by approval only. 
 
 

 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
Page 117 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.7.8 
Flow Controls 
9.7.8.1 
All flow controls shall be mounted within 30 feet (9144 mm) of the device it is 
controlling. The flow controls must be accessible to adjustments and must be out 
of the path of skidded vehicles or power and free loaded or unloaded carriers. 
9.7.8.2 
Pin stops shall have two flow controls, one for each cylinder, fed from the same 
valve board and shall be adjusted to cause the pin stops to rise together and 
retract together. 
9.7.8.3 
All flow controls shall be "lockable" with double nuts or jam nuts. 
9.7.9 
Exhaust, Piping, Mufflers and Re-Classifiers 
9.7.9.1 
All exhaust ports of all lubed for life pneumatic devices shall be piped to a muffler 
of sufficient size to reduce the exhaust air noise to acceptable levels. 
9.7.9.2 
Re-classifiers shall be used on devices requiring lubrication only. 
9.7.9.3 
Paint Shop requirements: all exhaust shall be piped and vented to the outside 
atmosphere using the minimum number of vents possible. All vents shall be 
pointed down with a drip pan under the vent to catch residual liquids. 
9.7.10 
Air Cylinders 
9.7.10.1 
Air cylinders shall be medium duty, cushioned at both ends, square head, JIC 
and NFPA standard configuration, SAE-J514 port threads and shall conform to 
Ford Motor Company NC-9 Manufacturing Standards. 
9.7.10.2 
Lubed for life cylinders shall be used. A sign shall be posted at the cylinder with 
the following statement: “No Lubricant to be Added - Lubed for Life Cylinders”. 
9.7.10.3 
Unless noted otherwise in this specification, all cylinder rod clevis and rod eye 
attachments shall include a jam nut to prevent rotation. The cylinder rod style 
selected for the application shall provide sufficient thread length to accommodate 
a properly assembled rod clevis or rod eye used in combination with the jam nut.   
9.7.11 
Air Control Device Panels 
9.7.11.1 
Air control device panels – aka valve boards – shall be provided for each air 
circuit. This valve board shall contain the solenoid valves that control the air 
direction, the pressure regulator with pressure gage and the safety shut-off valve. 
9.7.11.2 
All devices shall be lubed-for-life i.e., no lubricators shall be utilized.  
9.7.11.3 
The location of the valve board(s) shall be as close to the device it operates as 
allowable but shall not interfere with the operation of the equipment. 
 
1. 
The valve board shall be accessible by maintenance without lifting any 
equipment. 
 

 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
Page 118 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
2. 
The valve board shall have clear line-of-site to the device(s) it is operating. 
 
3. 
The Valve board(s) shall not cause a pinch point between it and any moving 
equipment or vehicles. 
9.7.11.4 
The standard valve board(s) shall be 22 inches (560 mm) long and 15 inches 
(380 mm) high, constructed of 10 gage steel plate with the required holes and 
supports for the devices mounted on it. 
9.7.11.5 
The valve board(s) will be installed on a free-standing angle iron stand. 
9.7.11.6 
The valve board(s) may be stacked to a maximum of three (3) high with the filter 
unit board at the bottom, 6 inches (153 mm) clear above the floor or platform. 
See Figure No. 9.7.11.6 for typical layouts of valve boards mounted on valve 
stands. 
9.7.11.7 
No by-pass piping shall be used. 
9.7.11.8 
All Brad Harrison connectors (or Owner approved equivalent) shall have a 90-
degree connector to the solenoid valve(s) and shall be mounted so that the cord 
hangs down from solenoid valve. 
 
1. 
The solenoid valve connector may require repositioning to achieve the 90 
degree drop. 
 
2. 
The solenoid valve(s) shall be piped and wired for Port “4” to be 
Advance/Work and Port “2” to be Retract/Home.   
9.7.11.9 
All regulators shall have an air pressure gauge showing the regulated air 
pressure. These gauges shall be color coded green between 40 and 65 psig 
9.7.11.10

Pressure regulator

Two Valves - Valve Board BL-B: A two (2) valve, valve board shall have the

1. 
Lockable, vented safety shut-off valve 
 
2. 
Pressure regulator

9.7.12 
Existing Equipment 
9.7.12.1 
Existing pneumatic equipment may be reused in its existing location only if it 
meets the current standards, is in good physical condition and is approved by the 
Project Engineer or the Field Engineer for reuse. 
9.7.12.2 
Any equipment other than cylinders that must be relocated for the new conveyor 
path shall be replaced with new equipment to match the existing equipment and 
configuration unless approved by the Project Engineer or the Field Engineer. 
9.7.12.3 
All devices and cylinders not reused shall be turned over to the plant for disposal. 
9.7.12.4 
Pipe not reused shall be disposed of by the Contractor. 
9.7.13 
Testing 
9.7.13.1 
The Contractor, upon completion of his work, shall test all new or reworked 
supply piping for leaks and repair any that are present. All sensitive devices shall 
be isolated from the system using the shut-off valves or lock out valves during 
the leak test. 
9.7.13.2 
Following the leak test, the Contractor shall purge the new or reworked supply 
system at full plant air pressure with in-line pre-filters and mufflers in place for a 
minimum of two (2) minutes or until the system is clear of all dirt and sediment. 
After the purge, the Contractor shall inspect all filters and clean and/or replace if 
required. 
9.7.13.3 
All pneumatic devices and valve boards shall be factory tested for leaks and 
purged before delivery to the plant. All pneumatic devices shall be rated for 
operation from a minimum of 30 psig to a maximum of 150 psig.

9.8 
PAINT SHOP FLOOR CONVEYORS: 
For All Floor Conveyors between the exit of the ECoat Dip System and the 
beginning of the ECoat Oven are to be made of 316 stainless steel framework 
including the support legs.  316 Stainless steel center/top cover plates are 
required on all Roller Flights, Power Roll Beds and Power Roll Lift/Hold tables as 
well. 
BODY SHOP FLOOR CONVEYORS: 
 
There are two design size classifications for Body Shop floor conveyors: 
 
1. 
Skid conveyors up to 1500kg – for assembly plant skid applications, and 
 
2. 
Pallet conveyors up to 2000kg – used primarily for Body Shop pallet 
applications. 
9.8.1 
Power Roll Beds 
The Power Roll Bed (PRB) conveyor is used to transfer a single palletized or 
skidded unit on a horizontal plane. 
9.8.1.1 
The standard conveyor speeds for skid PRB conveyor applications are 60, 90, 
120, 180 and 250 FPM (nominal). For pallet PRB conveyor applications, the 
standard speeds are 90, 120, 180, 340 and 414 FPM (nominal). 
9.8.1.2 
The standard skid PRB height shall be 483 mm as measured from the 
plant/mezzanine floor to the top of roller (TOR).  The standard pallet PRB height 
shall be 600 mm TOR. 
9.8.1.3 
The center-to-center distance between rollers, for a single conveyor or between 
adjacent conveyors, shall be such that the bottom flat portion of the pallet or skid 
rail is always supported by a minimum of three rollers. The minimum overall PRB 
frame length shall be sufficient to stage a single pallet/skid plus 300 mm. 
9.8.1.4 
The roller shall be designed as specified in GVOSS Section 3.2.24.  
9.8.1.5 
The PRB shall be constructed to allow static discharge from the skid or pallet. 
i.e., static charge build-up on the skid, pallet or body is not acceptable. 
9.8.1.6 
All PRB conveyor frames shall be constructed from laser cut formed steel end-
plates and side channels, as illustrated above, and may be bolted or welded 
construction. 
9.8.1.7 
All PRBs shall be mounted to the plant floor or mezzanine using adjustable leg 
assemblies.  The height adjustment shall be 25 mm (+/-) by means of 
jackscrews. The leg assemblies shall be bolted to the frame rails and shall be 
properly braced. 
 

 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
Page 122 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.8.1.8 
All PRBs shall incorporate bolt-on lead-in side guides at the entrance end of the 
frame.  Reversible PRB applications shall have lead-in side guides mounted on 
both ends of the PRB frame. For skid applications, additional bolt-on intermediate 
side guides shall be provided the full length of the PRB frame between the 
powered rollers. For Skid Applications in a Paint Shop that have a collapsible 
skid, a bolt-on skid angle guide shall be provided on one side of the DugomRulli 
double-flanged roller, for the length of the PRB.  The bolt-on skid guides are to 
be mounted to the side framework and have slots so they can be adjusted.  
Loctite 242 is to be applied to the bolts to prevent from coming loose, after Field 
Commissioning. 
9.8.1.9 
All PRB gearmotors shall be outboard hollow shaft mount and shall be sized in 
compliance with the requirements specified in GVOSS Section 3.3.3. 
9.8.1.10 
All gearmotors for power roll beds operating at speeds greater than 20 m/min 
shall be VFD control and shall include a mechanical brake with manual release. 
9.8.1.11 
Where layout conditions require the gearmotor to be located inboard, or between 
the frame side channels, an outboard brake release handle shall be provided. 
The brake release handle shall be located such that the operator is not in the 
path of the moving load if required to release the brake.  
9.8.1.12 
The roll-to-roll power transmission shall utilize synchronous belts as specified in 
GVOSS Section 3.2.23. All belts in the drive train shall be capable of handling 
the tension applied by the gearmotor under a stall condition. 
9.8.1.13 
A safety guard shall be provided for the power transmission belt system. The 
safety guard shall be designed to be removable without use of tools. Individual 
guard sections shall not weigh more than 16kg (35 pounds) each. 
9.8.1.14 
All PRBs shall be provided with center covers and side skirts when utilized for 
production operations. 
9.8.1.15 
The PRB assembly shall include all required control devices, distributed control 
panel(s), power and control cables, etc. (as described in the applicable conveyor 
controls documentation) shop mounted directly to the conveyor frame.  
9.8.1.16 
The Contractor shall verify proper skid/pallet/skillet transfer between adjacent 
conveyors without tipping due to center-of-gravity placement and roll-to-roll 
distance. The maximum roll-to-roll distance between two separate power roll 
beds must be no more than 4'-0" (1220 mm).

9.8.2 
Turntables  
Turntable conveyors are used to change flow direction or product orientation 
when transferring a single palletized or skidded unit on a horizontal plane. 
Turntables may be "end pivot" or "center pivot" configuration. 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: Jan.10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 125 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Figure No. 9.8.2 – End- and Center-Pivot Turntables 
9.8.2.1 
The standard rotation speed for skid and pallet turntables shall be 213 FPM 
(nominal). 
9.8.2.2 
The standard skid turntable height shall be 483 mm as measured from the 
plant/mezzanine floor to the top of roller (TOR).  The standard pallet turntable 
height shall be 600 mm TOR. 
9.8.2.3 
All PRBs used with turntable conveyors shall meet the requirements identified in 
GVOSS Section 9.8.1, excluding the requirement for adjustable mounting legs. 
9.8.2.4 
The turntable conveyor is constructed from a formed steel base frame with end-
trucks, pivot-base frame and curved turning track assemblies. 
9.8.2.5 
The PRB shall be bolted to the turntable base frame utilizing clip angle 
connections that allow shimming to ensure a plumb, square and level installation. 
Clamp style connections for mounting the PRB to the turntable base are 
prohibited. 
9.8.2.6 
The turntable conveyor shall include gravity actuated over-travel stops mounted 
on the turntable PRB and the adjacent "charge" conveyor. The over-travel stops 
shall be actuated by fixed floor-/conveyor-mounted cams and shall be designed 
such that a full speed impact does not cause permanent damage to the over-
travel stop or the conveyor. 
9.8.2.7 
The turntable base frame shall include integral end-trucks for mounting the 
rotation drive, drive wheel and load bearing idler wheels. Each end-truck 
assembly shall include frame mounted wipers to clear debris from the turning 
track as the turntable rotates. 
9.8.2.8 
Each drive and idler wheel shall be oriented tangent to the turntable rotation. The 
drive and idler wheels shall be identically sized polyurethane coated steel hub 
wheels mounted on steel axles supported by anti-friction bearings. 
End Pivot Turntable 
Center Pivot Turntable 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: Jan.10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 126 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.8.2.9 
The turntable rotation drive shall utilize a hollow spline shaft gearmotor mounted 
external to the framework of the conveyor for maintenance accessibility.  The 
gearmotor shall be sized in compliance with the requirements specified in 
GVOSS Section 3.3.3. The drive wheel shall remain in contact with the turning 
track throughout all motion of the conveyor. 
 
 
Figure No. 9.8.2.9 – Turntable Rotation Drive Arrangement 
9.8.2.10 
A split UHMW pivot bearing shall be bolted to the turntable base frame. The pivot 
bearing shall be mounted to the base frame such that it is easily visible by 
maintenance personnel without disassembly. 
9.8.2.11 
The pivot base shall include a round steel pivot tube welded to an adjustable 
steel base plate. The pivot tube shall include opening(s) at the base for power 
and control cable routing through the center of the tube and a protective "collar" 
at the top of the tube to prevent cable wear during turntable rotation. The base 
plate shall be designed with a 25 mm (+/-) height adjustment by means of 
jackscrews. 
9.8.2.12 
A floor-mounted curved steel turning track shall be provided to serve as a level 
and smooth surface to support the rotation drive and idler wheels. The surface of 
the turn track must be such that it does not induce accelerated wear on the drive 
wheel but shall not have a friction magnitude less than needed to accomplish the 
rotating function. The turn track shall be supported to minimize deflection. The 
track shall be designed with a 25 mm (+/-) height adjustment by means of 
jackscrews. 
9.8.2.13 
Floor mounted shock absorbing over-travel stops shall be provided at the 
terminal ends of rotation.  The floor mounted shock absorbers shall be designed 
to withstand a full speed impact. i.e., in such a manner that a full speed impact 
does not cause permanent damage or deflection to the conveyor and shock 
absorber. 
9.8.2.14 
The turntable conveyor shall be provided with perimeter guard rail, access gate 
and hazardous warning signage in compliance with the design requirements 
identified in GVOSS Section 9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: Jan.10, 2014

9.8.3 
Cross-Belt Transfer Conveyors 
Cross-Belt (X-belt) transfer conveyors are used together with lift- and hold-tables 
to transfer palletized units to/from adjacent conveyors on a horizontal plane in a 
cross-wise orientation. All X-belt conveyors are installed above the floor and/or 
mezzanine and are two-strand configuration as illustrated below. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
(a)   Isometric View of X-belt Conveyor 
 
(b)   Plan View of Wide-Narrow-Wide X-Belt Configuration 
 
Figure No. 9.8.3 – Cross-Belt Transfer Conveyor 
9.8.3.1 
The standard conveyor belt center-to-center dimensions are 4166 mm (13 feet, 8 
inches), a.k.a. Wide X-belt, and 3150 mm (10 feet, 4 inches), a.k.a. narrow X-
belt. 
9.8.3.2 
Maximum X-belt Conveyor Length. 
 
1. 
The maximum X-belt conveyor length shall be such that pallet travel is 
limited to 29'-7 (9017 mm), resulting in a maximum center-to-center drive 
pulley to take-up pulley distance of 39’-7 (12065 mm). 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: Jan.10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 129 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
2. 
Where layout conditions require a distance greater than the maximum 
distance allowable, the X-belt system may be configured using alternating 
wide and narrow X-belt conveyors as illustrated in Figure No. 9.8.3(b). 
9.8.3.3 
The X-belt conveyor head, tail and intermediate track frame assemblies shall be 
constructed from formed steel shapes and plates.  The frames shall be designed 
such that the conveyor belt(s) can be installed and/or removed without major 
disassembly. i.e., by simply relieving the belt pretension and "collapsing" the 
sliding take-up assembly as needed to install and/or remove the conveyor belt. 
9.8.3.4 
The drive assembly shall be located at the head, or discharge end, of the X-belt 
conveyor.  Pushing the top strand of the conveyor belt is not allowed. 
9.8.3.5 
X-belt Conveyor Drive Assembly. 
 
1. 
The X-belt conveyor drive shall utilize a foot-mounted integral gearmotor 
with dual output shafts using universal-joints to connect the gearbox output 
shaft, jack shafts and driving drums. 
 
2. 
The connecting jack shafts shall be as manufactured by Elbe & Sohn Inc., 
or Owner approved equivalent. The connecting shaft manufacturer must 
approve the component calculations and final selection recommendation(s). 
The connecting shaft manufacturer approval documentation shall be 
submitted to the Owner at the engineering design review meeting. 
 
3. 
Power transmission safety guarding shall be provided to guard the potential 
entanglement hazard presented by the rotating drive shafts and associated 
universal joints. The guarding panels shall be limited to 50 lb. (23 kg) max. 
weight and shall be complete with lifting handles to assist with removal/re-
installation. 
9.8.3.6 
The driving drum shall be a polyurethane coated steel hub with integral through-
shaft supported by flange mount anti-friction bearings on both ends.  The 
polyurethane coating shall incorporate concentric lagging rings for improved 
driving traction.  The driving drums shall be fabricated and installed as "matched 
pairs" on each X-belt conveyor. 
9.8.3.7 
The X-belt conveyor head and take-up frame assemblies shall include adjustable 
drum shaft bearing mounts with jack screws sufficient to provide the necessary 
drum alignment for proper conveyor belt tracking. 
9.8.3.8 
The take-up shall incorporate compression die-springs sufficient to provide the 
required belt pre-tension necessary to prevent belt slippage on the driving drums 
during normal pallet transfer operation.  The take-up springs are not to be used 
for belt tracking. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: Jan.10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 130 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.8.3.9 
The intermediate track assembly shall incorporate a conveyor belt slider bed 
constructed from 1/8-inch-thick 25% glass reinforced PolyTetraFluoroEthylene 
(PTFE), as manufactured by DuPont or Owner approved equal.  The PTFE slider 
bed material shall be one continuous piece the full length of the conveyor track, 
on both sides, from drive pulley to take-up pulley. The conveyor belt shall return 
on UHMW idler rolls mounted below the slider bed. 
9.8.3.10 
Belt guides fabricated from UHMW material shall be provided on top of the slider 
bed, staggered on both sides of each conveyor belt.  The belt guides shall be 
fastened using flat head machine screws. 
 
              
 
               Figure No. 9.8.3.9 – Typical X-Belt Intermediate Track X-Section   
9.8.3.11 
The X-belt conveyor head, tail and intermediate track frame shall be designed 
with a 25 mm (+/-) height adjustment. 
9.8.3.12 
Floor-mounted Hard Stops. 
 
1. 
A floor-mounted hard stop shall be provided at the entering lift-table location 
when this lift-table is not a "pass through" type configuration. This hard stop 
is intended to prevent inadvertent transfer of the pallet off the lift-table when 
operating in automatic mode. 
 
2. 
A floor-mounted hard stop shall be provided at the exiting lift-table location 
on the side opposite from the discharge flow. This hard stop is intended to 
prevent inadvertent transfer of the pallet off the lift-table when operating in 
manual mode. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: Jan.10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 131 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.8.3.13 
Frame-mounted Hard Stops. 
 
1. 
Frame-mounted hard stops, one for each belt strand, shall be provided at 
the discharge lift-table location. These hard stops shall be used to position 
the pallet at the discharge lift-table for transfer to the adjacent conveyor 
when operating in automatic mode. 
 
2. 
Frame-mounted hard stops, one for each belt strand, shall be provided at 
the charge lift-table location. The hard stops at the charge end shall be used 
as over-travel stops to prevent in advert transfer of a pallet beyond the 
charge table location when operating in manual mode. 
 
3. 
The frame-mounted hard stops shall incorporate UHMW pads for impact 
and wear resistance.  The UHMW pads shall be bolted to the hard stop 
bracket to facilitate future replacement. 
9.8.3.14 
The conveyor belt shall be a continuous loop constructed from multiple braided 
steel chords with rubber cover and fabric backing. The conveyor Supplier shall 
review the X-belt application and obtain approval from the conveyor belt 
manufacturer. The conveyor belt specification(s), calculation(s), and belt 
manufacturer approval documentation must be submitted by the conveyor 
Supplier to the Owner for review/approval at the engineering design review 
meeting. 
9.8.3.15 
Proximity sensors shall be used to identify the pallet deceleration and stopping 
positions.  The controls logic shall incorporate pallet skew detection at each 
pallet stopping location. 
9.8.3.16 
Where the layout configuration requires a combination of wide and narrow X-belt 
conveyors, the controls logic shall be designed such that the adjacent X-belt 
conveyors have achieved equivalent steady-state speed before the transferring 
pallet reaches the overlapping conveyor belt region. 
9.8.3.17 
A palletized unit may stop directly on a X-belt in lieu of using a hold-table, 
however, only one pallet may transfer on an individual X-belt conveyor at any 
given time. 
9.8.3.18 
The X-Belt conveyor assembly shall include all required control devices, 
distributed control panel(s), power and control cables, etc. (as described in the 
applicable conveyor controls documentation). The proximity switches may be 
shop or field mounted directly to the conveyor frame. To facilitate belt 
removal/replacement, the distributed control panels(s), I/O blocks, splitters, 
switches, etc. shall be field installed on floor mounted stands located near the 
conveyor drive for which they are associated. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: Jan.10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 132 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.8.3.19 
X-Belt Conveyor Branch Standards List – Pallet Applications 
(1) 3ZF-36401-Pallet X-Belt Conveyor 
9.8.4 
Chain Cross-Transfer Conveyors 
9.8.4.1 
Chain Cross-Transfer X-transfer conveyors are used together with lift- and hold-
tables to transfer skidded units to/from adjacent conveyors on a horizontal plane 
in a crosswise orientation.  X-transfer conveyors may use steel (CC5) conveyor 
chain or modular plastic belt (MPB) conveyor chain, depending on the 
application. 
9.8.4.1.1 
CC5 X-transfer conveyors are used for skid applications, only. CC5 X-transfers 
are prohibited for use on pallet delivery and production conveyor applications. 
CC5 X-transfer conveyors may be above floor mounting configuration for typical 
delivery and production operations or “flush” floor for skid aisle crossing conveyor 
applications (see GVOSS section 9.8.5). 
9.8.4.1.2 
MPB X-transfer conveyors are used for skid and pallet applications. MPB X-
transfers are prohibited for use on skid aisle crossing conveyor applications. 
MPB X-transfer conveyors are used in above floor mounting configuration for 
delivery conveyor applications. 
9.8.4.1.3 
CC5 and MPB X-transfer conveyors may be 2- or 3-strand configuration. 
9.8.4.2 
Accumulation on X-transfer conveyors is accomplished by stopping the skidded 
unit on lift-/hold-tables and, where layout conditions allow stopping directly before 
a lift-/hold-table on the conveyor chain.  Only one skidded unit shall stop directly 
on any given X-chain conveyor at a given time. 
9.8.4.3 
Two-strand configuration X-transfer conveyors are used to transfer common 
length skids.   For excessively long skids, center deflection should be determined 
to verify it is not an issue for lift-/hold-table clearance. 
9.8.4.4 
X-transfer conveyors transporting various length skids may require a three-strand 
configuration. The conveyor Supplier shall be responsible to design the three-
strand center-to-center chain configuration to suit the specific project 
requirements. 
9.8.4.5 
All three-strand X-transfer conveyor track assemblies at lift-/hold-table locations 
must be designed utilizing bolted construction to be easily removable for 
maintenance access if needed. 
9.8.4.6 
X-transfer conveyor drive assembly location. 
9.8.4.6.1 
The drive assembly for CC5 X-transfer conveyors shall be located at the head, or 
discharge end, of the X-chain conveyor. Pushing the top strand of the CC5 X-
transfer conveyor chain is acceptable only for special applications – i.e., sloped 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: Jan.10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 133 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
conveyors, lift-chain applications, etc. – and only with prior written approval from 
the Owner. 
9.8.4.6.2 
The drive assembly for MPB X-transfer conveyors may be located at the head 
end (for uni-direction flow) or center of the conveyor (for bi-directional flow), 
depending on the application. 
9.8.4.7 
For above-floor X-transfer conveyor applications, the conveyor chain drive unit 
shall utilize a foot-mounted integral gearmotor with dual output Cardan shafts 
using universal-joints on both ends (see GVOSS 3.2.27) to connect the gearbox 
output shaft, jack shafts and driving sprockets, similar to the X-belt conveyor 
design. 
9.8.4.8 
X-transfer conveyor chain. 
9.8.4.8.1 
For above-floor CC5 chain X-transfer delivery conveyor applications, the 
conveyor chain shall be CC-5 with UHMW roller, where duty cycle permits usage 
based on chain pull and UHMW roller load limitations. Otherwise, the standard 
CC-5A chain with steel roller shall be used. 
9.8.4.8.2 
For above floor MPB chain X-transfer conveyor applications, the conveyor chain 
shall be Uni-Chain Part No. NL-SS-XLB-00-K800 8-inch wide chain with stainless 
steel connecting pins, or Owner approved equivalent. The conveyor supplier 
shall work with the MPB chain supplier to review and approve the application 
design prior to starting fabrication. The conveyor supplier shall provide the MPB 
chain supplier approval documentation at the conveyor design review meeting. 
9.8.4.9 
X-transfer conveyor take-up. 
9.8.4.9.1 
All CC5 X-transfer conveyor take-ups shall be adjustable screw type for all X-
transfer conveyor applications. Each chain strand shall be individually adjusted. 
9.8.4.9.2 
All MPB X-transfers shall utilize a catenary type take-up on each bottom chain 
return section of track. The conveyor supplier shall work with the MPB chain 
supplier to review and approve the catenary take-up design prior to starting 
fabrication. The conveyor supplier shall provide the MPB chain supplier approval 
documentation at the conveyor design review meeting. Each tail shaft assembly 
shall include threaded rods for adjusting the top strand tension. 
9.8.4.10 
X-transfer conveyor track   
9.8.4.10.1 
All CC5 X-transfer conveyor track shall include a Sure-Alloy, or Owner approved 
equivalent, high-carbon steel wear bar on the top load bearing and bottom return 
tracks. 
9.8.4.10.2 
All MPB X-transfer conveyor track shall include a chain slider bed fabricated from 
Uni-Chain “Unicon” material, or Owner approved equivalent. The conveyor 
supplier shall provide the MPB chain supplier approval documentation at the 
conveyor design review meeting. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: Jan.10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025

 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.8.4.11 
A removable guard shall be provided over the rotating drive shaft in compliance 
with the requirements of GVOSS Section 9.4 Safety Requirements for 
Conveyors. 
9.8.4.12 
See GVOSS paragraph 9.8.3.12 for X-transfer floor-mounted hard stop design 
requirements. 
9.8.4.13 
See GVOSS paragraph 9.8.3.13 for X-transfer frame-mounted hard stop design 
requirements.

9.8.5 
Aisle Crossing X-Chain Conveyors 
9.8.5.1 
Aisle crossing X-chain conveyor designs shall comply with the specifications 
identified in Section 9.8.4 with the additional requirements described within this 
section. 
9.8.5.2 
The flush-floor mounting configuration shall provide a 25 mm (1 inch) top-of-
chain elevation. 
9.8.5.3 
The conveyor drive sprockets shall be mounted to a common head shaft 
supported on a single modularly constructed frame, including the gearmotor 
mounting base. 
9.8.5.4 
The conveyor chain shall be standard CC-5A and must be capable of 
withstanding the forces applied by all aisle traffic in the dynamic load condition. 
9.8.5.5 
Support steel and cover plate shall be provided, mounted to the conveyor and pit 
curb angle, at the aisle crossing(s) and shall extend a minimum of 6 feet (1829 
mm) from the aisle line, both sides.  The framework and cover plate at the aisle 
crossing(s) must withstand the forces applied by all aisle traffic. 
9.8.5.6 
The floor area adjacent to the aisle crossing between the open pit area and the 
aisle shall be painted with black and yellow "tiger stripes".  The minimum width of 
this area shall be 6 feet (1829 mm) on each side. 
9.8.5.7 
A highway style guard shall be installed around the perimeter of the exposed pit 
area.  The highway guard shall be anchored to the plant floor to allow removal for 
maintenance access, if needed. 
 
                  
 
       Figure No. 9.8.5.7 – Elevation View of Aisle Crossing X-Chain Perimeter Guard

SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 136 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.8.5.8 
Openings in the highway guard shall be provided to allow skid transfer to/from 
the adjacent conveyors. The opening width shall be sufficient to provide 457 mm 
(18 inches) minimum clear to the widest point of the body-on-skid, both sides. 
The gap between the highway guard and the adjacent charge/discharge 
conveyor shall include a 457 mm (18 inches) high "knee knocker" personnel 
barrier to prevent inadvertent access into the Aisle Crossing X-Chain conveyor. 
9.8.5.9 
A sloped ramp type pinch point guard shall be installed in the pit on the leading 
side of the aisle crossing, from the pit floor to the plant finish floor. 
9.8.5.10 
Accordion style skirting shall be provided below/around all Stroke-Arm and Side-
Arm high lift-tables. 
9.8.5.11 
Access stairs from each side of the aisle shall be provided for each pit area

9.8.6 
Power Roll Lift Tables and Hold Tables 
Lift- and hold-tables are used with cross-transfer conveyors to provide 
accumulation and transition between cross-transfers and the adjacent 
charge/discharge conveyors. 
 
 
 
Figure No. 9.8.6 – Lift- and Hold-Tables 
9.8.6.1 
Lift- and hold-tables are constructed from two major sub-assemblies: (1) a base 
frame and (2) a lift frame.  These frames are assembled using formed steel 
shapes and plates and may be bolted or welded construction. The lift- and hold-
table designs provided on the project shall maximize use of common 
components. e.g., common base frames, lift arms, power transmission belts, etc.  
9.8.6.2 
The lift-table shall be designed so as to not tip when the conveyed load is 
concentrated over the end roller(s) as it transfers on/off the lift-table. 
9.8.6.3 
All lift- and hold-tables shall be "eccentric arm" lifting design. The base frame 
shall incorporate cam followers assembled to shaft mounted eccentric arms.  The 
eccentric lift arm shafts shall be supported by pillow block mounted anti-friction 
bearings with jack bolts and shim pack for proper lift belt installation and 
alignment. 
9.8.6.4 
The base frame shall include vertical guides to maintain the hold/roller frame 
front-to-rear and side-to-side location. 
9.8.6.5 
The lifting and lowering action shall be accomplished using an integral electric 
gearmotor with "fast acting" brake.  Pneumatic cylinders for lift-/hold-tables shall 
be used only when identified in the project bid specifications to match existing 
designs and with prior written approval of the Owner. The lifting action shall be 
continuous direction. i.e., the eccentric arms will not reciprocate to complete the 
lifting motion. 
9.8.6.6 
Power shall be transmitted from the gearmotor to the eccentric lift arms using 
synchronous belt design as specified in GVOSS Section 3.2.20. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: Jan.10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 139 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.8.6.7 
For skid applications, skid stops shall be designed as stand-alone assemblies 
separate from the lift frame. These lift-/hold-tables shall be designed for a two-
position lift to facilitate stopping and transferring skids: i.e., (1) lower "clear" 
position, (2) upper "transfer" position. 
9.8.6.8 
For pallet applications, integral skid stops are not required on the lift frame. 
These lift-/hold-tables shall be designed for a two-position lift to facilitate stopping 
and transferring pallets: i.e., (1) lower "clear" position and (2) the upper "transfer" 
position. 
9.8.6.9 
The lift frame shall be modular design. i.e., powered rollers and holding slats 
shall be interchangeable within the same lift frame side channel design. The PRB 
design shall be incompliance with requirements specified in GVOSS Section 
9.8.1. 
9.8.6.10 
The lift frame shall include cam rollers mounted to the endplates that act upon 
base frame mounted vertical guides to maintain the front-to-rear and side-to-side 
location.  The guide rollers mounting bracket shall be adjustable to allow proper 
alignment with the guides and shall be drilled and pinned, or blocked, to the 
endplate after all adjustments have been made following final installation at the 
plant site. 
9.8.6.11 
Skid guides shall be provided on the outboard side of the skid rails for lift- and 
hold-tables when used with built-up rigid frame skid assemblies. i.e., skid rails 
are connected to each other using welded or bolted crossbars.  Skid guides shall 
be provided on the inboard and outboard side of both skid rails for lift- and hold-
tables used with "independent" skid rail systems. i.e., skid rails are not connected 
other than through the body or are connected using "drop on" crossbars. 
9.8.6.12 
Pallet guides shall be provided on the outboard side of the pallet side rails on 
both lift- and hold-tables.  
9.8.6.13 
All lift-/hold-tables shall be designed with a mechanical means of locking the load 
to control gravity and ensure safe maintenance access to areas under the 
elevated portion of the equipment and parts. All new gravity block, pin and 
receiver designs shall comply with GVOSS section 9.4.11 unless noted otherwise 
within this specification. 
 
1. 
Each X-Chain or X-belt transfer conveyor shall include a single gravity pin 
storage stand designed to stow the quantity of pins necessary for an 
individual eccentric arm lift-/hold-table. This set of gravity pins are intended 
to be used for all eccentric-arm lift- and hold-tables included with the 
associated X-transfer conveyor. 
 
2. 
The eccentric-arm lift/hold table gravity pin storage stand shall be located 
near the transfer conveyor drive assembly and shall include interlocks such 
that all lifting/lowering/ transferring motion is prevented if the pins are not 
placed in the normally stored location. 
 
3. 
Eccentric Arm High Lifts, Side Arm High Lifts and Thompson High Lifts shall 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: Jan.10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 140 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
be provided with gravity blocks or pins with storage stands for each 
individual lift. The gravity blocks/pins shall be stored outside the perimeter 
fence at a location convenient to the gravity pin receiver access locations. 
 
4.     The gravity pin receiver hole shall be designed to account for the lift chain 
stretch/wear-in and controls device up/down positioning tolerance. 
            
 
               Figure No. 9.8.6.13 – Lift-/Hold-Table Gravity Control Pin 
            (2000kg Class Lift-/Hold-Table shown for reference only) 
9.8.6.14 
All lift-/hold-tables shall be designed to minimize pinch points.  All pinch points 
shall be guarded in compliance with the current safety standards and 
specifications. 
9.8.6.15 
The lift-/hold-table assembly shall include all required control devices, distributed 
control panel(s), power and control cables, etc. (as described in the applicable 
conveyor controls documentation) shop mounted directly to the X-Transfer 
conveyor frame or floor mounted adjacent to the lift-/hold-table.

9.8.7 
Shuttle Cart Cross-Transfer Conveyor 
9.8.7.1 
Shuttle cart conveyors may be used in lieu of short travel X-belt and X-chain 
transfer conveyors. The maximum loaded shuttle cart transfer speed is 214 FPM 
(nominal). 
9.8.7.2 
The shuttle cart conveyor is constructed from a formed steel base frame with 
integrally mounted end-trucks, PRB frame and transfer track assemblies. 
9.8.7.3 
All PRBs used with shuttle cart conveyors shall meet the requirements identified 
in GVOSS Section 9.8.1, excluding the requirement for adjustable mounting legs. 
9.8.7.4 
The PRB shall be bolted to the shuttle cart base frame utilizing clip angle 
connections that allow shimming to ensure a plumb, square and level installation. 
Clamp style connections for mounting the PRB to the shuttle cart base are 
prohibited. 
9.8.7.5 
The shuttle cart conveyor shall include gravity actuated over-travel stops 
mounted on the shuttle cart PRB and the adjacent "charge" conveyor. The over-
travel stops shall be actuated by fixed floor-/conveyor-mounted cams and shall 
be designed such that a full speed impact does not cause permanent damage to 
the over-travel stop or the conveyor. 
9.8.7.6 
The shuttle cart base frame shall include integral end-trucks for mounting the 
transfer drive, drive wheels and load bearing idler wheels. Each end-truck 
assembly shall include frame mounted wipers to clear debris from the turning 
track as the shuttle cart transfers. 
9.8.7.7 
Each drive and idler wheels shall be oriented perpendicular to the shuttle cart 
transfer. The drive and idler wheels shall be identically sized polyurethane coated 
steel hub wheels mounted on steel axles supported by anti-friction bearings. The 
drive wheels shall remain in contact with the turning track throughout all motion 
of the conveyor. 
9.8.7.8 
The shuttle cart transfer drive shall incorporate a foot-mounted integral 
gearmotor/brake assembly with dual output shafts using universal-joints to 
connect the gearbox output shaft, jack shafts and driving wheels. The transfer 
drive shall be mounted external to the shuttle cart base frame for maintenance 
accessibility.  The gearmotor shall be sized in compliance with the requirements 
specified in GVOSS Section 3.3.3. 
9.8.7.9 
A floor-mounted transfer track shall be provided to serve as a level and smooth 
surface to support the drive and idler wheels. The surface of the track must be 
such that it does not induce accelerated wear on the drive wheels but shall not 
have a friction magnitude less than needed to accomplish the transfer function. 
The transfer track shall be supported to minimize deflection. The track shall be 
designed with a 25 mm (+/-) height adjustment by means of jackscrews. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 146 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.8.7.10 
Floor mounted shock absorbing over-travel stops shall be provided at the 
terminal ends of transfer.  The floor mounted shock absorbers shall be designed 
to withstand a full speed impact. i.e., in such a manner that a full speed impact 
does not cause permanent damage or deflection to the conveyor and shock 
absorber. 
9.8.7.11 
Highly flexible power and control cables shall be used to provide electrification of 
all equipment mounted on the shuttle cart.  The cables shall be individually 
secured in a "cat track" designed for the specific transfer application and in 
compliance with the requirements identified in applicable sections of the GVOSS 
specification. 
9.8.7.12 
The shuttle cart conveyor shall be provided with perimeter guard rail, access gate 
and hazardous warning signage in compliance with the design requirements 
identified in GVOSS Section 9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors. 
9.8.7.13 
The shuttle cart X-Transfer assembly shall include all required control devices, 
distributed control panel(s), power and control cables, etc. (as described in the 
applicable conveyor controls documentation) shop mounted directly to the 
conveyor frame.

9.8.8 
Aisle-Crossing Shuttle Cart  
9.8.8.1 
Aisle-crossing shuttle cart conveyors are used in lieu of short flush-floor aisle-
crossing cross-transfers.  The maximum shuttle cart travel shall be limited to 
10,287 mm (33 feet, 9 inches). The standard shuttle cart speeds shall be 150 
FPM (max.) when loaded and 300 FPM (max.) when empty. 
9.8.8.2 
The shuttle cart is constructed from three major sub-assemblies: (1) the transfer 
assembly, (2) the cart assembly and (3) the lifting table assembly. These 
assemblies are fabricated using formed steel shapes and plates and may be 
bolted or welded construction. 
9.8.8.3 
The transfer carriage assembly shall include trolleys with horizontal and vertical 
guide wheels that travel on a high-carbon steel channel track assembly.  The 
guide wheels shall be polyurethane coated steel hubs with integral "lube for life" 
anti-friction bearings mounted on steel shafts. 
9.8.8.4 
The transfer head, tail and intermediate track shall be mounted in a pit below the 
plant finished floor. 
9.8.8.5 
Support steel and removable steel checker plate shall be provided at the transfer 
track head and tail sections. Support steel and removable cover plate shall be 
provided the full length of the intermediate track mounted to the intermediate 
track and pit curb angle. The intermediate track framework and cover plate at the 
aisle crossing must be designed to withstand the forces applied by all aisle traffic. 
9.8.8.6 
The intermediate transfer track shall include maintenance access gates at both 
ends of travel to facilitate removal of the trolley assemblies if needed. 
9.8.8.7 
Two flat conveyor belts shall be connected to the transfer assembly to provide 
horizontal motion.  The conveyor belts shall be sized to provide a minimum 1.25 
service factor each, for a combined 2.5 service factor.  The conveyor Supplier 
shall review the shuttle cart conveyor belt application and obtain approval from 
the belt manufacturer. The conveyor belt specification(s), calculation(s), and belt 
manufacturer approval documentation shall be submitted by the conveyor 
Supplier to the Owner for review/approval at the engineering design review 
meeting. 
9.8.8.8 
The transfer assembly conveyor belts shall be driven by an integral gearmotor 
with brake. The gearmotor and brake shall be sized in compliance with the 
requirements specified in GVOSS Section 3.3.3. 
9.8.8.9 
A synchronous belt transmission shall be used to power the driving drum which 
shall be assembled to the head shaft using a shrink-disk type bushing. i.e., 
Ringfeder, B-Loc or Owner approved equivalent. 
9.8.8.10 
A main and standby transfer drive assembly shall be provided, unless otherwise 
identified in the project bid specifications. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 150 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.8.8.11 
The transfer assembly, head and take-up assemblies shall include adjustable 
drum shaft bearing mounts with jack screws sufficient to provide the necessary 
drum alignment for proper conveyor belt tracking. 
9.8.8.12 
The take-up shall incorporate a separate belt tension assembly for each belt. The 
belt tension assembly shall be complete with compression die-springs sufficient 
to provide the required belt pre-tension necessary to prevent belt slippage on the 
driving drums during normal skid/pallet transfer operation.  Each belt shall be 
tensioned individually.  The take-up pre-tension springs are not to be used for 
belt tracking. 
9.8.8.13 
The transfer carriage shall be connected to the cart assembly using hardened 
steel pins.  The "front" pin shall insert into a receiver complete with hardened 
steel bushing mounted to the cart assembly frame.  The "rear" pin shall engage 
an adjustable spring tensioned receiver mounted on the cart assembly frame.  A 
calibrated gage shall be included with the adjustable spring tensioned receiver 
assembly. 
9.8.8.14 
The cart assembly frame shall include four load bearing Phenolic caster wheels.  
The caster wheels shall be carried on a floor track embedded in the concrete 
plant finished floor.  The floor track shall include a wear bar riding surface welded 
to an I-beam support member. 
9.8.8.15 
A floor mounted collector rail with lead-in shall be provided at both terminal ends 
of travel.  Two 4-conductor collector shoe assemblies, one on each side, shall be 
mounted to the cart assembly to provide lifting/lowering power and control to the 
lift assembly. 
9.8.8.16 
A lift- or hold-table shall be assembled to the cart frame. The lift-/hold-table shall 
be bolted to the cart assembly frame utilizing clip angle connections that allow 
shimming to ensure a plumb, square and level installation. Clamp style 
connections for mounting the lift-/hold-table to the cart assembly frame are 
prohibited. 
For 
additional 
information 
regarding 
lift-/hold-table 
design 
requirements, refer to GVOSS specification section 9.8.6. 
9.8.8.17 
Floor mounted shock absorbing over-travel stops shall be provided at the 
terminal ends of transfer.  The floor mounted shock absorbers shall be designed 
to withstand a full speed impact in such a manner that the impact does not cause 
permanent damage or deflection to the conveyor and shock absorber. 
9.8.8.18 
The shuttle cart and adjacent conveyors shall be provided with perimeter guard 
rail or safety fence (based on application), access gate and hazardous warning 
signage in compliance with the design requirements identified in GVOSS Section 
9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors. 
9.8.8.19 
Aisle Crossing Shuttle Cart Branch Standards List: 
(1) 3ZF-5410-36407 PALLET CROSS AISLE SHUTTLE

9.8.9 
Accumulating Conveyors 
9.8.9.1 
For "body on pallet" applications, accumulation of pallets shall be "zero pressure" 
using standard PRBs and X-belt conveyors as specified herein.  Accumulating 
pallets by means of touching the pallets together is prohibited. 
9.8.9.2 
For "body on skid" applications, accumulation of skids may be accomplished by 
touching the front/rear of each skid together on single-/two-strand configuration 
chain or belt conveyors, or by using standard PRBs and X-chain conveyors. The 
standard speed is 30 FPM (nominal) for chain accumulating conveyor and 45 
FPM (nominal) for belt conveyor. 
9.8.9.3 
The single-strand accumulating conveyor configuration may be used with built-up 
or fixed frame skid designs only – i.e., the skid design incorporates welded/bolted 
cross-ties used to rigidly fasten both skid rails together as a "single skid frame" 
assembly. Additionally, single-strand accumulating conveyors may be used only 
with prior written approval of the VOME Implementation Supervisor or designated 
representative. 
9.8.9.4 
Body Shop and Paint shop applications shall use an electrically actuated 
accumulation stop located at the exit end of the accumulating conveyor. Final 
Assembly accumulating conveyors shall use an air actuated stop. The stop shall 
engage the leading vertical surface of both skid rails, whether single- or two-
strand configuration.  Skid cross members are not allowed to be used as 
stopping surfaces. Skid mounted buck blocks may be utilized only with prior 
written approval from the Owner. 
9.8.9.5 
The head frame, tail frame, intermediate track and all related equipment – i.e., 
skid stops, skid spacers, etc. – shall be modular design and construction. 
9.8.9.6 
The driving gearmotor shall be located at the exit end and outboard of the 
conveyor framework.  The gearmotor shall be shaft mounted wherever possible. 
9.8.9.7 
All take-ups shall be adjustable screw type. 
9.8.9.8 
The track wear bar life expectancy is ten years.  There shall not be a loss of 
material anywhere along the entire surface of the wear bar in excess of 1/16 inch 
during the 10-year life of the conveyor under the designed loads. 
9.8.9.9 
Accumulation conveyors shall be provided with lead-in skid guides at the 
entrance (tail) end of the conveyor.  Accumulation conveyors requiring reversing 
operation shall be provided with lead-in skid guides at the head and tail ends. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 152 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.8.9.10 
Skid guide rollers, CL Frost #439 or Owner approved equivalent, shall be 
provided the full length of the accumulating conveyor. The skid guide rollers shall 
be located outboard of each skid rail on not more than 2 feet 6 inches (760 mm) 
centers. 
9.8.9.11 
Chain accumulation conveyors shall utilize Ford standard CC5-B chain with 
raised rollers and UHMW guide blocks, as shown on Branch Standard 3ZF-
36424 Pallet & Skid Components, Sheet 12. The raised rollers and UHMW guide 
blocks shall be spaced on 12-inch (305 mm) center-to-center multiples not to 
exceed 3 feet (915 mm) center-to-center. The chain pull calculations shall be 
provided by the conveyor Supplier to the Owner for review/approval at the 
engineering design review meeting. 
9.8.9.12 
Belt accumulation conveyors shall be designed to include a belt mounted roller 
trolley with free rollers supported by the trolley frame. The conveyor belt shall be 
3-1/2 inch (87.5mm) wide rubber belt with steel chords rated for 105 kN breaking 
strain, free of silicone and abrasion proof. The roller trolleys must be guided in all 
three axes – i.e., x-, y- and z-axis – and shall be spaced on 12-inch (300 mm) 
center-to-center multiples not to exceed 2 feet 11-1/2 inches (900 mm) center-to-
center.

9.8.10 
Production Conveyors 
9.8.10.1 
For "body on pallet" production line applications, standard PRB type conveyors 
as specified herein shall be used. For "body on skid" production lines 
applications, two-strand configuration chain type conveyors shall be used.   
9.8.10.2 
The production conveyor shall be VFD controlled to provide conveyor speed 
variability, job space variability and speed matching. The conveyor drive 
assembly and controls components shall be sized to provide for a 10% (+/-) 
conveyor speed and job space variance to the base line rate identified in the 
project specifications. 
9.8.10.3 
Chain production conveyors shall utilize Ford standard CC5-A chain with UHMW 
guide blocks, or Owner approved equivalent. The chain pull calculations shall be 
provided by the conveyor Supplier to the Owner for review/approval at the 
engineering design review meeting. 
9.8.10.4 
For chain type production conveyors, the head frame, tail frame, intermediate 
track and all related equipment – i.e., skid stops, skid spacers, etc. – shall be 
modular design and construction. 
9.8.10.5 
For chain type production conveyors, the driving gearmotor shall be located at 
the exit end and outboard of the conveyor framework.  The gearmotor shall be 
shaft mounted wherever possible. 
9.8.10.6 
All take-ups shall be adjustable screw type. 
9.8.10.7 
For chain type production conveyors, the track wear bar life expectancy is ten 
years.  There shall not be a loss of material anywhere along the entire surface of 
the wear bar in excess of 1/16 inch during the 10-year life of the conveyor under 
the designed loads. 
9.8.10.8 
Production conveyors shall be provided with skid guides at the entrance (tail) end 
of the conveyor. 
9.8.10.9 
Skid guide rollers, CL Frost #439 or Owner approved equivalent, shall be 
provided the full length of the production conveyor. The skid guide rollers shall be 
located on both sides of each skid rail on not more than 760 mm centers. 
9.8.10.10 
Production conveyors shall be provided with cover plates and side guide roller 
toe-plates between the out-board skid guide rollers and side skirts. 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 158 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
9.8.10.11 
Production chain conveyors shall utilize Ford standard CC5-A chain, as shown 
on Branch Standard 3ZF-36424 Pallet & Skid Conveyor Common Components, 
Sheet 10. The chain pull calculations shall be provided by the conveyor Supplier 
to the Owner for review/approval at the engineering design review meeting. 
9.8.10.12 
For 2-Strand CC5 chain production conveyor machinery list, refer to Ford VO 
Branch Standard drawing 3ZF-36420. 
9.8.10.13 
For Paint Spray Booth Production Conveyors, Contractor is to fully remove old 
conveyor chain and track is to be high pressure cleaned and scraped, prior to 
any new chain being installed, to ensure that all paint build-up and debris has 
been removed.  Contractor will not be allowed to leave old chain as a method to 
pull the new chain through the track. 
9.8.11 
Single Strand Conveyors 
9.8.11.1 
The use of single strand conveyors is only an acceptable conveyance when 
explicitly requested by the Ford engineer.  Unless single strand conveyance is 
requested in a Scope of Work, they shall never be provided as base bid.

9.8.12 
Body Shop Tooling Pallet Conveyors 
9.8.12.1 
This section is intended to outline the requirements for standard pallet conveyors 
used within Body Shop tooling systems. 
9.8.12.2 
Tooling pallet conveyors shall be designed and fabricated in compliance with the 
GVOSS specification release that is current when the specific program/project 
RFQ is issued. The conveyor supplier shall verify the GVOSS release level at the 
project kick off meeting. 
9.8.12.3 
Tooling pallet conveyors shall be fabricated in compliance with the requirements 
identified in Ford Global Manufacturing Standards “w-KA1 Weight Marking of 
Manufacturing Equipment” and “w-KA2 Manufacturing Equipment Handling and 
Packaging”, including: 
1. 
Lift points for each item shall be labelled. 
2. 
Center of gravity points for each item shall be labelled. 
3. 
Shipping weight for each item shall be labeled. 
9.8.12.4 
Tooling pallet conveyors shall be fabricated in compliance with GVOSS section 
“1.4 VISUAL FACTORY REQUIREMENTS FOR NEW EQUIPMENT”, including: 
1. Labels for motor directional arrow to be installed. 
2. Labels for warning “hands free” to be clear of motor and belt to be 
installed. 
3. Labels for lubrication and toxicology to be installed. 
4. Paint flow direction arrow using stencils to roller beds of all 
equipment. 
5. Paint station number of each table using stencils. 
6. Paint “NO STEP” using stencil on guards and covers that are not 
meant to be stepped on. 
7. All tags, labels and stenciling for equipment to be installed in 
Mexico plants will also have verbiage in Spanish.

9.8.12.10 
Precision Power Roll Bed (PPRB) 
1. 
PPRB conveyors are used within the tooling cell for delivery, re-
spot and inspection process operations. The term “precision” refers 
to the accuracy of the location of the pallet without use of external 
mechanical locating devices. 
2. 
Refer to drawing 3ZF-5410-36828 for the standard 6-Roll PPRB 
conveyor design. Refer to drawing 3ZF-5410-36845 for the 
standard 3-Roll PPRB conveyor design. 
3. 
The PPRB locating tolerance in x, y, and z axes are +/- 0.9 mm. 
The tolerances within a precision power roll bed are achieved as 
follows: 
(a) 
The ‘X’ axis (with flow), by the P&F bar code reader. 
(b) 
The ‘Y’ axis (cross car), by the flanges on the precision 
rollers. 
(c) 
The ‘Z’ axis by the OD tolerance of the precision rollers 
combined with the (6) points of adjustment on the legs of the 
conveyor. 
4. 
The standard pallet PPRB conveyor speeds are 119 FPM (min) and 
414 FPM (max), steady state speed. 
5. 
The rolls motor shall be VFD controlled. 
6. 
The 3-roll and 6-roll PPRB pallet accel, deceleration and in-position 
locations shall be controlled using a P&F bar code and reader. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 162 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
7. 
Include leveling jack screws as shown on the design drawings. 
8. 
Include bracket and mounting holes in the side frame for the RFID 
reader. 
9. 
Include top covers located on belt side of the table only. (Kits can 
be added to the table for a full width covers.) 
10. 
Include Unistrut cable races for routing cables to the rolls 
gearmotor and encoder controls device on the transfer motor. 
11. 
Include Unistrut cable races for routing cables to the rolls 
gearmotor and encoder controls device on the transfer motor. 
9.8.12.11 
Drop and Locate Bed (DLOC) 
1. 
DLOC conveyors are eccentric arm lifts used within the tooling cell 
for geometric assembly process operations. A DLOC unit is 
required when higher precision is needed. 
2. 
Refer to drawing 3ZF-5410-36831 for the standard DLOC conveyor 
design. 
3. 
The DLOC tolerances in x, y, and z axes are +/- .13mm. The 
tolerances are achieved as follows: 
(a) 
The ‘X’ and ‘Y’ axis tolerances are achieved by 
mating the pallet with the 2 way and 4 way locators. 
(b) 
The ‘Z’ axis tolerance is achieved by setting the pallet 
on the hard Z rests. 
4. 
The standard pallet DLOC lift is 90 mm stroke. The TOR height in 
raised position is 600 mm TOR. An internal gearmotor is used for 
lifting. 
5. 
The standard pallet DLOC conveyor rolls speed is 414 FPM (max), 
steady state speed 
6. 
The lift and rolls motors shall be VFD controlled. 
7. 
The DLOC pallet rolls accel, deceleration and in-position locations 
shall be controlled using a P&F bar code and reader. The lift 
positions are controlled using sensors. 
8. 
Include leveling jack screws as shown on the design drawings. 
9. 
Include bracket and mounting holes in the side frame for the RFID 
reader.

10. 
Include top covers located on belt side of the table only. (Kits can 
be added to the table for a full width covers.) 
11. 
Include Unistrut cable races for routing cables to the rolls 
gearmotor and encoder controls device on the transfer motor. 
12.

to the brown (fixed) base frame and one ground hole (without wire)

entrance end and (one) at the exit end. 
9.8.12.12 
Single Power Roll Stand (SPRS) 
1. 
SPRS conveyors are used within the tooling cell for delivery and 
process operations. They are used to reduce the roll-to-roll gap 
between adjacent conveyors. 
2. 
Refer to drawing 3ZF-5410-36832-01 (SPRS10000) for the 
standard High Speed SPRS conveyor design.  (Refer to Ford Geo 
Pallet Tooling Roll Beds Master List V_0.xls) 
3. 
The standard pallet PPRB conveyor speeds are 119 FPM (min) and 
414 FPM (max), steady state speed. Refer to standard drawing 
MP000100 for high speed drive motor assembly and MP000200 for 
low speed drive motor assembly. 
4. 
The rolls motor shall be VFD controlled. 
5. 
Include leveling jack screws as shown on the design drawings. 
6. 
Include Unistrut cable races for routing cables to the rolls 
gearmotor and encoder controls device on the transfer motor. 
7. 
Include (one) ground hole located in the PPRB base frame. (The 
Tooling Vendor provides connection to the conveyor ground hole 
and the cable to ground.)
