Figure No. 9.10.7.6.2 – Trolley Conveyor Track Wear Measurement Tag 
 
9.10.8 
Traction Wheel Turns  
9.10.8.1 
All horizontal turns for Overhead Monorails shall be traction wheel type turns. 
9.10.8.2 
For 4" monorail systems the minimum traction wheel pitch diameter shall be 36 
inches (914 mm). For 6" monorail systems the minimum traction wheel pitch 
diameter shall be 48 inches (1220 mm).  
9.10.8.3 
Traction wheels shall be constructed and equipped as specified below:  
 
1. 
The traction wheel shall be fabricated with a solid plate hub.  
 
2. 
The wheel hub when assembled to the rim shall be central to the width of 
the rim. Wheel rims shall be constructed of minimum 1045 steel.  
 
3. 
Traction wheel elevation in relation to the conveyor track shall be set-up 
such that the conveyor chain will completely engage the wear bar on the 
traction wheel rim, and not cause any damage or wear to any trolley side 
arms, bolts, nuts, or other components or parts of the chain.  
 
4. 
Unless otherwise specified, all traction wheels shall be equipped with 
Dodge traction wheel hub assembly size no. 70 complete with tapered roller 
bearings, drip pan and ball check type grease fitting.  
 
Tag with holes for 
locating test 
points mounted to 
bottom of rail (top 
if upper flange of 
rail is stress point) 
3/8" diameter holes in 
laminated template attached 
with 2 component epoxy 
adhesives 
Hole centers to 
match trolley 
wheel gage 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 192 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
5. 
On oven conveyors, or other elevated temperature operating environments, 
all traction wheels shall be equipped with Dodge traction wheel hub 
assembly size no. 75-HT complete with sleeve bearing (graphite bronze 
bushing). The retainer collar shall hold the bushing in the hub on 6" systems 
only.  
 
6. 
Assembly of the traction wheel to the hub shall be by means of high 
strength bolts, flat washers, through bolted, complete with castellated nuts, 
and shall be safety wired after assembly.  
 
7. 
Traction wheels that are mounted overhead but not directly above the 
conveyor falling parts guard shall include a wheel retainer to prevent the 
traction wheel from falling in the event of a catastrophic hub failure. The 
wheel retainer shall be designed to provide maintenance access, shall be 
constructed from structural steel angle hangers bolted directly to the traction 
wheel mounting frame.  
9.10.8.4 
All traction wheel turns shall be suspended by a minimum of three (3) hangers. 
All hangers shall be sway braced in tension.  
9.10.8.5 
On large radius horizontal turns that cannot be negotiated by the carrier or part 
using a single traction wheel, or when multiple load attachment points are 
required per job space, segmented traction wheel turns shall be provided. 
Traction wheels in segmented turns shall be spaced to suit cording requirements 
at all load bars or chain assemblies. Segmented traction wheel turns shall not 
exceed 30 degrees between any two traction wheels.  
9.10.8.6 
All elevated traction wheels shall be easily and safely accessible from the 
conveyor basket guard or from the plant floor utilizing suitable (scissor-lift or 
boom-lift) man-lift equipment. Dedicated service platforms with ladders and/or 
catwalks shall be provided where conditions prohibit access from the conveyor 
basket guard or with man-lift equipment from the plant floor. 
9.10.8.7 
Traction wheels located in the basket guard at an elevation less than 7'-0" 
(2130mm) from the basket guard shall be considered as "floor level" with respect 
to guarding requirements.  
9.10.9 
Vertical Curves  
9.10.9.1 
All vertical curves shall be rolled smooth and true radius with the flanges 90 
degrees to the web. Straight sections, if required between the curved sections, 
shall be tangent to the curves.  
9.10.9.2 
The radius on all vertical curves shall be designed so that the imposed radial 
loads due to chain tension cording and live load at the trolley do not exceed the 
maximum load carrying capability of the trolley assembly and/or conveyor track.  

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 193 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.10.9.3 
The radius and angle of vertical curves shall be determined by the part, carrier 
and load or trolley attachment centers.  
9.10.9.4 
All vertical curves shall be 12'-0" (3660 mm) minimum radius at 45 degree 
maximum for 4" systems using X-458 chain and 20'-0" (6096 mm) minimum 
radius at 30-degree maximum angle for 6" systems using X-678 chain.  
9.10.9.5 
A minimum dimension of one trolley spacing or load bar assembly shall be 
provided between the tangent line of a vertical curve and the tangent line of a 
horizontal turn. On conveyors where the load is supported by two or more points, 
the minimum dimension between the tangent lines shall be the dimension from 
center to center of the load suspension points.  
9.10.9.6 
All vertical curves shall be suspended by a minimum of three (3) hangers with the 
maximum spacing being 8'-0" (2438 mm) between hangers. All hangers shall be 
sway braced in tension. 
9.10.9.7 
For Phosphate/E-Coat applications, a 304L stainless steel wear bar shall be 
added to the top/bottom flanges of the vertical curves. When kitting existing 
systems, the Contractor is responsible to analyze the existing trolley wheels to 
ensure the wheel diameters do not create a bind condition with the added wear 
bar. 
9.10.10 
Expansion Joints  
9.10.10.1 
Expansion joints shall be provided in the tracks at all points where the conveyor 
crosses a building expansion joint and the conveyor hanger lengths of 4'-0" 
(1220 mm) or less or inside ovens. Expansion joints shall utilize the Ford Motor 
Company standard design, reference Branch Drawing 3ZF-20707, and shall 
permit 4½" (114 mm) expansion or contraction at each joint.  
9.10.10.2 
Expansion joints for oven conveyors shall be provided as indicated in the formula 
on Branch Drawing 3ZF-20707 for 4" expansion (max.) at each joint.  
9.10.10.3 
Building expansion joints shall be designed to eliminate sharp edges, 
obstructions, and voids in the track along the path of the trolleys and shall be 
installed to suit the conveyor chain direction of flow.  
9.10.10.4 
Each side of each expansion joint shall be adequately supported to prevent 
deflection, deformation, or binding due to imposed live and static loads.  
9.10.10.5 
Expansion joint gap shall be calculated to suit anticipated building expansion and 
contraction in-line with the building structure and gradient temperatures at the 
time of installation.  
9.10.11 
Drives  
9.10.11.1 
Whenever possible, all drives shall have the caterpillar chain located on the right-
hand side of the conveyor when facing in the direction of travel. i.e., right hand 
drive. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 194 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.10.11.2 
Whenever possible, drives shall be located at the top of inclines and just prior to 
take-ups. Drives shall not be located at the bottom of inclines or declines.  
9.10.11.3 
Conveyors requiring only one drive shall be provided with a standby drive as 
specified in Paragraph 9.5.7 of this specification. This standby drive shall be 
identical to the main drive and installed prior to the main drive.  
9.10.11.4 
All power chain above the drive and/or take-up balconies shall be considered at 
"floor level” with regard to guarding requirements. All chain drive guards installed 
on drive and take-up platforms shall be removable. i.e., guards shall be mounted 
to frames and secured with standard fasteners. 
9.10.11.5 
All elevated drives shall have 4" monorail service rails installed above the drive 
location, to facilitate removal and replacement of the reducer, motor, and other 
large or bulky components. 
 
1. 
The rail(s) shall be complete with curve sections, or as required, to provide 
adequate access for removal of components. 
 
2. 
Rails shall extend beyond the drive platforms so that components may be 
lowered to the plant floor. 
 
3. 
Rails shall be complete with double ends stops, and a rail trolley suitable for 
the capacity of the rail. 
 
4. 
Attachments shall be included to allow installation of an air hoist or chain fall 
on these trolleys.  
9.10.11.6 
Plant area lighting shall be supplemented as required to provide a minimum 60 
foot-candle lighting intensity for the entire drive platform.  
9.10.11.7 
All drives shall be rotary floating caterpillar type with the drive machinery 
mounted on an inner frame which will rotate about a circular surface concentric to 
the reducer output shaft on heavy duty anti-friction rollers. 
 
1. 
The floating frame shall be supported by a minimum of four (4) fully 
machined anti-friction wheels. 
 
2. 
Adjustable compression springs shall provide the required pre-load and 
overload capacity and a minimum frame travel (rotation) of 3 inches (75 
mm). One inch (25 mm) of travel should be equivalent to 500 pounds (227 
kg) of drive "pull".  
9.10.11.8 
All component parts of the drive units shall be mounted on a common frame and 
shall be arranged so that all parts may be removed and replaced without 
disturbing the remaining units.  

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 195 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.10.11.9 
Standardization of all conveyor components will be carried out to the greatest 
degree possible within practical limits. Special attention shall be given to 
conveyor drive motors\reducers\gearmotors to reach this objective.  
9.10.11.10 
Wherever possible, the drive shall be supported from the platform support steel. 
The platform support steel shall be supported from the overhead building 
structure.  
9.10.11.11 
Over-Torque Protection: 
 
1. 
Under excessive loads the inner frame will rotate actuating an overload limit 
switch which will stop the conveyor. The limit switch shall be set to operate 
at 500 pounds (227 kg) above the calculated maximum chain pull capacity 
of the drive. 
 
2. 
The overload limit switch circuit shall be "fail-safe" design, with limit switch 
actuator "maintained" during normal torque condition. The actuator shall be 
released, returning to normal position, when an overload condition exists 
i.e., the normal position shall indicate a "fault" condition.  
 
3. 
All drives shall be equipped with an indicator and calibrated gauge to 
indicate the load being carried by the drive. The chain pull scale shall be 
calibrated in the shop or in the field prior to the chain being connected 
through the drive unit. The gauge shall be located in a position so it can be 
easily read. One inch of movement on the gauge shall correspond to 500 
pounds (227 kg) of drive "pull". The gauge shall be a metal or lamacoid 
engraved tag, mechanically affixed to the drive frame. 
9.10.11.12 
Design Calculations and Capacities: 
 
1. 
Friction Factor: The Contractor shall furnish with this proposal point-to-point 
chain pull calculations based on a 2 percent friction factor for both 4" 
systems and 6" systems. This formula is considered the minimum friction 
factor for all conveyors provided, with the exception of an oven or spray 
booth environment. All oven and spray-booth conveyors shall be based on a 
5 percent friction factor.  
 
2. 
Maximum Allowable Chain Pull: The maximum allowable chain pull to be 
used in locating drives shall be: 
A. 
2,500 pounds (1136 kg) for X-458 chain system, and 
B. 
4,500 pounds (2045 kg) for X-678 chain system. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 196 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
3. 
Maximum Allowable Chain Tension: The maximum allowable chain tension 
shall be: 
A. 
4,000 pounds (1818 kg) for X-458 chain system, and 
B. 
7,000 pounds (3182 kg) for X-678 chain system.  
 
4. 
Service Factor: All drive components shall be sized using a service factor of 
1.3 times the maximum required chain pull.  
 
5. 
Minimum Drive Capacity: No drives shall be provided with a capacity of less 
than: 
A. 
2,500 pounds (1136 kg) for X-458 chain system, and 
B. 
4,500 pounds (2045 kg) for X-678 chain system. 
9.10.11.13 
Multiple Drive Design Considerations: 
 
1. 
Design of a multiple drive system shall have each drive located such that 
each drive's pull is approximately the same. 
A. 
In addition, drives are to be located such that any one drive can be 
removed for service. When this occurs, the remaining drives must be 
capable of accepting the additional chain pull requirement. 
B. 
With one drive removed, the maximum chain pull allowed shall be 
4,000 pounds (1818 kg) for an X-458 chain conveyor system and 
7,000 pounds (3182 kg) for an X-678 chain conveyor system. 
C. 
All drive component design on a multiple drive system shall meet these 
requirements. 
 
2. 
Production conveyors with multiple drives shall include individual drive 
controls with speed balancing capability.  
 
3. 
Drives provided shall be designed and positioned to suit normal production 
conditions including all system fill, gap, drive out of service, or strip 
sequences. 
 
4. 
When a main and standby drive is installed, they shall be installed with a 
minimum of 4 feet (1220 mm) between drive frames and shall be on a 
common platform to facilitate access.  
 
5. 
The Contractor is responsible for providing any additional drives to satisfy 
these requirements.  

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 197 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.10.11.14 
Caterpillar Chain: 
 
1. 
The caterpillar chain shall consist of RC-160 roller chain with hardened steel 
rollers and bushings with forged hardened dogs compatible with the type of 
conveyor chain used. The caterpillar chain assembly shall be reversible so 
that the opposite face of the dogs can be utilized. 
 
2. 
The caterpillar chain and all components shall be manufactured in America.  
 
3. 
An automatic lubrication system shall be provided for caterpillar chain.  
9.10.11.15 
Back-up Bar and Roller Nest: 
 
1. 
The caterpillar chain shall be guided through the drive between a roller nest 
assembly and an adjustable back-up bar. 
A. 
The roller nest assembly shall consist of hardened steel rollers 
mounted to a support frame. 
B. 
The entire subassembly shall be mounted to the drive frame. 
C. 
Adjustment of both the back-up bar and the roller nest are required in 
order to maintain alignment.  
 
2. 
The back-up bar shall be constructed of high carbon hardened and 
tempered steel. 
A. 
The back-up bar shall be rigidly mounted to an adjustable support 
frame to maintain chain alignment. 
B. 
The back-up bar adjustment screw shall have the means to be locked 
in place for prevention of bar movement after alignment of the chain 
has been achieved. 
 
3. 
All drives shall be provided with a retractable back-up bar assembly. The 
adjustable back-up bar assembly is to be capable of retracting the 
caterpillar chain completely from the conveyor chain by means of a jack 
screw and hand wheel assembly. 
A. 
The hand wheel shall be equipped with a means to be locked in 
position with a removable retaining pin. 
B. 
The back-up bar support shafts (glide rods) shall be pinned to prevent 
movement after frame assembly.  
 
4. 
Back-up rollers shall be equipped with ball type check grease fittings and 
shall be terminated at a central lubrication block. The connection at each 
roller shall be flexible and easily removable.  
 
5. 
Proximity switches shall be provided to monitor each drive for caterpillar 
chain engaged/disengaged condition. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 198 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.10.11.16 
Caterpillar Drive Sprocket: 
 
1. 
All drives shall utilize 19 tooth RC-160 sprockets mounted, bolted and 
safety wired to the reducer output shaft. 
A. 
Two (2) bolts minimum shall be used. 
B. 
Holes for bolts shall be drilled into the reducer output shaft and tapped 
"opposite hand" thread to the rotation of the drive.  
 
2. 
When required, on constant speed multiple drive systems the reducer 
output shaft shall utilize an overriding clutch for balancing of the drives. This 
requirement generally pertains to systems reworked utilizing existing drives 
without back-out capability.  
 
3. 
All clutches shall be FORMSSPRAG FSO series. Written permission from 
the Ford Project Engineer must be obtained for use of clutches on any 
drive.  
9.10.11.17 
Caterpillar Take-up Sprocket: 
 
1. 
Take-up shall utilize a 19 tooth RC-160 sprocket and shall be mounted on a 
2-7/16" minimum diameter CRS shaft. 
A. 
The shaft shall be mounted to an adjustable sliding frame with two 
flange mounted tapered roller bearings. 
B. 
The support frame for the idler shaft shall be sized to prevent 
deflection and to suit the maximum loads imposed in a "jam" condition.  
9.10.11.18 
Reducers: 
 
1. 
Reducers shall be Philadelphia "CDR" series or Falk "DTC" series with a 
vertical shaft, unless otherwise specified.  
 
2. 
All reducers shall have their input horsepower rating equal to or greater than 
the name plate horsepower rating of the motor driving them.  
 
3. 
Reducers shall be selected on the basis of torque and overhung load 
requirements using the following parameters:  
System Size 
 
Single Drive 
 
Multiple Drives  
 
X-458 Chain 
2,500 lb.’s (1136 kg) 
4,000 lb.’s (1818 kg)  
X-678 Chain 
4,500 lb.’s (2045 kg) 
7,000 lb.’s (3182 kg) 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 199 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.10.11.19 
Motor-Reducer Connection: 
 
1. 
All motors and reducers are to be "belt" coupled, then individual 
adjustments are to be provided to remove slack from both the drive belts, as 
well as the brake belts if used. All belts should be "cogged" or "no-slip" 
design. "V" type belts shall not be used.  
 
2. 
On multiple drive systems belts shall be "cogged" or "timing" belts to 
eliminate slippage. 
 
3. 
Belts shall be over-sized using a 2:1 service factor, or twice the required 
rating. 
9.10.11.20 
Brakes: 
 
1. 
All drives shall be equipped with safety brakes. 
A. 
Brake manufacturer shall be approved in writing by the Ford Project 
Engineer. 
B. 
Brakes shall be foot-mounted, self-adjusting, disc brakes with external 
manual release lever. 
C. 
Brakes shall be operated off the reducer input shaft.  
 
2. 
Brakes are to be engaged through electrical interlocks when the conveyor is 
not running. Brakes are to be disengaged by electrical interlocks or the 
manual release lever.  
 
3. 
Electrical controls for manual operation (release) of the brake shall be 
provided.  
9.10.11.21 
Access: 
 
1. 
The design shall provide maintenance platforms, catwalks, and access 
stairs for all drive assemblies. 
 
2. 
The maintenance platform shall run full length both sides of the drive and 
along both ends. The maintenance platform shall provide complete access 
to all four sides of the drive, with easy access to all devices.  
 
3. 
All large vertical openings along the interior and exterior edges of the 
maintenance platform toe-board and the fixed frame of the drives and take-
up are to be protected with the addition of bolt-on angles to the platform 
hangers. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 200 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
4. 
The maintenance platforms for the drive and take-up shall be truss 
supported, and independent of the drive and take-up support steel. 
 
5. 
The drive and take-up platforms shall be bi-level design with all drives, 
reducers, and controls elevated above the platform or with adjacent integral 
walkways to access all components. A single level full width platform may 
be provided, based on application, and written approval of the Ford Project 
Engineer. 
 
6. 
Access walkways provided shall completely enclose drive and take-up 
frame. Elevated ladder shall be provided to access all drive or take-up 
components from the basket guard location.  
9.10.12 
Take-Ups  
9.10.12.1 
All take-ups shall be of the type defined in the project bid specifications using 
traction wheels as the moveable part of the take-up. The take-up design shall be 
as shown on Branch Drawing 3ZF-20705.  
9.10.12.2 
Minimum traction wheel size shall be in accordance with Paragraph 9.9.6.2 of 
this Specification Section.  
9.10.12.3 
Take-ups shall be installed on the non-loaded section of the conveyor.  
9.10.12.4 
All oven conveyors shall have an air over oil take-up complete with service 
platform.  
9.10.12.5 
Take-ups shall be located directly after the drive at a point of minimum chain 
tension. Alternate locations must be approved in writing by the Ford Project 
Engineer.  
9.10.12.6 
All take-ups shall be one of the following types:  
 
1. 
Screw – All applications for screw take-ups must be approved at the time of 
original proposal by the Owner.  
 
2. 
Air operated  
 
3. 
Air over oil operated  
9.10.12.7 
The effective travel of the take-up shall be sufficient to remove one complete job 
spacing plus a minimum of 2'-0" (610 mm) additional travel for disassembly and 
re-assembly of the conveyor chain. The minimum travel shall be 4 linear feet 
(1220 mm) of take-up movement.  

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 201 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.10.12.8 
On conveyors which require synchronous job-spacing or positioning along the 
conveyor, additional screw-type take-ups shall be installed to allow synchronizing 
the location of the loads at the various stations. i.e., load and unload stations. 
These shall be in addition to the air or air-over-oil type take-up located at the 
drive. 
9.10.12.9 
Plant area lighting shall be supplemented as required to provide a minimum 60 
foot-candle lighting intensity for the entire take-up platform.  
9.10.12.10 
Frame Design: 
 
1. 
The take-up frame shall consist of a fixed outer frame and a floating inner 
frame. 
 
2. 
The fixed outer frame shall consist of 2'-0" (610 mm) long dropout track 
sections as required to achieve the unit chain spacing removal 
requirements. 
 
3. 
All drop out sections shall be bolted to the take-up frame steel when 
delivered. 
 
4. 
Wheel tracks and retainers shall be provided to accommodate the 
requirements of the floating frame.  
 
5. 
The inner frame shall be equipped with six (6) trolley wheels for linear travel 
and our (4) flat face rollers with anti-friction bearings for side to side 
alignment to support the inner frame.  
 
6. 
On air operated take-ups, as well as air-over-oil take-ups, the floating frame 
shall be attached to the cylinder with one ½" Herc-Alloy heat treated chain. 
Two (2) 3/8" safety chains shall also be provided.  
 
7. 
Take-up shall have a guard "pocket" containment pan, or a similar means, 
to collect the excess cylinder chain, as well as safety chains, in such a 
manner which prevents interference with conveyor chain and allows easy 
adjustment of chain length.  
 
8. 
All expansion bridge edges which the conveyor trolley can contact shall be 
feathered to allow smooth trolley passage.  
 
9. 
The take-up shall be installed with expansion bridges lapped in the direction 
of travel.  
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 202 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
10. Permanent markers shall be installed on the fixed outer frame and movable 
inner frame, to indicate limits of travel for the inner frame. e.g., fully 
extended, fully retracted, etc.  
9.10.12.11 
Air Cylinder and Devices: 
 
1. 
The pre-lubricated air cylinder shall have a minimum stroke of 4'-0" (1220 
mm) and shall be sized to produce a force equal to 300 percent of the 
calculated chain tension. The design air pressure to be used shall be 60 psi.  
 
2. 
All air cylinders shall be piped as shown on the Branch Drawings. Valve 
boards shall be utilized for mounting the pneumatic components. All bleed 
valves, shut-off valves, etc. shall be safely accessible from the outside 
maintenance platform or basket guarding, without requiring personnel to 
climb onto the take-up, etc. 
 
3. 
The Contractor shall install one 6" liquid damped pressure gauge, piped so 
as to be visible from the Plant floor, permanently marked with Low, Normal, 
and High operation pressure, etc.  
 
4. 
Lamacoid tags are to be provided at each air take-up to indicate proper air 
setting based on design chain tension/drive calculations. Information shown 
on the tags shall include the minimum and maximum recommended air 
pressure requirements based on chain pull data.  
 
5. 
A separate air supply port shall be installed off of the valve board supply 
line, in easy reach from the platform, for maintenance use. It shall have a 
separate shut-off valve, quick-connect coupling, etc.  
9.10.12.12 
Controls  
 
1. 
Each take-up shall be equipped with limit switches and a cam operated 
actuator mounted to the cylinder rod end to indicate the following conditions:  
A. 
Take-up fully retracted.  
B. 
Take-up fully extended. 
C. 
Take-up maintenance required (approaching fully extended).  
D. 
An air pressure switch shall be provided to detect pressure loss or no 
air. If either of these conditions occurs, a field light will illuminate, and 
an alarm will sound. The field light and alarm are provided by this 
Contractor.  
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 203 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
2. 
Conditions "A, B, or D" will stop the conveyor, illuminate a red field light and 
sound an alarm. All components defined are to be provided by this 
Contractor.  
 
3. 
Condition "C" will illuminate a yellow field light only which is to be provided 
by this Contractor.  
 
4. 
Actuator shall be designed long enough so that condition "C" will be 
maintained when condition "B" occurs.  
 
5. 
Correct locations for the switches shall be permanently marked in the take-
up frame.  
9.10.12.13 
Access: 
 
1. 
All take-up installations shall be provided with a maintenance platform, 
catwalk, and access ladder to service all take-up assemblies. The take-up 
assembly shall be installed over a full width platform with 30" (762 mm) 
clearance on each side and end, or with a dedicated maintenance platform 
installed the full length both sides of the take-up and along the rear. The 
platform elevation shall be approximately 3'-0" (914 mm) below the top of 
the take-up frame. 
 
2. 
If dedicated walkways are utilized, all large vertical openings along the 
interior edges of the maintenance platform toe-board and the fixed frame of 
the take-up are to be protected with the addition of bolt-on angles to the 
platform hangers.  
 
3. 
Walkways, stairs, or other means shall be provided for access to all portions 
of the platforms, where they intersect trusses, utility piping mains, buss 
ducts, or other interferences. In no case shall these interferences, or any 
other structures, other than the platform and related ladders, etc. be used as 
a means of accessing conveyor components. If sufficient clearance cannot 
be maintained, then those interferences shall be relocated.  
 
4. 
Wherever possible, the take-up shall be supported from the platform support 
steel. The platform support steel shall be supported from the overhead 
building structure.  
9.10.13 
Drip/Dirt Pans  
9.10.13.1 
At a minimum, drip pans shall be provided in the following areas:  
 
1. 
All areas following dip and/or spray processes, 
 
2. 
Under all drives, 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 204 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
3. 
Under air-over-oil take-ups, 
 
4. 
Under all lubricators, 
 
5. 
Under all traction wheel hubs, 
 
6. 
Under return chain where dripping can occur on personnel, equipment, and 
stock below, and 
 
7. 
Under all other sources of oil, water, or other dripping fluids.  
9.10.13.2 
Drip pans under drives are to extend under the entire cat chain and conveyor 
chain, where applicable.  
9.10.13.3 
Drip pans shall be sections formed from 14-gauge sheet metal, having 2" (50 
mm) high sides. Assembly of the sections shall be lapped, with gasket, and 
fastened with self-tapping sheet metal screws.  
9.10.13.4 
Drip pans for air-over-oil take-up shall be large enough to completely cover the 
take-up cylinder and controls and be sized to hold the entire quantity of oil in the 
circuit.  
9.10.13.5 
Dirt pans shall be installed on the conveyor rails when the conveyor path is 
located over certain process operations which cannot tolerate contamination. 
These include Phosphate and E-Coat operations where "C" attachments shall be 
included in the part hook design to allow for this pan. Pan shall be installed 
continuous for the entire conveyor chain length. 
9.10.13.6 
When conveyor is installed in a corrosive environment, a "water seal" pan shall 
be included, to prevent corrosive vapors from damaging the conveyor chain. An 
"S" attachment shall be included in the part hook design to accommodate this 
seal.  
9.10.13.7 
When there are empty hang chains, carriers, or return chain passing over loaded 
portions of the conveyor, personnel, equipment, or stock located below the 
conveyor, then dirt containment pans shall be installed continuous beneath the 
conveyor, to eliminate the possibility of contamination.  
9.10.13.8 
Part Drip Pans: 
 
1. 
Drip pans shall be installed under parts when parts are exiting process 
equipment or areas where they are subjected to moisture or are otherwise 
dripping. These pans shall be liquid-tite and shall be continuous for a 
sufficient distance for all dripping to stop, assuming "worst case" carrier time 
or maximum carrier transit time.  
 
2. 
Pans shall be complete with drain lines as required, dependent on amount 
of fluid. Fluid should not continuously accumulate in the drip pans, so as to 
fill the entire floor of the pan, during continuous production operation. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 205 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.10.13.9 
Part Dirt Pans: 
 
1. 
When conveying parts which are susceptible to damage from dirt – e.g., 
seats, interior trim components, door trim panels, etc. – dirt pans shall be 
provided overhead to protect 100% of the parts from the dirt and /or liquid 
contamination. 
A. 
When the pans are installed from the conveyor rail "C" hook 
attachments shall be utilized to protect the parts from chain dirt. 
Additional dirt pans shall be installed on the individual part hooks or 
carriers, as required. 
B. 
All dirt pans shall be constructed so as to provide continuous 
protection and shall not be damaged by the conveyor or parts. 
C. 
Care shall be taken in the pan design and installation, to accommodate 
all load, unload, production operations, as well as conveyor controls 
and actuators. 
D. 
Pans shall be designed with no sharp edges and be positioned so as 
not to interfere with personnel ergonomic concerns.  
 
2. 
When the product being conveyed has loose wires, cables, or other portions 
which may hang down and contact the falling parts guard, a safety dirt pan 
shall be installed in the bottom of the falling parts guard. 
A. 
This dirt pan shall be continuous and lapped in the direction of flow to 
eliminate any shape edges or other means by which a cable or other 
component may snag on or otherwise be damaged by the pan.  
9.10.14 
Part Hooks  
9.10.14.1 
The Contractor shall be responsible for the installation of all part hooks whether 
supplied by him or by others. The Contractor shall provide and install all chain 
trolleys, trolley attachments, load pins, bolts, washers, nuts, cotter pins, safety 
chains, safety cables, cable clamps, etc., required for hook or carrier installation  
9.10.14.2 
Part hooks shall be designed and supplied by this Contractor for all conveyor 
systems as follows:  
 
1. 
All "small" parts (sub-assemblies, etc.). These parts are normally conveyed 
on light duty monorail and P&F conveyors.  
 
2. 
All conveyors which utilize manual load and unload stations exclusively.  
 
3. 
Unless otherwise indicated in the Detailed Equipment Descriptions section 
for the individual conveyor unit.  

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025

9.10.14.3 
Part hooks shall be designed and supplied by "others" for the following 
conveyors: 
 
1. 
All conveyors which utilize automatic load or unload stations, or other 
automatic interfaces with tooling or other process equipment. In each case, 
this must be verified in the individual bid specifications or clarified in the 
submitted proposal.  
9.10.14.4 
When part hooks are supplied by "others", this Contractor retains the 
responsibility to coordinate with the Supplier, and to ensure that the hook 
supplied will work with the proposed system and layout and shall modify the 
system as required to suit.  
9.10.14.5 
For overhead carrier design criteria refer to GVOSS Section 9.4 Safety 
Requirements for Conveyors.  
9.10.14.6 
Carriers, load bars, hang chains, etc. shall be guided and stabilized as required 
to achieve smooth loading, unloading, and other operations along the conveyor. 
Guiding and centering surfaces shall be replaceable and designed to eliminate 
frictional wear on the carriers, hooks, track surfaces, etc.  
9.10.14.7 
When conveying parts which are susceptible to damage by contact with the part 
hook or carrier – e.g., interior trim parts, non-metallic parts, seats, facias, etc. -- 
the part hook or carrier shall be constructed of materials so as to prevent 
damage to the parts. 
 
1. 
Metal hooks or carriers shall be plastisol coated. 
 
2. 
Coating shall extend from the bottom of the hook or carrier up to the load 
bar or chain attachments.  
9.10.15 
Testing and Acceptance  
9.10.15.1 
Prior to acceptance by the Owner and during the facility launch period, the 
Contractor is to provide to the Owner the results of chain tension testing. 
9.10.15.2 
This testing shall be accomplished utilizing tension links and recording media to 
indicate actual tension at final installation. 
9.10.15.3 
The testing will evaluate the conveyor based on loaded, gap, strip, and drive out 
of service (for multiple drive systems) conditions. 
9.10.15.4 
Excessive tensions or slack conditions noted during the tests shall be corrected 
at no cost to the Owner.  
9.10.16 
Pendulum Conveyors (Phosphate & ECoat) 
9.10.16.1 
All above applicable items of Section 9.10 shall also apply to the Pendulum 
Conveyor.  Contractor to provide the System Design that includes the track, 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 207 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
chain, sprockets, drives, take-up, pendulum guide to return track and lubrication, 
in their Bid Quote for Owner’s Approval and adherence to this Specification. 
9.10.16.2 
All chain details along with a 10’-0” sample of chain shall be provided/shown to 
the Ford project engineer for approval prior to manufacturing. 
9.10.16.3 
Chain strands shall be match marked in pairs for installation.  The chains shall be 
manufactured so that they are not right or left hand specific and can be used for 
either side of the system. 
9.10.16.4 
The maximum allowable tension for Vertical Pendulum type chain shall not 
exceed 15,500 lbs. per strand or 31,000 lbs. for two strands.  Chain shall have a 
minimum average ultimate strength of 95,000 lbs. or ten times the chain tension 
under average loading conditions, whichever is greater.  A detailed point to point 
chain pull analysis showing maximum chain tension in the system and maximum 
chain pull at the drive(s), under worst case conditions considering strip and fill 
requirements, shall be issued to the Ford project engineer for approval, during 
the design/engineering phase. 
9.10.16.5 
Chain pins shall be stainless steel.  The chain pins shall have retention holes or 
slots to keep the pin in place with a stainless steel retaining device.  The pins 
shall be cross drilled and tapped for a zerk fitting on the outboard side of the 
chain. 
9.10.16.6 
The chain shall include a method of guiding and maintaining chain alignment 
through the system. 
9.10.16.7 
The track shall provide a minimum of 10 years corrosion warranty and all 
hangers, sheet metal shrouding, drip pan and hardware shall be 316L Stainless 
Steel.  Any track supports that penetrate the process housing shall require seals 
and stainless steel flashing. 
9.10.16.8 
The take-up shall be counterweighted to assist in removing slack chain from the 
system however, the spring assembly shall be utilized to properly tension the 
chain.  The Counterweight shall provide a means to add/remove weight to help 
tension the chain. 
9.10.16.9 
The take-up guide rollers shall be of a robust design and only allow ¼” overall 
tolerance from one side to the other. 
9.10.16.10 
Pendulum spacing to be designed to allow for them to not touch on the return 
side of the conveyor.  Pendulum guide mechanism used after Unload, is to be a 
robust design and not attached to the moving Take-up. 
9.10.16.11 
Main and Standby drives are required with main/standby VFDs and easy switch 
over capability provided for both.  See sketch below for Drive setup.
