9.11 
FRICTION CONVEYOR SYSTEMS 
9.11.1 
General 
9.11.1.1 
This section is intended to identify the minimum Friction Conveyor System (FCS) 
design requirements for delivery conveyor applications. Friction conveyor 
systems are used primarily as delivery conveyors where high speed delivery is 
required. 
9.11.1.2 
The FCS conveyor system may be overhead or inverted configuration. The 
standard FCS delivery conveyor speed shall be 25 meters per minute (82 FPM). 
9.11.1.3 
The FCS conveyor system shall be designed such that carrier accumulation is 
maximized between the carrier load/unload stations. i.e., the accumulated carrier 
quantity shall be as close to a representative P&F conveyor of similar length and 
configuration. 
9.11.2 
FCS Drives 
9.11.2.1 
The spacing between drives for: 
1. All areas such that the carrier tow-/load-bar is always in contact with a 
minimum of one FCS drive assembly. 
Delivery zones shall be no more than 1’ less than the friction bar length (or 
barged carriers friction bar length), to ensure that friction wheels are always 
engaged into the full height friction bar, and to prevent bumping of carriers 
within the system. Bumping of carriers within delivery areas (other than slow 
speed docking areas) is not allowed. 
9.11.2.2 
The FCS drive assembly consists primarily of a gearmotor driven friction wheel 
and pressure roller mounted on a modular steel frame.  As much as possible, all 
FCS drive components provided to a plant for a given project shall be identical 
design, including purchased and fabricated components. 
9.11.2.3 
All components of the FCS drive assembly shall be mounted to a single modular 
steel frame.   
4. The mounting frame shall be bolted to the conveyor track mounting bracket(s) or 
track yokes, with adjustment sufficient to provide proper wheel and track 
alignment.  
5. Where spacing requires friction drive frames to be track mounted with beam 
clamps, the clamp bracket shall be blocked/gibbed to the track to prevent 
shifting. 
6. The mounting frame shall be designed with sufficient rigidity to resist the imposed 
load(s) without excessive deflection. A Finite Element Analysis (FEA) shall be 
provided at the design review for all drive assembly designs implemented on a 
given project. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 210 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.11.2.4 
The gearmotor shall be assembled to a pivoting mounting base. The pivoting 
mounting base shall incorporate an adjustable compression spring to ensure 
adequate driving pressure is transmitted to the friction wheel for the entire carrier 
transfer cycle. The pivot assembly axle and associated mounting hardware shall 
be mechanically locked to prevent loosening due to vibration and/or cyclic 
loading with a keeper plate as illustrated in Figure 9.11.2.4. Use of setscrews 
only for this purpose is not acceptable. 
9.11.2.5 
All pivot axles and axle mounts shall incorporate adequate grease fittings and 
capillaries to allow for proper lubrication of these items. 
 
Figure No. 9.11.2.4 - FCS Drive Pivot Pin 
 
9.11.2.6 
Drive assemblies located on horizontal turns shall be assembled to a spring-
loaded pivoting mounting frame.  
1. The pivoting mounting frame shall be self-adjusting such that the drive wheel and 
pressure roller maintain contact with the load-/tow-bar as the carrier chords 
through the turn. 
2. The pivoting mounting frame shall be mounted such that the drive and reaction 
wheels are not centered on the track, but are offset to allow for smooth carrier 
entry/exit and also to prevent a hard jam condition between the bumper stop and 
the frame. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 211 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
3. The pivoting mounting frame shall fully return to the initial position once the 
carrier has transferred through the zone.  
4. A sound-deadening "hard" stop shall be included to prevent the assembly from 
over-rotating beyond the initial position.   
5. The pivot frame axle and associated mounting hardware shall be mechanically 
locked to prevent loosening due to vibration and/or cyclic loading with a keeper 
plate similar to that illustrated in Figure 9.11.2.4. Use of setscrews only for this 
purpose is not acceptable. 
9.11.2.7 
The driving gearmotor shall utilize a helical-bevel type gearbox. Worm-gear type 
gearboxes will not be allowed. The conveyor contractor shall provide gearmotor 
size and selection calculations, together with gearmotor supplier approval 
documentation, at the design review meeting. The calculations shall apply to both 
straight and turn drives. 
9.11.2.8 
The driving gearmotor at carrier load and unload stations shall be designed to 
facilitate variable speed requirements for accel/decel and the steady-state, or 
normal, operating speed.  
9.11.2.9 
The friction wheel shall be fabricated from a steel hub coated with a polyurethane 
material and assembled to the output shaft of the gearmotor. The friction wheel 
shall be keyed to the reducer output shaft for torque transmission. A retaining 
cap shall be bolted to the end of the gearmotor output shaft and shall be secured 
to prevent loosening during normal operation. 
9.11.2.10 
The pressure roller shall be fabricated from a steel hub coated with a thermo-set 
material. The pressure roller shall be mounted directly opposite the friction wheel 
and is intended to ensure the friction wheel maintains contact with the carrier 
tow/load bar as it transfers through the drive control zone.  The pressure roller 
axle and mounting hardware shall be secured to prevent loosening during normal 
operation. 
9.11.2.11 
The friction wheel and pressure roller thermo-set material shall be a minimum 90 
to 95 Shore A hardness. 
9.11.2.12 
A sheet metal "falling part" guard shall be provided below the friction wheel to 
retain the wheel in the event of a mounting hardware and/or retaining cap failure. 
9.11.2.13 
Proximity switches shall be used to identify that a carrier is engaged and/or 
disengaged with the respective FCS drive assembly. The use of photo eyes to 
detect carrier engagement shall be prohibited. The proximity switch shall be 
mounted to the drive assembly fixed frame and the prox flag shall be mounted to 
the pivoting frame. 
9.11.2.14 
Friction drive power, control and communication cables shall be routed and 
secured such that no rubbing of cables can occur during operation of the 
conveyor system. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 212 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.11.2.15 
All friction drive distributed control modules – i.e., Movifits, Armorstarts, etc. - 
shall be located within line-of-sight of the associated gearmotors. The distributed 
control modules shall be mounted for convenient maintenance access that does 
not require lifting equipment, ladders, or Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) 
for access. 
9.11.2.16 
All FCS part hooks/carriers shall be provided with a secondary safety restraint as 
specified in GVOSS Section 9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors. The 
secondary restraint orientation must be designed to eliminate the possibility of 
binding during chording through the system. 
9.11.3 
Straight Track 
9.11.3.1 
All track shall be constructed from standard structural steel shapes and shall be 
new and free of imperfections, distortion, dirt, rust, slag, scale, or other foreign 
matter.  All track shall be of uniform cross section, free of warps and twists and 
with tolerances not exceeding limits set by the ASTM. 
9.11.3.2 
The complete track section shall be jig assembled and welded using uniformly 
symmetrical yokes fabricated from steel plate. 
9.11.3.3 
Where steel trolley wheels are used, the track shall be constructed from high 
carbon steel or as approved in writing from the Owner.  Hot rolled and heat-
treated steel shapes is not acceptable for steel trolley wheel applications. 
A minimum 1/2" (13 mm) thick Sure-Alloy, or Owner-approved equivalent, 
reinforcement bar shall be welded to the bottom flanges of both free tracks. 
1. 
The reinforcement bar shall be flush with the toe edges for the full length of 
all horizontal turns. 
2. 
The bar shall extend back to and connect to the track yokes. 
3. 
A design analysis will determine the proper configuration of the bar stock 
required. 
4. 
This reinforcement bar is not required where the Contractor track is a rolled 
section and the bar is an integral part of the form. 
9.11.3.4 
The track bearing flange(s) shall be designed and reinforced as required to 
support the load resulting from a single trolley load equal to 400 pounds (182 kg) 
per wheel. 
9.11.3.5 
Conveyor track splice joints shall be designed and located to accommodate 
installation of supports adjacent to the splice.  Bending moment and radial loads 
at the splice joint shall be as close to zero as possible. 
9.11.3.6 
All supports shall be provided on centers which limit conveyor track deflection to 
a maximum of 1/16 inch (1.5 mm).  In no case shall the support spacing exceed 
10 feet (3050 mm).  All FCS track shall be sway braced to the conveyor 
superstructure as necessary to provide a sufficiently rigid conveyor installation. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 213 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.11.3.7 
All supports, hangers and sway bracing shall be bolted to track yokes. The bolted 
connections from the support/hanger shall be designed in such a way as to 
distribute all loads from each track section at a joint evenly to the hanger. 
9.11.3.8 
Track yokes shall be a minimum 3/8-inch plate and shall be spaced at a 
maximum of 3’-4” (1016 mm) centers for straight track sections and 2’-0” (610 
mm) for horizontal turns and vertical curves. Track yokes shall be installed on 
each end of the straight, horizontal and vertical curve track sections. 
9.11.3.9 
All track ends shall be saw cut square with a gap joint not to exceed 1/16 inch 
(1.5 mm). 
9.11.3.10 
All track joints shall be assembled by means of bolting the two outboard yokes 
from adjacent track sections together. The track joint bolt connections shall be 
high strength ASTM F3125/F3125M bolts, flat washers, and steel insert locknuts.  
The threaded portion of the bolt must not be in the shear plane of the track joint. 
Proper alignment of adjoining track sections must be maintained. 
9.11.3.11 
Field welding, if required to maintain track alignment will be permitted for these 
field installed yokes only. 
9.11.3.12 
Straight track sections shall have all miscellaneous components assembled to 
them in the shop according to their placement and final location to suit the final 
conveyor layout. 
9.11.3.13 
Track sections for transfers shall be completely assembled and tested for 
workability in the conveyor Supplier shop.  Transfers shall have all power only 
turns, traction wheels and all changes in elevation required by the design 
mounted as an assembly in the shop.  All track mounted accessories that may be 
required due to a manufacturers design shall be shop assembled to the transfer 
track section and all equipment shall be shop tested.  All proximity switches 
required for actuation through the transfer track section are to be installed in the 
shop, including wiring and conduit or cable tray. 
9.11.3.14 
The conveyor track shall be installed plumb, square and level.  Under no 
condition shall conveyor track be installed which will allow the carrier to “drift" 
backward or forward when the drive is disengaged. 
9.11.4 
Horizontal Turns 
9.11.4.1 
The radius of horizontal turns shall be determined by the part/carrier chording 
clearances, by the load or trolley attachment centers and the trolley load carrying 
capacity. The minimum turn radius shall be equal to 0.85 times the maximum 
center distance between trolleys on the carrier assembly, but not less than a 4-
foot radius. 
9.11.4.2 
All horizontal turns shall be rolled smooth and true radius with the track flanges at 
90-degrees to the web.  Horizontal turns shall include a minimum 1'-0" long (305 
mm) section of straight track beyond the tangent line(s) for each end of the turn. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 214 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.11.4.3 
All horizontal turns shall be supported at a minimum of three locations, with the 
maximum spacing being 8’-0” (2438 mm) between supports. All supports shall be 
sway braced in tension. 
9.11.4.4 
Where chain-assist "helper" drives are required for incline/decline vertical curves, 
the horizontal turn shall be located such that a complete carrier length – i.e., lead 
trolley to trailing trolley – is in-line on the straight track section prior to entering 
the chain-assist transfer zone.  
 
9.11.5 
Track Switches 
9.11.5.1 
All FCS track switches shall be shop assembled and shipped to the site as a 
modular assembly. The completed assembly shall be shop tested for functionality 
prior to shipping to the site for installation. All switch modules shall be complete 
with all controls field devices assembled in the shop to provide rapid installation 
in the field. All controls shall be placed to suit safety and plant maintenance 
requirements. 
9.11.5.2 
The FCS entrance switch shall be glide type configuration as illustrated in Figure 
No. 9.11.5.2. All FCS entrance switches shall be electrically actuated. 
9.11.5.3 
With the exception of repair spur entrance switches, all entrance switches shall 
be automatically operated for both switching directions by means of proximity 
switches activated by system carrier movement. The repair spur entrance switch 
shall be manually initiated by means of a manually operated pushbutton station. 
9.11.5.4 
All exit switches shall be tongue type configuration when using P&F style channel 
track and glide type when using I-beam style track. The tongue is to be 
mechanically actuated by the carrier trolley as the carrier passes through the 
switch. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 215 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
 
 
Figure No. 9.11.5.2 – Electrically Actuated FCS Glide Switch 
 
 
9.11.5.5 
Tongue Type Switch Design. 
1. 
A track switch assembly shall consist of a precision cut Sure-Alloy or 1045 
hardened steel tongue. The tongue shall be attached to a vertical pivoting 
shaft which is to be aligned with two thrust bearings and framing. This 
assembly is to be an integral part of the FCS track assembly. 
2. 
The underside of the tongue tip shall be supported by tongue receptacles at 
each switch position. The tongue receptacles are to have a lead-in 
incorporated into the design for smooth tongue engagement. The tongue 
receptacles are to be attached to the bottom flange of the track. The tongue 
receptacle shall provide a minimum of 1 inch (25 mm) bearing surface for 
the tongue. 
3. 
Rubber pads or other material shall be incorporated to absorb the sound 
energy when the switch tongue is operated. 
4. 
All moving components of the tongue switch assembly shall be painted 
Safety Orange. 
5. 
All bearings and pivots of the track switch shall have lubrication fittings 
installed. Fittings to be easily accessible through use of remote lubrication 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 216 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
blocks as required. 
9.11.5.6 
Glide Type Switch Design. 
1. 
Glide type track switches shall be designed as sliding units with straight and 
curved track sections to direct trolleys to the appropriate conveyor branch. 
The sliding section shall be supported on ball bearing guides or cam 
followers in tracks and shall be indexed by a gearmotor connected to crank 
arms. The gearmotor shall include an electrically actuated brake with a 
mechanical release for manual operation. 
2. 
The motor motion will be stopped utilizing proximity switches mounted to the 
fixed frame and flags mounted to the moving frame. The switch curved and 
straight track sections shall be driven into adjustable hard stops for proper 
track alignment. 
3. 
Mechanically actuated safety stops shall be installed on the switch carriage 
to prevent trolleys from proceeding into the open zone during switch 
transition. 
4. 
Open track ends immediately adjacent to a switch, shall be supported from 
the same steelwork as the switch to aid with track alignment and to ensure 
smooth trolley movement through the switch zone. 
5. 
Support bars shall be mounted to the bottom of the adjacent fixed track rail 
sections, extending approximately 2 inches (50 mm) past the end of the rail, 
to support the sliding switch frame rail when in the transfer position, 
assuring a smooth transition joint and preventing the trolley load from being 
transferred onto the switch-sliding frame. 
6. 
Track switches shall be factory assembled. Each switch shall be an 
independent, interchangeable modular unit. 
7. 
Maintenance access shall be provided to all operational parts of the glide 
switch. 
8. 
A zone blocking control arrangement shall be used to assure that carriers 
do not enter the switch when it is not in the correct transfer position. 
9. 
Proximity switches shall be used to detect the switch in the open or closed 
(straight or turn track) position. 
10 
A manual pushbutton control station shall be located adjacent to the switch 
area. These pushbutton stations shall include a selector switch to select 
automatic or manual control mode. Manual controls shall allow the switch to 
move to all its positions. When in manual mode, no carriers will be allowed 
to automatically enter the switch zone unless the track switch is in the 
proper transfer position. No safety circuits will be bypassed when in manual 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 217 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
mode. 
9.11.5.7 
All bearings and pivots included in the track switch assembly shall have 
lubrication fittings installed.  The lube fittings shall be easily accessible through 
use of remote lubrication blocks. 
9.11.5.8 
Rubber pads or other sound deadening material shall be incorporated to absorb 
the sound energy when the switch is operated. 
9.11.5.9 
All movable components of the track switch tongue assembly shall be painted 
Safety Orange. 
9.11.5.10 
Carriers shall not be permitted to accumulate through the individual track 
switches. 
9.11.6 
Expansion Joints 
9.11.6.1 
Expansion joints shall be provided in the FCS track at all points where the 
conveyor crosses a building expansion joint and the conveyor hanger length is 
4’-0” (1220 mm) or less.  Expansion joints shall be in compliance with Ford Motor 
Company VO Branch Drawing 3ZF-20707 and shall permit 4-1/2 inches (114 
mm) expansion or contraction at each joint. 
9.11.6.2 
The expansion joint shall be designed to eliminate sharp edges, obstructions, 
and voids in the track along the path of the trolleys and shall be installed to suit 
the conveyor chain direction of flow. 
9.11.6.3 
Each side of each expansion joint shall be adequately supported to prevent 
deflection, deformation, or binding due to imposed live and static loads. 
9.11.7 
Inspection Gates 
9.11.7.1 
All FCS conveyor systems shall incorporate a minimum of three free track 
inspection gates regardless of the conveyor length. The inspection gates shall be 
located on the inbound side of each carrier load and unload station and at the 
carrier repair spur. The carrier repair spur inspection gate shall be located to 
allow changing of any carrier free trolley without interruption of production flow 
along the main line portion of the conveyor adjacent to the repair spur. 
9.11.7.2 
The inspection gate shall be the hinged panel type with a lock pin or quick 
disconnect pin for fast and easy removal. 
9.11.7.3 
The side of the track adjacent to and including the inspection gate shall be 
painted yellow with black diagonal stripes to indicate the existence of a 
potentially dangerous area. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 218 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.11.8 
Vertical Curves 
9.11.8.1 
Vertical curves shall be rolled smooth and true radius with the flanges 90 
degrees to the web.  Straight sections, if required between the curved sections, 
shall be tangent to the curves. 
9.11.8.2 
The vertical curve radius and angle of inclination shall be determined by the 
part/carrier chording clearances, by the lead/load trolley attachment centers and 
the trolley load capacity. The minimum radius shall be 15 feet and the maximum 
angle shall be 20 degrees. 
9.11.8.3 
The vertical curve drive assembly shall be similar in construction to the 
monoplane drive except the pressure roller is to be replaced with an additional 
friction drive wheel. i.e., there shall be two friction wheels per vertical curve drive 
assembly. 
9.11.8.4 
The vertical curve friction wheel face width shall be designed as necessary to 
accommodate the additional force required to "lift" the carrier through the vertical 
curve angle of inclination. 
9.11.8.5 
The spacing between drives on vertical curves shall be such that the carrier tow-
/load-bar maintains contact with a minimum of one FCS drive assembly. The 
vertical curve friction drive gearmotors shall incorporate an integrally mounted 
disc brake sized to immediately stop the conveyed load on the incline/decline in 
the event an E-stop condition exists. 
9.11.8.6 
The incline and decline vertical curves shall include anti-backup and anti-
runaway devices in compliance with the requirements identified for P&F 
conveyors in GVOSS Section 9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors. 
9.11.8.7 
Chain-assist conveyors may be used in lieu of vertical curve friction drive 
assemblies. 
9.11.8.8 
Vertical curves shall be supported at a minimum of three locations with a 
maximum spacing of 8'-0" (2438 mm) between supports. All supports shall be 
sway braced in tension. 
9.11.9 
Chain-Assist Conveyor 
9.11.9.1 
Chain-assist conveyors may be used in lieu of vertical curve friction drive 
assemblies. Chain-assist conveyors shall be provided where the layout 
configuration requires incline and decline vertical curves greater than the 
maximum 20-degree angle of inclination. 
9.11.9.2 
The chain-assist conveyor may engage the carrier free trolley, similar to a 
conventional P&F conveyor system, or a pusher pin mounted to the carrier tow-
bar or trolley side plate. In either case, the chain-assist conveyor shall engage 
the carrier as close to the center-of-gravity of the lead trolley as the system 
design allows. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 219 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.11.9.3 
The conveyor Supplier is responsible to determine the reaction loads transmitted 
through the lead trolley to the conveyor track to ensure the track is capable of 
resisting the imposed forces.  These calculations shall be reviewed at the initial 
design review meeting. The conveyor Supplier shall provide track reinforcement, 
if required, at no additional cost to Ford Motor Company. 
9.11.9.4 
The chain-assist conveyor chain and trolleys shall be as specified in GVOSS 
Section 9.9 Overhead Monorail Conveyors. 
9.11.9.5 
Alloy steel trolley dog depressors shall be provided at all transfer zones to 
prevent jamming of the chain-assist pick-up dog into the carrier trolley/pin dog at 
the transfer zone. 
9.11.9.6 
The chain-assist conveyor incline and decline vertical curves shall include anti-
backup and anti-runaway devices in compliance with the requirements identified 
in GVOSS Section 9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors. 
9.11.9.7 
Carriers shall not be permitted to accumulate through the chain-assist transfer 
region(s). 
9.11.9.8 
The chain-assist conveyor drive may be caterpillar chain or sprocket drive and 
shall be located at the top of the incline and decline vertical curve(s). Locating 
drives at the bottom of incline or decline vertical curves is prohibited. 
9.11.9.9 
For decline vertical curves, the chain-assist shall be designed to engage the 
carrier prior to the point where the leading trolley reaches the upper tangent of 
the vertical curve. The chain-assist shall remain "dogged" to the carrier until the 
full carrier (trailing trolley) has completely transferred past the lower tangent of 
the lower vertical curve. 
9.11.9.10 
Proper engagement of the chain-assist "pusher dog" to the carrier shall be 
verified prior to the point where the leading trolley reaches the upper tangent of 
the decline vertical curve. e.g., installation of a "pitch-up" similar to a P&F 
conveyor decline. 
9.11.9.11 
For incline vertical curves, the chain-assist shall be designed to engage the 
carrier prior to the point where the leading trolley reaches the lower tangent of 
the lower incline vertical curve and shall remain dogged to the carrier until the full 
carrier (trailing trolley) has completely transferred past the upper tangent of the 
upper vertical curve. 
9.11.9.12 
The chain-assist conveyor and drive/take-up shall be independently supported 
from the FCS conveyor track.  A service platform with access ladder from the 
plant finished floor and/or basket guard level shall be provided for maintenance 
access to the drive and take-up. 
9.11.9.13 
All elevated drives shall include a service rail installed above the drive to facilitate 
removal/replacement of the reducer, motor, and other large or bulky components.  
The rail shall be complete with curved sections, if required, to provide adequate 
access for removal of components. The service rail shall extend beyond the drive 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 220 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
platform so that components may be lowered to the plant floor. The service rail 
shall be complete with double ends stops, and trolley suitable for the capacity of 
the rail. Attachments shall be included to allow installation of a chain fall on the 
trolley. 
9.11.9.14 
The drive shall be VFD controlled with the capability to indicate if a torque 
overload condition exists.  If an overload exists, the drive system shall be 
automatically shut down and the appropriate fault alarms/indicators shall be 
activated and/or displayed at the control panel(s). The overload limit switch circuit 
shall be “fail-safe” design, with limit switch actuator “maintained” during normal 
torque condition. The actuator shall be released, returning to normal position 
when overload condition exists.  This normal position shall indicate a “fault” 
condition. 
9.11.9.15 
Chain-assist conveyor drives shall utilize an integral gearmotor with disc brake 
and shall be designed as an independent modular unit. 
9.11.9.16 
The chain-assist conveyor drive assembly shall be equipped with an integrally 
mounted electrically released spring set “fail-safe” caliper disc brake. The brake 
shall be normally held open and shall close when power is lost, or the conveyor 
is not running allowing the carrier(s) to come to a safe stop. The brake shall be 
self-adjusting and shall include an external manual release lever. The brake shall 
be engaged through electrical interlocks when the conveyor is not running.  
Brakes may be disengaged by electrical interlocks or using the manual release 
lever. The brake shall be designed to be capable of holding a load equal to 200 
percent of the conveyor drive rating. 
9.11.9.17 
The chain-assist conveyor shall include a spring tensioned screw type take-up 
assembly consisting of a fixed outer frame and a floating inner frame. The take-
up shall be installed on the return chain side of the chain-assist conveyor and 
shall be located after the drive at a point of minimum chain tension. 
9.11.9.18 
The take-up assembly shall be designed as an independent modular unit and 
shall be completely shop assembled and tested prior to shipping, including all 
limit switches, etc. The effective travel of the take-up shall be sufficient to remove 
one complete dog space plus a minimum of 2’-0” (610 mm) additional travel for 
disassembly and re-assembly of the conveyor chain. Visual factory markers shall 
be installed on the fixed outer frame and movable inner frame indicating 
maximum limits of travel to alert maintenance personnel that an adjustment is 
required. e.g., green indicating acceptable operating range, yellow indicating 
adjustment required, red indicating fully extended. All expansion joints shall be 
lapped in the direction of travel and shall be feathered to allow smooth trolley 
passage. 
9.11.9.19 
A central lubrication block shall be provided, and shop piped for access to all 
required drive and take-up assembly lube points. 
9.11.9.20 
The sequence of mechanical operations shall be such that the carrier lead/towing 
trolley (with pickup dog) arrives in position for pick-up in the transfer zone prior to 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 221 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
the chain-assist chain dog arrival and yet not engage the previously transferred 
carrier. 
9.11.9.21 
The entering/exiting friction drive assemblies and the chain-assist conveyor shall 
be electrically interlocked to ensure smooth and continuous flow through the 
transfer zone(s). The entering friction drive(s) shall stop if the chain-assist 
conveyor is stopped when the chain is engaged with a carrier that has not yet 
cleared the friction drive(s) control zone. Examples of control interlocks to be 
included are: (a) carrier is in transfer, (b) carrier is ready to transfer, (c) carrier is 
transferred, (d) carrier is out of position, etc. 
9.11.9.22 
A field located pushbutton station shall be installed with indicating lights to show 
all position(s) of the carrier(s) and transfer devices and to allow for manual 
operation of the transfer and related devices. The field located pushbutton station 
shall indicate all transfer faults and "out-of-sequence” conditions and shall 
contain pushbuttons and controls for manual operation and reset. 
9.11.10 
Carrier Positioner 
9.11.10.1 
A carrier positioner shall be installed at every automatic load and unload 
operation, or whenever precision carrier location is required. 
9.11.10.2 
Sufficient quantity and type of positioner(s) shall be installed to accurately locate 
all carriers in all required axis. The location tolerance shall be as specified in the 
applicable project specification. 
Reference drawing number 3ZF-5540-36291 for a carrier positioner that locates 
the carrier arm. 
9.11.10.3 
The positioner shall be electrically or pneumatically operated and may be 
required to act upon the actual carrier, instead of the carrier trolleys. 
9.11.10.4 
Removed 
9.11.10.5 
Sufficient controls and interlocks shall be provided to prevent damage to 
personnel, equipment, or product by improper sequencing or “out of position” 
conditions of the positioning device. The conveyor Contractor shall coordinate all 
required interlocks with the tooling/transfer Contractor. 
9.11.10.6 
A field located push button or HMI station shall be installed with indicating lights 
to show all position(s) of the carrier(s) and positioning devices and to allow for 
manual operation of the positioner and related devices.  The field located station 
shall indicate all positioner faults and "out-of-sequence” conditions and shall 
contain pushbuttons and controls for manual operation and reset. 
9.11.11 
Part Carrier 
9.11.11.1 
Carriers shall be shop assembled complete and shipped to the site; i.e., free 
trolleys shall be assembled to load bars, load pins, and tow bars, carrier locating 
pin and pusher pin, etc. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 222 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.11.11.2 
The conveyor carrier design shall be such that a single trolley wheel load does 
not exceed 400 pounds (182 kg), assuming a 4-inch (100 mm) diameter trolley 
wheel on a flat horizontal track with no influence from inclines, declines or chain 
tension. Refer to GVOSS Section 9.12 for additional specifications regarding 
carrier trolley design requirements. 
9.11.11.3 
Load bars shall be provided to attach the trolleys to the part carrier or part hook. 
Ideally, the load bar shall be designed such that the part/carrier load is equally 
distributed to two (2) load-bearing trolley assemblies, minimum.  Load bars shall 
be fabricated from structural steel shapes or steel tubing in such a manner that 
presents a smooth flat bearing surface to the friction drive friction wheel and 
pressure roller. Refer to GVOSS Section 9.12 for additional specifications 
regarding carrier load bar design requirements. 
9.11.11.4 
Friction conveyor systems shall be designed to prohibit carrier-to-carrier 
accumulation. However, to protect the conveyed product against inadvertent 
carrier accumulation and, where accumulation may be required on a chain-assist 
conveyor, towbars shall be provided. Towbars shall be fabricated from structural 
steel shapes or steel tubing in such a manner that presents a smooth flat bearing 
surface to the friction drive friction wheel and pressure roller. Refer to GVOSS 
Section 9.12 for additional specifications regarding carrier tow-bar design 
requirements. 
9.11.11.5 
A king pin shall be used to provide a pivot type connection to mount the load-bar 
to the carrier trolley. Refer to GVOSS Section 9.12 for additional specifications 
regarding carrier king pin design requirements. 
9.11.11.6 
All carrier load bars shall be labeled by the carrier Supplier. Labels shall be 3 
inch (75 mm) tall black characters on a white background. These labels may be 
installed by stenciling, decaling, or the installation of tags. The carrier 
identification labels shall be permanent and shall be designed and located in 
such a way that they are visible from both sides of the carrier and are not 
damaged by the process or friction drive equipment as the carrier travels along 
the conveyor path. 
9.11.11.7 
The standard FCS carrier trolley shall be constructed from a generic fabricated 
steel or cast-iron trolley body and shall incorporate polyurethane coated load- 
and guide-wheels. i.e., proprietary trolley designs shall not be accepted without 
prior written approval from the Owner. Steel load-/guide-wheels may be used for 
applications where the carrier wheels are exposed to high heat or other 
environmental conditions that are not compatible with the polyurethane coated 
wheels. It is the Contractors responsibility to select the proper wheel for the 
specific application. The wheel and rail configuration shall be identified in the bid 
proposal. 
9.11.11.8 
The carrier shall be designed such that the center-of-gravity is centered about 
the center of the conveyor for both the loaded and unloaded carrier condition. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 223 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.11.11.9 
The carrier assembly shall include a stabilizer wheel/roller assembled to the part 
hook. The stabilizing wheel/roller shall engage a stabilizer rail at the load/unload 
stations. The stabilizer wheel mounting bracket shall be designed to provide 
adjustment on the horizontal and vertical planes – i.e., the x-, y- and z-axis – to 
ensure all carriers interact with the stabilizer rail as needed. 
9.11.11.10 
The carrier shall incorporate a positioner stop block that will be engaged by the 
positioning device at the load/unload stations. The positioning block mounting 
bracket shall provide adjustment on the horizontal plane. i.e., the x- and y-axis. 
9.11.11.11 
All FCS part hooks/carriers shall be provided with a secondary safety restraint as 
specified in GVOSS Section 9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors. The 
secondary restraint orientation must be designed to eliminate the possibility of 
binding during chording through the system. 
9.11.12 
Repair Spur 
9.11.12.1 
The conveyor system shall include a repair spur located on the empty side of the 
conveyor system. The repair spur shall be sized to contain a minimum of two 
complete carrier/skillet assemblies for maintenance access, including space 
sufficient to store the required carrier checking fixtures(s), while allowing carriers 
to pass on the mainline. The carrier checking fixture(s) sourcing and installation 
of the fixture(s) shall be as identified in the project bid specification. 
9.11.12.2 
When elevated, the repair spur platform floor shall be constructed of solid non-
slip finish plate on structural steel framework. The repair spur shall include 
standard handrail complete with continuous toe plate and access stairs with a 
spring-return gate located at the top stair landing. The gate shall include signage 
that indicates the area is for "Authorized Personnel Only". 
9.11.12.3 
For overhead or inverted systems, the repair spur track shall include an 
inspection gate for each trolley on the carrier assembly. Inspection gates shall be 
positioned to facilitate removal of an entire carrier without disassembly of the 
carrier. 
9.11.12.4 
Visual inspection slots shall be included in the track design for inspection of the 
free trolleys. These shall be provided for each trolley. 
9.11.12.5 
A manually operated entrance switch shall be installed at the entrance to the 
spur.  A manually operated exit switch shall be installed at the exit end of the 
spur. 
9.11.12.6 
An FCS drive assembly shall be installed on the repair spur, prior to the exit 
switch. This drive assembly shall be manually activated via an operator PB 
station located adjacent to the exit switch. 
9.11.12.7 
A field located pushbutton station shall be installed with indicating lights to show 
all position(s) of the carrier(s) and positioning devices and to allow for manual 
operation of the exit and entrance switches. The field located pushbutton station 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 224 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
shall indicate all position faults and "out-of-sequence” conditions and shall 
contain pushbuttons and controls for manual operation and reset. 
9.11.12.8 
An overhead service rail complete with trolley(s) shall be provided to allow 
installation of an air operated hoist. The rail and trolley(s) shall be configured and 
positioned such that a complete carrier assembly may be removed from the free 
rail and lowered to the plant floor. Where required, a removable section of 
handrail shall be provided to facilitate carrier extraction. The removable handrail 
shall be fastened to the adjacent handrail such that the structural integrity of the 
handrail system is maintained. Fall protection tie off and signage shall be 
provided at the removal/insertion location. 
9.11.12.9 
Layout conditions may require an access hatch in the repair spur platform to 
allow carriers to be lowered to the plant floor from the platform. When required, 
the access hatch shall include a removable handrail to guard the fall hazard 
presented by the open hatch. The hatch shall be hinged or sliding, depending on 
the application. The hatch shall be sized for a “one-man” operation (maximum 50 
lbs. lifting/sliding force) The hatch shall be designed for a minimum 20 psf live 
load. The hatch and a 2 feet wide area of the floor surrounding the hatch shall be 
painted with yellow and black "tiger stripes". 
9.11.12.10 
LED light fixtures shall be provided for the platform per FCS Section 26 51 00. A 
minimum six (6) 4’-0” long fixtures shall be provided for 60-foot candles 
maintained light intensity. 
9.11.12.11 
The repair spur platform shall include one (1) 3/4-inch air drop with quick 
disconnect, four (4) Duplex 120v power outlets and one (1) 60a Weld Power 
outlet located centrally to the platform workspace area. 
9.11.12.12 
Carrier trolley load and guide wheel lubricators shall be provided and located at 
the Repair Spur entrance switch location. 
9.11.13 
Carrier Load/Unload Stations 
9.11.13.1 
Carriers may be manually or automatically loaded or unloaded. Unless noted 
otherwise in the project bid specification, carrier stabilizer rails shall be provided 
through the manual and automated load/unload stations. The Contractor is 
responsible to coordinate the final load/unload station design with the VOME 
process engineer and/or the tooling Supplier. 
9.11.13.2 
The carrier load/unload stations shall be designed for an individual carrier 
transfer. i.e., transfer of "latched" carrier trains is not acceptable through the 
load/unload station. 
9.11.13.3 
The charge drive assembly, load/unload station drive assembly and discharge 
drive assembly shall have VFD controlled gearmotors to provide "high speed" 
indexing through the load/unload stations. The Contractor shall provide the 
mechanical drive assemblies, including position sensing controls devices, for 
shop or field mounting to the applicable FCS track sections. The Contractor is 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 225 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
responsible to provide the controls for charge and discharge gearmotors only. 
The tooling Supplier shall control the load/unload station gearmotor. 
9.11.13.4 
A carrier trolley positioner shall be provided at the load/unload station when the 
carrier is automatically loaded or unloaded. The positioner shall be electrically 
actuated as noted in GVOSS 9.11.10. The electrically actuated positioner device 
shall be designed to locate the carrier to within (+/-) 3mm (1/8"). The Contractor 
shall provide the mechanical positioner assembly, including position sensing 
controls devices, for field mounting to the FCS track section. The Tooling 
Supplier shall be responsible for controlling the positioner. 
9.11.13.5 
The Contractor shall work with the tooling Supplier to develop controls logic to 
ensure all the necessary interlocking signals are coordinated at the automated 
load/unload stations. This Contractor shall be responsible to connect to the 
tooling Supplier provided Interlock Panel. 
9.11.13.6 
The falling parts guard shall be configured to provide automation or robot end-
effector and part clearance at the load/unload operation. The Contractor shall 
provide a maintenance catwalk with ladder access on both sides, as shown in 
Figure No. 9.11.13.7, at these locations. The load/unload station shall include fall 
hazard identification and protection in compliance with GVOSS Section 9.4 
Safety Requirements for Conveyors. e.g., including fall protection tie-offs, tread 
plate, rope curtain and signage, etc.

9.12 
OVERHEAD POWER AND FREE CONVEYORS 
9.12.1 
General 
9.12.1.1 
This Section is to be used in conjunction with the following specifications.  This 
document shall supersede any conflicts between this specification and those 
listed below. 
 
1. 
GVOSS Section 9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors 
 
2. 
GVOSS Section 9.5 Conveyor Mechanical Engineering 
 
3. 
GVOSS Section 9.6 Conveyor Controls Engineering 
 
4. 
GVOSS Section 9.9 Overhead Monorail Chain Trolley Conveyors 
 
5. 
GVOSS Section 9.25 Lubrication for Conveyors 
 
6. 
GVOSS Section 9.26 Conveyor Support Steel and Platforms 
9.12.1.2 
This Section shall be used in conjunction with the following Branch Drawings: 
 
1. 
3ZF-20712 Pneumatic and hydraulic schematics 
 
2. 
3ZF-20705 Overhead monorail drives and take-ups 
 
3. 
3ZF-20707 Overhead monorail expansion joints 
 
4. 
3ZF-20708 Overhead monorail safety devices, guards, drip pans and 
miscellaneous equipment 
9.12.1.3 
All conveyor components must be Owner approved. Any deviation must be 
requested and approval received in writing from the Owner prior to design 
incorporation. 
9.12.1.4 
Unless otherwise specified, maximum chain speed for design purposes shall be 
60 feet (18.3 meters) per minute. Under no condition shall the actual chain speed 
be greater than 60 feet per minute without written permission from the Ford VO 
conveyor standards engineer. 
9.12.1.5 
All slotted connections shall be field welded after final alignment. 
9.12.1.6 
All holes shall be drilled or punched. Burning of holes will not be permitted. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 228 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.12.1.7 
All load carrying connections at trolleys, load bars, clevis, etc. shall utilize ASTM 
F3125/F3125M bolts with slotted nuts and cotter pins. All pivoting, swivel and 
suspending joint holes shall be lined with a press fit hardened steel drill bushing. 
The bushing inner diameter shall provide a running fit. All pivoting, swivel, and 
hanging connecting pins shall be heat treated alloy steel equal to the drill bushing 
hardness. The bolt head is to be blocked to prevent rotation by means of a 
keeper block. All bolted connections other than mentioned shall utilize either 
slotted nuts with cotter pins or locknuts. 
9.12.1.8 
Unless otherwise specified, all maintenance pushbuttons for overhead devices 
are to be located on the floor in site of the device as well as near the device in 
the overhead basket guard, on the platform, etc. 
9.12.2 
Superstructure 
9.12.2.1 
All conveyor track, service platform, and screen guard support steel shall comply 
with the requirements identified in GVOSS Section 9.26 Conveyor Support Steel 
and Platforms. 
9.12.2.2 
Where process requirements dictate, the conveyor track and hangers are to be 
offset adjacent to bridge crane installations to allow the bridge/hoist to travel 
parallel to the conveyor track. 
9.12.2.3 
The Contractor shall install pipe guards the full length of the bridge/hoist travel to 
avoid entanglement between the conveyor structure and the hoist assembly or 
hoist chain. 
9.12.2.4 
Where the hoist chain shall contact the pipe guards as a normal function of the 
operation, the pipe guards shall be sleeved with a suitable material to prevent 
damage to the hoist chain or assembly. These sleeves shall be free to rotate 
about the pipe guard. 
9.12.2.5 
The P&F Conveyor and bridge crane or other process equipment support steel 
shall be independent. 
9.12.3 
P&F Conveyor Chain 
9.12.3.1 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.9 Overhead Monorail Chain Trolley Conveyors for 
conveyor chain requirements. 
9.12.3.2 
All material requirements for unique power and free conveyor chain components 
shall conform to Ford Manufacturing Standards Section CX2. 
9.12.3.3 
Pusher Dogs: 
 
1. 
The power chain pusher dog shall conform for the same manufacturing 
processes and specifications as indicated in paragraph 9.12.3.2 of this 
Section. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 229 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
2. 
The pusher dog shall withstand a 6,000 pound (2730 kg) jamming load for a 
4-inch chain conveyor and 10,000 pound (4545 kg) jamming load for a 6-
inch chain conveyor. 
 
3. 
The pusher dog chain link shall be located between two (2) trolley 
assemblies. Integral pusher dog and trolley assemblies shall be permitted 
only with prior approval from the Owner. 
 
4. 
Pusher dogs shall be placed at a maximum distance, to be determined, to 
be a function of the system design rate and the unit spacing, using the 
minimum conveyor speed. 
9.12.4 
Power and Free Trolleys 
9.12.4.1 
Design of all power chain trolleys, load trolleys, free trolleys, and side guide 
rollers shall comply with GVOSS Section 9.9 Overhead Monorail Chain Trolley 
Conveyors. 
9.12.4.2 
The free trolley assemblies shall contain high tensile malleable iron or high 
tensile single plate machined wheels, side guide rollers, and where required tow 
bar attachments, accumulating dog actuator, accumulating cam, retractable 
chain engagement dog and holdback dog made of heat-treated alloy steel, 
tripper pins, proximity switch target plates, carrier locating pin and pusher pin. 
9.12.4.3 
The target plates provided for the proximity switch control system shall be easily 
removable, reversible and shall provide capability for use on any load trolley or 
carrier provided with the system. 
9.12.4.4 
The free trolley wheels shall be flangeless and constructed from hardened steel 
machined to suit the contour of the free track flange. The bearing arrangement 
shall have a split inner race locked in place to prevent spreading under thrust or 
radial load. 
9.12.4.5 
The free trolley wheels shall be bolted through the trolley body. Bolted 
assemblies shall utilize studded wheels fastened to the trolley body with a slotted 
nut and cotter pin. 
9.12.4.6 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.25 for free trolley load wheel and side guide roller 
lubrication requirements. 
9.12.4.7 
The side guide rollers shall be constructed with hardened steel races and rims. 
Ball bearing design shall be double row full ball complement with grease retainer 
and seals. The rollers are to be easily removable from the trolley body for 
replacement purposes. 
9.12.4.8 
The trolley body shall be assembled using high strength structural bolts, 
complete with slotted nuts and cotter pins. Deformed thread lock nuts shall not 
be used. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 230 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.12.4.9 
The free trolley shall be designed such that in the event of a bolt loosening at the 
side plates, correct accumulating shall occur between carriers. i.e., no carrier 
jamming shall occur. 
9.12.4.10 
All free trolley wheel assemblies are to be load carrying type as defined in 
Paragraph 9.12.22.2.1 of this Section. 
9.12.4.11 
The system design shall limit a single trolley wheel load to 400 pounds (182 kg) 
on a 4" wheel and 600 pounds (273 kg) on a 6" wheel, with the trolley on a flat, 
horizontal track with no influence from inclines, declines or chain tension. 
9.12.5 
Conveyor Track 
9.12.5.1 
All P&F conveyor track shall be new and free from any imperfections, dirt, rust, 
slag, scale, or other foreign matter. The track shall be formed from AISI 1045 
high carbon steel. All track shall be of uniform cross section, free of warps and 
twists and with tolerances not exceeding limits set by the ASTM. 
9.12.5.2 
Power Track: 
 
1. 
Conveyor power track shall be a 4-inch I-beam at 7.7 pounds per foot. 
 
2. 
All power only track joints shall be assembled with a splice angle.  The 
splice angle shall be 2½" x 2" x ¼" x 6" long LLV. 
 
3. 
The splice angle ends shall be located central each side of the track joint 
welded continuously around on all four sides. The power track shall be 
welded at the top and bottom of both flanges and continuously on one side 
of the web. All welds shall be ground smooth and follow the surface contour 
of the beam. 
 
4. 
Refer to Ford Manufacturing Standards, classification CX-2, Appendix "A" 
for further information. 
9.12.5.3 
Free Only Track: 
 
1. 
All P&F Free Only Track shall be constructed of the same materials and 
methods as standard Power & Free Track, except less the Power Track. 
9.12.5.4 
Power & Free Track: 
 
1. 
Conveyor free track shall be constructed of the following members. 
 
Conveyor System 
Free Track Member Type 
 
4 inch 
Two (2) 4-inch channels at 5.4 pounds per foot 
6 inch 
Two (2) 6-inch MC channels at 12.0 pounds per foot 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 231 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
2. 
Conveyor free track lower flanges shall be adequately reinforced to support 
any load resulting from any single trolley wheel loading exceeding the 
following limits: 
 
Conveyor System 
Free Track Trolley Load 
 
4 inch 
400 pounds per trolley wheel 
6 inch 
600 pounds per trolley wheel 
 
9.12.5.5 
Power and free conveyor track sections shall be jig assembled and welded using 
uniformly symmetrical yokes fabricated from steel plate. The plate thickness for 
track yokes shall be 3/8 inch (10 mm) thick for 4-inch systems and ½ inch (13 
mm) thick for 6-inch systems. Where carrier stabilizer track is provided, it shall 
also be shop assembled using a jig to properly maintain required tolerances. 
9.12.5.6 
Conveyor track yokes shall be spaced at a maximum of 3'-4" (1016 mm) centers 
for straight track sections and 2'-0" (610 mm) for horizontal turns and vertical 
curves. 
9.12.5.7 
Conveyor track supports shall be provided on centers which will limit conveyor 
free track deflection to a maximum of 1/16 inch (1.5 mm). In no case shall the 
spacing exceed 10 feet (3048 mm). 
9.12.5.8 
Engagement bite of the chain pusher dog to the free trolley pusher dog is to be 
achieved by varying the distance between the power track and the free track.  
The three required conveyor track configurations are as follows: 
 
1. 
Normal Track Drop: Used in areas of level carrier transport and 
accumulated carrier storage areas. 
 
2. 
Positive Track Drop: Used on level carrier runs through process equipment 
(automation cells) and in normal manned operator stations on the 
production line where the ability to de-dog the trolley may help facilitate 
repair or maintenance. This chain and free trolley dog engagement is tighter 
than normal track drop. Carriers will not automatically accumulate in this 
bite. It is possible to de-dog a free trolley by applying upward force to the 
chain dog, or other manual means. It shall not be required to disassemble, 
disconnect, or re-adjust portions of the conveyor or trolley to de-dog the 
trolley. 
 
3. 
Closed Track Drop: This reduced track drop is used for safety on all decline 
and incline vertical curves. This chain and free trolley dog engagement will 
not allow de-dogging. Disassembly of the conveyor or trolley shall be 
required to de-dog a free trolley in this bite. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 232 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.12.5.9 
All power and free track joints, and free track joints shall be assembled by means 
of modular (bolted) construction. 
 
1. Track yokes shall be installed on each end of the track section. Both the 
power and free track sections shall be cut square and flush with the outboard 
edges of the track yokes. 
2. Assembly of all track sections shall be by means of bolting two (2) outboard 
yokes together from the adjacent track sections. The assembled track joint 
gap shall not exceed 1/16 inch (1.5 mm). Proper alignment of adjoining track 
sections must be maintained. 
3. Bolting the two (2) adjoining yokes at the track joint shall utilize high strength 
ASTM F3125/F3125M bolts, flat washers, and steel insert locknuts. The 
threaded portion of the bolt must not be in the shear plane of the track joint. 
4. No welding at track joints will be allowed. 
5. Conveyor track splice joints shall be designed and located to accommodate 
installation of supports adjacent to the splice. Bending moment and radial 
loads at the splice joint shall be as close to zero as possible. 
9.12.5.10 
All methods of modular construction which do not conform to the method 
described in the above paragraph must be identified by the Contractor.  Approval 
of alternative methods must be received in writing from the Owner. 
9.12.5.11 
All power and free track sections shall be transit leveled and installed to the 
proper elevation. 
9.12.5.12 
The conveyor track shall be installed true and level except on accumulating runs 
where the track may be sloped downward approximately ¼ inch (6 mm) per 100 
linear feet (30.5 meters) following the conveyor flow whenever possible. Under 
no conditions shall the conveyor track be installed which will allow the carrier to 
"drift backwards or forwards" when disengaged. 
9.12.5.13 
One hinged free track inspection gate shall be provided for every 400'-0" (122 
meters) of conveyor to facilitate the addition or removal of free trolleys. 
 
1. 
All power and free conveyor systems shall incorporate a minimum of three 
(3) free track inspection gate regardless of the conveyor length. 
 
2. 
These inspection gates shall be located at the carrier load and unload 
stations and the carrier repair spur. 
 
3. 
The carrier repair spur location will allow the changing of any carrier free 
trolley without interruption of production flow along the main line portion of 
the repair spur. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 233 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.12.5.14 
A straight track section shall be provided between the tangent line of a vertical 
curve and the tangent line of a horizontal turn.  On conveyors where the carrier 
design utilizes two (2) free trolleys, this straight track section shall be the center-
to-center trolley distance, minimum.  When the carrier design utilizes more than 
two (2) free trolleys, this straight track section length shall be the maximum 
distance between any two (2) trolleys of the carrier design. 
9.12.5.15 
All conveyor track power rail and free channels installed in a corrosive 
environment such as phosphate and E-coat machines, etc. shall be of AISI 304L 
stainless steel or Owner approved equal. The track yokes, hangers, bracing, as 
well as all other support steel, members, guards, pans, etc. which are exposed to 
the corrosive environment shall also be fabricated from AISI 304L stainless steel, 
minimum. In certain countries, were availability and cost are in favor of S316 this 
can also be used as an alternate to S304L. 
9.12.5.16 
Carrier stabilizer rail/track is to be provided in all manned operator/production 
zones. 
9.12.6 
Horizontal Turns 
9.12.6.1 
Horizontal turn assemblies, as well as supports, for both traction wheel and roller 
turns shall be designed to support 250 percent of the ultimate chain tension 
which can be applied to the turn, without any lateral movement. 
9.12.6.2 
A minimum ½" (13 mm) thick Sure-Alloy, or Owner approved equivalent, 
reinforcement bar shall be welded to the bottom flanges of both free tracks. 
 
1. 
The reinforcement bar shall be flush with the edges for the full length of all 
horizontal turns. 
 
2. 
The bar shall extend back to and connect to the track yokes. 
 
3. 
A design analysis will determine the proper configuration of the bar stock 
required. 
 
4. 
This reinforcement bar is not required where the Contractor track is a rolled 
section and the bar is an integral part of the form. 
9.12.6.3 
All horizontal turns shall be supported with a minimum of three (3) hangers with 
the maximum spacing being 8'-0" (2440 mm) between hangers. All hangers shall 
be sway braced in tension. 
9.12.6.4 
All elevated horizontal turns shall be easily and safely accessible from the 
conveyor basket guarding, or other catwalk, wherever possible. 
9.12.6.5 
Traction Wheel Turns: 
 
1. 
Traction wheel turns are to be used on all power only horizontal turns. 
 
2. 
Traction wheels shall be constructed and equipped as specified in GVOSS 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 234 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
Section 9.9 Overhead Monorail Chain Trolley Conveyors. 
3. 
Where the system operating environment exceeds 300 degrees Fahrenheit 
(150 C), segmented traction wheel turns shall be used for P&F horizontal 
turns, as well as power only horizontal turns. The traction wheel pitch 
diameter shall be as defined in GVOSS Section 9.9 Overhead Monorail 
Chain Trolley Conveyors. These segmented traction wheel turns shall not 
exceed 30 degrees between any two (2) traction wheels. 
9.12.7 
P&F Roller Turns 
9.12.7.1 
All power and free horizontal turns shall utilize roller turns except in cases where 
the operating environment exceeds 300 degrees Fahrenheit (150 C). These 
installations shall utilize segmented traction wheel turns as defined in Paragraph 
9.12.6.5.3 of this Section. 
9.12.7.2 
Rollers shall be constructed of a hardened steel machined shell with a double 
row of tapered roller bearings, grease retainer seals, ¾ inch diameter axle drilled 
through the center for lubrication and a ball type check grease fitting.  Assembly 
nuts are to be steel insert heavy full locknuts.  Each roller is to have a radial load 
rating of 2,000 pounds (910 kg) at 100 rpm. 
9.12.7.3 
The minimum roller turn radius shall be equal to 0.85 times the maximum center 
distance between free trolleys on the carrier assembly, but never less than 4'-0" 
(1220 mm). 
9.12.8 
Vertical Curves 
9.12.8.1 
All vertical curves shall be rolled smooth and true radius.  Straight sections, if 
required between the curved sections, shall be tangent to the upper/lower curve 
sections. 
9.12.8.2 
All vertical curves shall be suspended by a minimum of three (3) hangers with the 
maximum spacing being 8'-0" (2440 mm) between hangers. All hangers shall be 
sway braced in tension. 
9.12.8.3 
Power Only Vertical Curves – Power only vertical curves shall meet the following 
requirements: 
 
Conv. Free Track Type 
Minimum Radius 
Maximum Slope

function of the system operating intent as determined by the part 
and carrier design. However, the following are design parameters 
which must not be violated: 
 
Conv. Free Track Type

9.12.8.5 
All power and free vertical curve assemblies are to be of closed track drop type. 
9.12.8.6 
Pusher dog confinement angles shall be provided the full length of the vertical 
curve assembly on power and free vertical curves. 
9.12.8.7 
A minimum ½" (13 mm) thick Sure-Alloy, or Owner approved equivalent, 
reinforcement bar is to be welded to the bottom flanges of both free tracks, 
 
1. 
The reinforcement bar shall be flush with the free track channel toes for the 
full length of the vertical curve. 
 
2. 
The reinforcement bar shall extend back to and connect to the track yokes. 
 
3. 
Design analysis will determine the proper configuration of the reinforcement 
bar stock required. 
9.12.8.8 
On delivery chain conveyors, an automatic air operated stop shall be installed at 
the bottom of inclines prior to the conveyor track drop transition and at the top of 
declines prior to the inclined power and free track section, unless otherwise 
approved with a signed deviation request. 
9.12.8.9 
Power & Free Decline Curves: 
 
1. 
On power and free decline curves the transition from normal to closed track 
drop shall occur a minimum of 1'-0" (305 mm) prior to the top curve tangent 
line. 
 
2. 
All power and free declines shall be preceded by a minimum 4'-0" (1220 
mm) long inclined section of the power and free track, with a slope of one 
inch per foot. It shall be located so that at least one load bearing trolley is on 
the inclined section before the towing trolley enters the transition zone to 
closed track drop. 
 
3. 
The transition from closed track drop back to normal track drop shall occur 
at the bottom of the decline at the front trolley position when the rear trolley 
of the carrier is past the bottom tangent line. 
9.12.8.10 
Power & Free Incline Curves: 
 
1. 
On power and free incline curves the transition from normal to closed track 
drop shall occur at a location which will insure correct and positive 
engagement between the free trolley and the chain dog. Load bearing free 
trolleys shall not be allowed to enter the vertical incline until positive 
engagement of the tow trolley to chain dog is achieved. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 236 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
2. 
The transition from closed track drop back to normal track drop shall occur 
at the top of the incline at the front trolley position when the rear trolley of 
the carrier is a minimum 1'-0" past the top tangent line.  A mechanical anti-
backup device shall be installed to prevent a carrier from rolling back into 
the vertical incline, once it has passed this transition section. 
9.12.9 
Expansion Joints 
9.12.9.1 
Expansion joints shall be provided at all building expansions where the top of the 
power track is less than 4'-0" (1220 mm) below the bottom chord of truss, and 
within process equipment producing temperature rises. 
9.12.9.2 
Power only expansion joints shall be as specified in GVOSS Section 9.9 
Overhead Monorail Chain Trolley Conveyors. 
9.12.9.3 
Expansion joint construction shall allow for a maximum travel of 2 inches (100 
mm). One expansion joint shall be provided for each 1½ inches (38 mm) 
expansion. 
9.12.9.4 
Expansion joints for conveyors shall be calculated at a rate of 0.8 inches (20 mm) 
of growth per every 100 degrees Fahrenheit (38 C) temperature rise for every 
100'-0" (30.5 meters) of track assembly. 
9.12.9.5 
Each side of each expansion joint shall be adequately supported to prevent 
deflection, deformation, or binding due to imposed live and static loads. 
9.12.9.6 
The expansion joint gap shall be calculated to suit anticipated building expansion 
and contraction in-line with the building structure and gradient temperature at the 
time of installation. 
9.12.9.7 
The expansion joint design shall be the reinforced matching half-track assembly 
type sections contained within a guiding steel envelope. The guiding steel 
envelope is to be welded to one half of the track section. Each power and free 
track to be offset half type allowing the trolley wheels to bear on a track half while 
traveling over the gap. 
9.12.10 
Track Switches 
9.12.10.1 
Carriers shall not be permitted to accumulate through the individual track 
switches. 
9.12.10.2 
All bearings and pivots of the track switch shall have lubrication fittings installed.  
The fittings are to be easily accessible through use of remote lubrication blocks 
as required. 
9.12.10.3 
Air stops shall be installed in conjunction with track switches. Refer to Paragraph 
9.12.15 of this Section for additional P&F stop design info. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 237 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.12.10.4 
Track Switch Tongue: 
 
1. 
A track switch assembly shall consist of a precision cut Sure-Alloy or 1045 
hardened steel tongue. The tongue shall be attached to a vertical pivoting 
shaft which is to be aligned with two (2) thrust bearings and framing. This 
assembly is to be an integral part of the power and free track assembly. 
 
2. 
The underside of the tongue tip shall be supported by tongue receptacles at 
each switch position. The tongue receptacles are to have lead-ins 
incorporated into the design for smooth tongue engagement. The tongue 
receptacles are to be attached to the bottom flange of the free track. The 
tongue receptacle shall provide a minimum of 1 inch (25 mm) bearing 
surface for the tongue. 
 
3. 
Rubber pads or other material shall be incorporated to absorb the sound 
energy when the switch tongue is operated. 
 
4. 
All moving components of the track switch tongue assembly shall be painted 
Ford Motor Company Safety Orange (formerly M-314). 
9.12.10.5 
Entrance Track (Divert) Switch: 
 
1. 
The entrance switches shall be actuated by an air cylinder which is to be 
connected to the tongue assembly with a clevis bracket. A roll pin shall be 
assembled through the clevis bracket and cylinder rod to prevent rotation (in 
lieu of a jam nut). The cylinder shall be sized to produce a blade tip force of 
150 pounds with a 60-psi plant air supply. 
 
2. 
Entrance switches shall be automatically operated for both switching 
directions and shall be activated by system carrier movement except for 
repair spur entrance switches. The switch tongue position shall be 
monitored by means of proximity devices for each direction. 
 
3. 
Repair spur entrance switch actuation shall be manually initiated by means 
of a manually operated pushbutton station. Similar to the automatic track 
switch, the tongue shall return to main line flow automatically after the 
carrier rear trolley has cleared the “clear of tongue tip” proximity switch. 
 
4. 
All entrance switches shall be provided with tongue position detection 
proximity switches.  Switches shall be mounted in such a manner so as to 
detect the tip of the tongue as it is correctly seated in the tongue 
receptacles. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 238 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
5. 
All pneumatically operated track switches shall include the following 
components: 
A. 
Pneumatic control valves and position detection proximity switches for 
automatic operation. 
B. 
Filter/Regulator unit for air supply. 
C. 
Pre-lubricated air cylinder complete with control valve mounted on the 
valve board. 
D. 
All associated electrical controls. 
9.12.10.6 
Exit Track (Merge) Switch: 
 
1. 
The exit track switch construction shall be the same as described for 
entrance switches less the air cylinder actuation and proximity switch 
detection of the tongue position. 
 
2. 
The exit track switch tongue shall be mechanically actuated by the carrier 
trolley passage. 
9.12.11 
Carrier Stops 
9.12.11.1 
The P&F carrier stop shall be designed and installed such that the stop blade 
and other related moving components are enclosed within the stop frame. At no 
time shall moving components be allowed to extend past the stop frame. 
Removable "see-through" guarding shall be provided to fully enclose the moving 
components. 
9.12.11.2 
All P&F air cylinders shall include adjustable cushions for smooth operation. All 
cylinder connections shall be drilled and roll pins installed. 
9.12.11.3 
Carrier stops shall be provided at, but not limited to, the following locations: 
 
1. 
At all carrier load stations, 
 
2. 
At one (1) carrier space prior to all load stations, 
 
3. 
At all carrier unload stations, 
 
4. 
At one (1) carrier space prior to unload stations, 
 
5. 
At the bottom of incline vertical curves, 
 
6. 
At the top of decline vertical curves, 
 
7. 
At the entrance to horizontal turns, only where accumulation on turns is not 
possible, 
 
8. 
At the entrance to P&F Drop Lifter track section, 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025
