9. 
At entrance prior to drop lift section, 
 
10. At one (1) carrier space prior to stop and pusher transfers, 
 
11. Prior to all chain transfers, 
 
12. At automatic process operation, 
 
13. At one (1) carrier space prior to automatic process operations, 
 
14. On the repair spur at the repair station, 
 
15. On the repair spur prior to exit switch, 
 
16. On the main track prior to repair spur exit switch, 
 
17. On the main track prior to diverting track switches, 
 
18. On the main track prior to merge points, 
 
19. On the secondary track prior to merge points, and  
 
20. At all static workstations and control zones where carriers cannot 
accumulate. 
9.12.11.4 
Stop Blade: 
 
1. 
The stop blade shall not come in contact with the free trolley wheels and 
shall be mounted so as to be self-relieving from the trolley body upon 
retracting. 
 
2. 
The blade tip in the extended position shall bear against a steel block to 
prevent any torque moment transferred to the cylinder rod due to carrier 
pressure. 
 
3. 
The blade and blade side guides shall be constructed of 1045 grade steel, 
minimum. The blade and side guides/slides shall be equipped with lube 
fittings or other means to provide lubrication without disassembly of the stop 
mechanism or guarding. 
9.12.11.5 
All carrier stops shall include the following required components: 
 
1. 
Pneumatic control valves for automatic operation, 
 
2. 
Filter/Regulator unit for air supply, 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 240 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
3. 
Single solenoid valve, 
 
4. 
Pre-lubricated air cylinder, complete with control valve mounted on a valve 
board, 
 
5. 
Quick connect couplings for connection to the air cylinder, identified as port 
A and B, 
 
6. 
Cylinder clevis mounted to the blade with the clevis fastened to the cylinder 
rod.  A roll pin shall be assembled through the clevis bracket and cylinder 
rod to prevent rotation (in lieu of a jam nut) and 
 
7. 
All associated electrical controls. 
9.12.11.6 
All stops, except those located within the repair spur, are to be automatically 
controlled and operated. Stops within the repair spur are to be manual 
pushbutton control. 
9.12.11.7 
All stop blades are to extend into the carrier stop position upon de-energization of 
the stop valve. 
9.12.11.8 
Proximity switches shall be installed to verify correct stop blade positions. 
9.12.11.9 
Anti-Backup Device: 
 
1. 
A counterweighted anti-backup device with noise dampening is to be 
provided at all stop locations. This is to prevent bounce back or drifting of 
the carrier while in the stop. 
 
2. 
If required for correct carrier location, proximity switches shall be included to 
verify proper engagement of the anti-backup device. 
9.12.12 
Chain-to-Chain Transfers 
9.12.12.1 
Carriers shall not be permitted to accumulate through the individual chain to 
chain transfers. 
9.12.12.2 
Alloy steel free trolley dog depressors shall be provided at all transfers to prevent 
jamming of the pick-up chain dog into the free trolley assembly. 
9.12.12.3 
Sufficient interlocks and controls shall be included to protect against problems in 
transferring due to a conveyor stopping while a carrier is "in transfer", "ready to 
transfer", "out of position", etc., or other condition which would put the cylinder 
pusher in compression. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 241 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.12.12.4 
The carrier transfer between conveyor chains shall be as follows: 
 
1. 
Slow Chain to Slow Chain 
A. 
Transferring shall be accomplished by means of secondary dogs on 
the carrier and chain (if required), dog to dog transfer between chains 
without the need of the secondary means, or an automatic air-operated 
pusher. The automatic air-operated pusher should only be considered 
if the other methods described are proven unacceptable. 
B. 
Both slow conveyors and the pusher, if used, must be synchronized 
and electrically interlocked. 
C. 
Sufficient controls shall be included to detect a "missed" transfer, or 
incorrect job spacing. 
 
2. 
Fast Chain to Fast Chain 
A. 
Transferring shall be accomplished by means of a secondary dog on 
the carrier or a dog to dog transfer between chains without the need of 
the secondary means. 
B. 
An automatic air-operated stop with separate solenoid control shall be 
installed prior to this transfer. 
 
3. 
Slow Chain to Fast Chain 
A. 
Transferring shall be accomplished by means of a secondary dog on 
the carrier and chain (if required), dog to dog transfer between chains 
without the need of the secondary means, or a bump across transfer.  
If the bump across transfer method is used, then provisions shall be 
incorporated to allow automatic transfer of the "final" carrier on the 
slow chain conveyor to the fast chain, without the requirement of 
another carrier being present. 
B. 
Both conveyors are to be electrically interlocked so that whenever the 
fast chain is stopped or a carrier space is not available on the fast 
chain and a carrier is ready to transfer to the fast chain, or a carrier is 
in transfer to the fast chain, the slow chain will stop. Starting the fast 
chain or creating space available on the fast chain will start the slow 
chain automatically unless the slow chain conveyor is stopped 
manually. 
 
4. 
Fast Chain to Slow Chain 
A. 
Transferring shall be accomplished by means of an automatic air 
operated stop and pusher and one automatic air operated stop one (1) 
carrier space preceding the stop and pusher, or a dog to dog transfer 
and one automatic air operated stop one (1) carrier space preceding 
the transfer. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 242 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
B. 
Electrical interlocking of the conveyors may not be required. Control of 
the automatic air operated stop and pusher shall be by sensors located 
on the slow conveyor detecting slow conveyor chain dog position and 
the automatic air operated stop and pusher detecting a carrier present. 
C. 
Control of the automatic holdback stop shall detect clearance of the 
stroked carrier from the pusher so that the carrier released from this 
stop does not bump the transferring or transferred carrier. Sensors to 
control this holdback stop shall be provided at the stop and pusher and 
the stop itself. The holdback stop is not to be controlled by the pusher 
position alone. 
9.12.12.5 
Strip Mode Controls: 
 
1. 
Unless otherwise specified, a field located pushbutton operator station shall 
be included to allow "normal" or "strip" modes of operation for all transfers 
from delivery conveyors or production line conveyors to production line 
conveyors. 
 
2. 
Operating mode verification shall be indicated by lights located at the 
operator station, as well as at the control panel. 
 
3. 
When in "strip" mode, carriers shall not be transferred to the production line 
conveyor. 
9.12.12.6 
Gap Controls: 
 
1. 
A field located pushbutton operator station shall be installed containing a 
"key-operated" selector switch to provide for the selection of a "no job gap" 
or "job gap" operating mode to control the entrance of all slow conveyor 
chains. 
 
2. 
Operating mode verification shall be indicated by the use of indicating lights 
provided at the operator station, as well as at the control panel. 
 
3. 
If the "no job gap" mode is selected, the slow chain will stop if no job is 
detected at the transfer when the slow chain dog detection sensor is 
activated. 
 
4. 
If the "job gap" mode is selected, the slow chain will continue to run, even if 
a carrier is not in position at the transfer. 
 
5. 
Automatic operation of the "feed-in" stop shall be the same for gap and no-
gap operation. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 243 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.12.13 
Carrier Pusher 
9.12.13.1 
The carrier transfer pusher assembly shall consist of the following: 
 
1. 
Pusher supporting frame, 
 
2. 
Pusher carriage tracks, 
 
3. 
Pusher carriage, 
 
4. 
Pusher dog, 
 
5. 
Sensors to detect all pusher positions, 
 
6. 
Pre-lubricated air cylinder, 
 
7. 
Quick connect couplings for connection to the air cylinder, identified as port 
A or B, 
 
8. 
Flow control valves, and  
 
9. 
Pneumatic control board with double acting solenoid and related 
components. 
9.12.13.2 
The pusher supporting frame shall consist of structural shape members 
assembled to mount the carriage guide tracks for the carriage wheels, side guide 
rollers and to support for the pusher air cylinder. 
9.12.13.3 
A removable plate shall be installed at the end of the carriage track to allow 
removal of the carriage assembly. The removable plate shall be designed such 
that it will prevent the carriage from being indexed out of the guide tracks. 
9.12.13.4 
The pusher frame assembly shall be fastened to the side of the power and free 
track assembly utilizing bolted frame construction. 
9.12.13.5 
The pusher carriage assembly shall consist of structural steel members 
containing four (4) roller bearing wheels, two (2) side guide roller bearing wheels, 
and an anti-backup assembly. The anti-backup shall be the counterweighted 
pivoting dog type with the pivoting dog constructed from 1045 hardened steel or 
Sure-Alloy steel. 
9.12.13.6 
The carriage pusher dog shall pivot up as the carrier pick-up passes by.  The 
carriage pusher dog shall be 6 inches behind the carrier pick-up dog when the 
carrier is de-dogged from the power chain dog at the transfer.  When the carrier 
pusher carriage is extended, the face of the carriage pusher dog shall be 6 
inches ahead of the pick-up point on the receiving conveyor. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 244 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.12.13.7 
The air cylinder rod shall be fastened to the carriage by means of a clevis 
bracket. A roll pin shall be assembled through the clevis bracket and cylinder rod 
to prevent rotation (in lieu of a jam nut). Clevis mounts and arrangements shall 
prevent side forces on the cylinder shaft to prevent cylinder seal failure. 
9.12.13.8 
All lubrication points shall be accessible when the pusher is in the "home" or 
retracted position. 
9.12.13.9 
A means to lock out pusher movement shall be provided for maintenance 
purposes. 
9.12.13.10 
Permanent tags or other means shall be provided to indicate normal operating 
positions of the pusher carriage and air cylinder. 
9.12.13.11 
Control of the carrier pusher shall be such that the carrier free trolley pickup dog 
arrives in position for pick-up prior to the chain dog arrival and yet not bump into 
the previously transferred carrier. 
9.12.13.12 
A field located pushbutton operator station shall be installed with indicating lights 
to show all positions of pusher, and to allow for manual operation of the pusher 
and related conveyor devices. 
9.12.13.13 
The field located operator station shall indicate all pusher faults and "out-of-
sequence" conditions and shall contain pushbuttons and controls for reset the 
out-of-sequence conditions. 
9.12.13.14 
For additional electrical control methods, refer to Section 9.12.12 Chain-to-Chain 
Transfer. 
9.12.14 
Carrier Stop and Pusher 
9.12.14.1 
The carrier stop and pusher shall be as described in Section 9.12.13 Carrier 
Pusher with the addition of a carrier holdback dog, incorporated into the carriage 
design, and a disengagement cam. 
9.12.14.2 
The carriage holdback dog is to retain the carrier utilizing the carrier pick-up dog.  
This addition to the carriage will retain the carrier while the carriage is retracted 
and in the traveling mode. 
9.12.14.3 
The carriage holdback dog shall be an anti-backup pivoting type dog constructed 
of 1045 hardened steel or Sure-Alloy steel. The carriage holdback dog shall be 
assembled to the carriage. 
9.12.14.4 
The face of the holdback dog shall be ahead and facing the pusher dog.  The 
face to face dimension shall be approximately 8 inches (200 mm). 
9.12.14.5 
The carriage holdback dog shall be cammed up for clear passage of the carrier 
pick-up dog 9 inches (225 mm) prior to the end of the cylinder stroke. 
9.12.14.6 
For additional electrical control methods, refer to Sections 9.12.12 Chain-to-
Chain Transfer and 9.12.13 Carrier Pusher. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 245 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.12.15 
Carrier Positioners 
9.12.15.1 
A separate carrier positioner shall be installed in addition to a carrier stop at 
every automatic load, unload, or process operation, or whenever precision carrier 
location is required. 
9.12.15.2 
A sufficient quantity and type of positioner shall be installed to accurately locate 
all carriers in all required axis. The standard location tolerance shall be +/-0.125" 
(+/- 3 mm). The tolerance measurement shall be taken at a point 3 feet (914 mm) 
outboard of the centerline of the free track, and at the same elevation as the 
bottom of the free track. 
9.12.15.3 
The positioner shall be air operated, with components and construction similar to 
those of a pusher, when possible. 
9.12.15.4 
Carrier positioners may be required to act upon the actual carrier, instead of the 
carrier trolleys. 
9.12.15.5 
Carrier positioners shall be designed to minimize damage to the positioner or 
carrier if the positioner is "out-of-position" when a carrier enters the stop. The 
rear engagement arm shall be "relieving" type, or of such a design which will 
allow a carrier to pass through if the rear arm is extended. 
9.12.15.6 
Controls set up shall be similar to Section 9.12.13 Carrier Pusher. Sufficient 
controls and interlocks shall be included to prevent damage to personnel, 
equipment, or product by improper sequencing or "out of position" conditions of 
the positioner. 
9.12.16 
P&F Conveyor Safety Devices 
9.12.16.1 
Conveyor safety devices shall be provided in accordance with GVOSS Section 
9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors. 
9.12.17 
P&F Decline Safety Conveyors 
9.12.17.1 
Decline safety conveyors shall be provided in accordance with GVOSS Section 
9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors. 
9.12.18 
Conveyor Guards 
9.12.18.1 
Conveyor equipment guarding shall be provided in accordance with GVOSS 
Section 9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors. 
9.12.19 
Drives 
9.12.19.1 
All P&F drives shall be in accordance with GVOSS Section 9.9 Overhead 
Monorail Chain Trolley Conveyors, except as noted below. 
9.12.19.2 
Where P&F drives are located on a power and free portion of conveyor, then 
carriers shall not be allowed to accumulate through the drive. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014

Revision Date: June 8, 2025

9.12.20 
Take-Ups 
9.12.20.1 
All P&F take-ups shall be in accordance with GVOSS Section 9.9 Overhead 
Monorail Chain Trolley Conveyors, except as noted below. 
9.12.20.2 
P&F take-ups shall be located on power only track directly after the drive at a 
point of minimum chain tension.  Alternate locations must be approved in writing 
by the Owner. 
9.12.20.3 
The effective travel of the take-up shall be sufficient to remove either one 
complete job spacing for fixed job centers and production line conveyors, or one 
chain pusher spacing for delivery conveyors, plus a minimum of 2'-0" (610 mm) 
additional travel for disassembly and re-assembly of the conveyor chain. The 
minimum travel shall be 4 linear feet (1220 mm) of take-up movement. 
9.12.21 
Carrier Repair Spur 
9.12.21.1 
Each P&F conveyor shall include a carrier repair spur. The repair spur shall be 
located on the empty carrier side of the conveyor. 
9.12.21.2 
The repair spur shall be sized to contain a minimum of two (2) complete carrier 
assemblies while still allowing carriers to pass on the mainline. On full body 
clamshell type carrier systems, the capacity of repair spur shall be three (3) 
complete carrier assemblies. 
9.12.21.3 
Repair Spur Conveyor Track: 
 
1. 
The repair spur track shall be P&F free-only track. 
 
2. 
The track shall include an inspection gate for each trolley on the respective 
carrier assembly. Inspection gates shall be positioned to facilitate removal of 
an entire carrier without disassembly of the carrier. 
 
3. 
Visual inspection slots shall be included in the track design for inspection 
and lubrication of the free trolleys. These inspection slots shall be provided 
for each trolley. 
 
4. 
Inspection slots shall be provided on both the main line track as well as the 
repair spur track. The main line inspection slots shall be located prior to the 
Repair Spur track switch. 
 
5. 
An automatic divert track switch shall be installed at the entrance to the 
repair spur. A merge switch shall be installed at the exit end of the spur. 
 
6. 
An operator station actuated air stop shall be installed on the repair spur 
prior to the merge switch. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 247 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.12.21.4 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.26.9.11 for Repair Spur flooring material type. 
9.12.21.5 
Fire protection shall be provided as required under the platform. 
9.12.21.6 
A 30" (762 mm) wide access stair shall be provided to access the repair spur 
platform. A spring-return gate shall be provided at the top of the stair, complete 
with signage indicating “Maintenance Access Only” to this area. 
9.12.21.7 
Sheet metal guarding shall be installed along the handrail, all sides of the 
platform.  This guarding shall extend up a minimum of 24" (610 mm) height. 
9.12.21.8 
An overhead service rail complete with trolley shall be provided to allow 
installation of an air-operated hoist. The rail shall be positioned so that a 
complete carrier assembly may be removed from the rail and lowered to the plant 
floor. 
9.12.21.9 
Where required to provide clearance to facilitate lowering the carriers to the plant 
floor, a removable handrail shall be provided. 
9.12.21.10 
The removable handrail shall be designed and sized for "one-man" operation. 
Each section shall be limited to a maximum 50 lb. (23 kg) weight. 
9.12.21.11 
The platform flooring shall be “tiger stripe” painted – i.e., yellow and black hash 
marks – in the area of the removable handrail measuring 6 feet (1828 mm) 
surrounding the fall hazard opening to the plant floor. An approved tie-off shall be 
provided in compliance with FAS08-108. 
9.12.21.12 
An air service make-up unit, complete with dual Filter/Regulator units, shall be 
installed at the platform handrail. 
9.12.21.13 
LED light fixtures shall be provided for the platform per FCS Section 26 51 00. A 
minimum six (6) 4’-0” long fixtures shall be provided for 60-foot candles 
maintained light intensity. 
9.12.21.14 
The conveyor stops shall be installed or positioned on both the main track and 
repair spur track to provide clear access from the stairs to the repair spur. 
9.12.22 
Carrier Load Bars and Tow Bars 
9.12.22.1 
Long tow bars and load bars which interfere with the track on vertical curves or 
position the load away from the center of the track on horizontal turns causing 
trolley tilting are not to be used. Additional trolleys and tow bars are to be added 
to the carrier assembly to avoid these conditions. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 248 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.12.22.2 
Load Bars: 
 
1. 
Load bars shall be provided when the part carrier or part hook loads are 
excessive for one trolley assembly and are to be distributed to two (2) trolley 
assemblies. Due to the shifting of load center of gravity on inclines and 
declines, the design of the load bar and the number of load bearing trolleys 
shall limit the resultant dynamic loading on any one wheel of a trolley 
assembly to 75 percent of the manufacturer recommended wheel load 
rating. 
 
2. 
Load bars are to be fabricated of structural steel or tubing and shall be 
equipped with horizontal pivots to connect to the trolley king pin. The 
horizontal swivel shall utilize a hardened steel bushing press fit into the load 
bar. The inner diameter of this bushing shall have a running fit interface with 
the king pin. The bottom surface of the bushing shall rest on a hardened 
steel and spherical washer which shall rest on the king pin shoulder or on 
slotted nuts equipped with a cotter pin in most cases. A lubrication fitting 
shall be provided on the pivot assembly. 
 
3. 
Full body, heavy load carriers shall be as defined in Paragraph 9.12.22.2.2 
above, except for the following changes: 
A. 
The hardened steel bushing shall have its inside diameter bored and 
equipped with press fit Oilite bushings, or Owner approved equivalent, 
which will produce the running fit interface with the king pin. 
B. 
The bottom surface of the bushing shall rest on a Bear-N-Bronze 
bearing, or Owner approved equivalent, between two (2) hardened 
steel washers which shall rest on the king pin shoulder. 
 
4. 
Alternate design utilizing opposing tapered roller bearings, shall be 
considered for full body (heavy) loads/carriers. 
9.12.22.3 
Tow Bars: 
 
1. 
Tow bars shall be provided when required to connect the leading power and 
free trolley to the intermediate of load bearing free trolley.  
 
2. 
Tow bars are not to be load bearing.  
 
3. 
Tow bars shall be constructed from structural steel members or tubing, 
equipped with clevises on each end which will form a universal joint when 
connected with the trolley tow bar rings. 
 
4. 
Under certain conditions due to excessive impact loads, or as specified by 
the Owner, shock absorbing type tow bars shall be used. The shock 
absorbing tow bars are to be Midwest "BB" shock type, or Owner approved 
equivalent. Use of shock absorbing tow bars in any application is to be 
approved in writing by the Owner. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 249 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.12.23 
Part Carrier/Hook 
9.12.23.1 
For overhead carrier design criteria refer to GVOSS Section 9.4 Safety 
Requirements for Conveyors. 
9.12.23.2 
The Contractor shall be responsible for the installation of all part hooks/carriers 
whether supplied by the Contractor or by OTHERS. The Contractor shall provide 
and install all free trolleys, tow bars, load bars, load pins, bolts, washers, nuts, 
cotter pins, safety chains, safety cables, cable clamps, etc., required for the 
complete part hook/carrier installation. 
9.12.23.3 
If the Contractor is responsible for providing the part hook/carrier and the design 
indicates they are to be supported by a load bar, the load bar may be designed 
as an integral part of the part hook/carrier. 
9.12.23.4 
If the part hook/carrier is provided by OTHERS and requires a load bar, the load 
bar shall be designed by the Contractor to accept the part hook/carrier without 
any modification to the part hook/carrier. 
9.12.23.5 
Butt welded joints shall not be permitted when fabricating carriers. All welded 
joints shall be gusseted and include stiff-backs to reinforce the joint. 
9.12.23.6 
The part hook/carrier and load bar shall be designed to evenly distribute the dead 
and live load between the front and rear load carrying trolleys. 
9.12.23.7 
King Pin Connection: 
 
1. 
A pivot type connection shall be utilized to connect the part hook/carrier to 
the load bar or free trolley and shall be made by use of a king pin. If the part 
hook/carrier provided by Others does not contain this feature, the Contractor 
is to provide this type of connection without modification to the part 
hook/carrier. 
 
2. 
All kingpins are to be heat treated alloy steel and shall incorporate a press 
fit hardened steel bushing. The bushing inside diameter is to be a running 
fit. 
 
3. 
Connection of the king pin to the part hook/carrier shall be made by use of 
hardened steel pins or bolts (ASTM F3125/F3125M grade minimum) with 
hardness equal to that of the king pin bushing. This connector pin shall 
incorporate a grease cavity and grease fitting to lubricate the pin bushing 
interfacing surfaces. 
4. 
The connector pin shall be stationary, non-rotating and blocked, yet 
removable. Bolt type pins shall not be used without a slotted nut and cotter 
pin for fastening. The threaded portion of a bolt type connector pin must not 
be in the shear plane of the connection. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 250 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
5. 
The part hook/carrier utilizing a king pin connector shall rest on a hardened 
and spherical washer which will rest on the king pin bottom shoulder. For 
heavy loads alternative design methods may be required. 
 
6. 
When required due to process equipment, the king pin may be electrically 
insulated from the carrier. This insulator shall be of sufficient size and 
strength to withstand all forces experienced. The insulator material shall be 
determined for maximum life and shall be approved by the Owner. 
9.12.23.8 
Secondary Carrier Safety Restraints: 
 
1. 
All part hooks/carriers shall be provided with a secondary safety restraint as 
specified in GVOSS Section 9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors. 
9.12.23.9 
Carrier Identification: 
 
1. 
All carriers shall be labeled by this Contractor.  Labels shall contain 3 inch 
(75 mm) tall black characters, on a white background. These labels may be 
installed by stenciling, decaling, or with tags. 
 
2. 
Carrier identifications shall be permanent and shall not be allowed to be 
damaged by process equipment or other equipment on the conveyor path. 
 
3. 
Each carrier on a particular system shall have a unique identification 
number. 
 
4. 
When carriers are subjected to buildup of paint, sealer, etc., the 
identification tags shall be permanently affixed to the carriers in such a 
manner which will prevent them from being dislodged when the carriers are 
cleaned. 
5. 
Carriers which are subject to build-up paint, sealer, etc., shall have 
identification tags with raised characters, such that they will be readable 
with a nominal buildup of material on the carrier. 
9.12.24 
Conveyor Drip Pans 
9.12.24.1 
All P&F conveyor drip pans shall be in accordance with GVOSS Section 9.10 
Overhead Monorail Chain Trolley Conveyors, except as noted below. 
9.12.24.2 
The P&F conveyor drip pans shall be installed under carriers when carriers are 
exiting process equipment or areas where they are subjected to moisture or are 
otherwise dripping fluids. The drip pans shall be liquid-tite and shall be 
continuous for a sufficient distance for all dripping to stop, assuming "worst case" 
carrier time. i.e., maximum carrier transit time. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 251 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.12.24.3 
The P&F conveyor drip pans shall be complete with drain lines, dependent on 
amount of fluid. i.e., the fluid should not continuously accumulate in the drip pans 
so as to fill the entire floor of the pan during continuous production operation. 
9.12.24.4 
The P&F conveyor drip pans shall be installed under power only track where it is 
installed over process sensitive operations or locations. e.g., seat buildup or 
delivery, interior trim components, etc. 
9.12.25 
Conveyor Lubricators 
9.12.25.1 
Automatic lubricators with pre-mounted heads shall be provided for a chain pins, 
power trolleys, free trolley load wheels, and free trolley side guide rollers. 
9.12.25.2 
Easily accessible remote reservoirs are to be provided for lubrication supply.   
9.12.25.3 
Automatic lubrication programming shall be controlled by a retentive counter 
which monitors conveyor usage. Lubricator system cycling shall be established 
by the Owner. 
9.12.25.4 
The Contractor shall provide at no cost to the Owner service personnel from the 
lubricator manufacturer to give monthly lubricator inspections and maintenance 
for a period of one year. Written reports of each visit are to be given to the Plant 
Engineering Manager. 
9.12.25.5 
Manual lubrication fittings shall be provided on all pivoting, rotating, or sliding 
surfaces, and bearings such as: guide rollers, traction wheel hubs, stop side 
guides, king pins, pivot pins, switch bearings, etc. 
9.12.25.6 
In addition, lubrication of all dog actuator lever points and accumulating can slide 
surfaces shall be performed by this Contractor prior to equipment start-up. 
9.12.26 
P&F Conveyor Drop Section and Indexing Conveyor 
9.12.26.1 
The P&F conveyor drop section assembly shall consist of the following: 
 
1. 
Free track section 
 
2. 
Carrier air stops 
 
3. 
Carrier positioners 
 
4. 
Track alignment cylinders 
 
5. 
Track section carriage 
6. 
Vertical towers guiding carriage 
 
7. 
Shock absorbing vertical stops 
 
8. 
Counterweights 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 252 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9. 
Operating cylinder and/or electric motor 
 
10. All required electric controls 
 
11. Safety caging protecting personnel 
 
12. Service platforms, access catwalks and ladders. 
9.12.26.2 
P&F Drop Section Power and Free Conveyor: 
 
1. 
The power and free conveyor chain servicing the stationary tracks feeding 
and taking away from the free track section shall deliver carriers into the 
automatic air-operated stop prior the drop section. The chain will release the 
carrier to the indexing conveyor and divert then merge in to pick up carriers 
which have been transferred out of the drop section. 
 
2. 
The delivery conveyor chain shall be a 60 fpm (18.3 meter/minute) max. 
delivery speed chain, with accumulating capability. 
 
3. 
Transfer from a production speed conveyor chain directly to the indexing 
conveyor shall not be allowed. 
9.12.26.3 
P&F Drop Section Indexing Conveyor: 
 
1. 
The indexing conveyor assembly shall consist of a 4-inch overhead 
monorail conveyor, pusher dogs, variable frequency drive, sprocket, screw 
take-up and controls. This conveyor shall be mounted adjacent to the drop 
section and stationary tracks. 
 
2. 
The indexing conveyor loop and pick-up dogs shall be of proper length and 
spacing that when activated, it will simultaneously transfer the carrier out of 
the drop section and the following carrier into the drop section. The carrier 
shall be designed to include a method of dogging up the index conveyor 
chain dog. 
 
3. 
The pusher engagement shall be designed such that the lateral forces 
transmitted to the carrier trolleys is minimized. 
 
4. 
The indexing conveyor design shall allow indexing of carriers on an 
individual basis, as well as on a continuous basis. 
5. 
Guarding or other provisions shall be provided to capture all high-speed 
transfer details in the event of shear or attachment failure on the indexing 
system. 
9.12.26.4 
P&F Drop Section Free Track Section: 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 253 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
1. 
The drop section free track minimum length shall be equal to the distance 
from front trolley to rear trolley of the carrier plus 4'-0" and shall be secured 
to the lift carriage. 
 
2. 
The free track section shall include the following components: 
A. 
Yokes, on 2'-0" (610 mm) centers, 
B. 
45 degree vertically beveled ends for mating with stationary free tracks 
in the up position, 
C. 
Flared end plates for laterally aligning the free track to the stationary 
free tracks, 
D. 
Anti-backup carrier stop, 
E. 
Mechanically operated anti-runaway front stop, 
F. 
Mechanically interlocking safety stop at rear end of track, and 
G. 
Strong backing to prevent any vertical or lateral deflections due to 
span and loading. 
 
3. 
The track shall be reinforced to withstand deformation in the event of 
conveyor malfunction, carrier jam, etc. 
 
4. 
Electrically actuated “carriage locks” shall be provided to align the fixed and 
moving track sections and prevent deflection in the track at the joints during 
carrier transfer. 
9.12.26.5 
P&F Drop Section Carriage: 
 
1. 
The carriage shall be designed to position the free track section away from 
the vertical guide towers to allow running clearance between the carrier 
and/or part and the vertical tower. 
 
2. 
The vertical spread distance between all guide wheels shall be at least the 
same as the horizontal distance from the wheels to the free track centerline. 
 
3. 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.9.8 for additional P&F Drop Section carriage 
design requirements. 
9.12.26.6 
Vertical Towers: 
 
1. 
Refer to GVOSS Sections 9.9.6 and 9.9.7 for additional P&F Drop Section 
vertical tower/mast design requirements. 
 
9.12.26.7 
Counterweights: 
 
1. 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.9.9 for additional P&F Drop Section 
counterweight design requirements.

9.12.27 
Synchronous Decking Loop 
9.12.27.1 
This Section provides the design intent related to the design, fabrication, and 
installation of the overhead conveyors and for their integration with Fori or other 
EMS, Moon buggy or similar and independently driven synchronous process 
conveyors 
9.12.27.2 
Design and layout of conveyors utilized for chassis marriage and synchronous 
decking processes shall (unless pre-approved buy Owner) consist of a heat 
treated/track reinforced 4x6x6 Power and Free conveyor, dedicated exclusively 
for use with the subassembly marriage/automated decking process equipment.  
9.12.27.3 
The Vendor's scope of work shall provide for engineering dedicated to 
coordination and interface with both the Owner and Owner's Tool/Equipment 
vendor to assure that conveyor and system elevations, center-line workstation 
process needs, and control interface needs meet all functional, process operation 
and Owner safety department requirements. 
9.12.27.4 
Mechanical System Requirements: 
 
1. 
Conveyor design shall provide one off-line (Owner standard) production 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 255 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
conveyor cat drive, and take-up assembly, per GVOSS Section 9.10 
Overhead Monorail Chain Trolley Conveyors. 
 
2. 
Owner approved decline safety conveyors at the transitions into the 
decking/marriage operations shall be provided, including a separate chain 
comprised of Owner standard X-678 chain components, and carrier retainer 
details on maximum 2'-0" (610 mm) centers. 
 
3. 
Incline vertical curves shall provide both free track & power track incline 
safety devices, all proximity/limit switches, local reset stations, wiring and 
control programming.  Safety devices furnished to be spaced to limit free-fall 
distance to less than 1'-0" (305 mm) in the event of a power chain or free 
trolley component failure. 
 
4. 
The overhead conveyor vendor shall provide and install one (1) Edon, or 
Owner approved equal, cat chain absolute encoder unit complete with 
mounting hardware, field devices, wiring, and all controls and start-up 
assistance. The Edon unit shall be furnished complete with a separate 
dedicated work platform, area LED lighting, all walkway and accesses to 
provide maintenance personnel with a means to access unit during normal 
operations. 
9.12.27.5 
Track Leveling/Centering Requirements 
 
1. 
The final elevation of the conveyor track through the synchronous tooling 
area will be coordinated with the tooling vendor. The overhead conveyor 
vendor shall establish the physical conveyor centerline and elevation datum 
points to be used for both conveyor and tooling installations. The datum 
points are to be a physical marking on a building column in the area of the 
decking process. The final location of the datum points are to be approved 
by the Owner. 
 
2. 
The Power and Free track section for the length of the synchronous 
tooling/decking station area shall be level and parallel across all conveyor 
track sections and level to within +/- 0.031" (0.8 mm) over a 40'-0" (12.2 
meters) linear track distance. 
 
3. 
A carrier stabilizer/cam rail shall be installed for the length of the system and 
shall be centered, level and parallel to the Power and Free track and on 
each side of the conveyor track section. The stabilizer rail shall be a spring-
loaded design to ensure constant downward pressure/contact with the 
carrier stabilizer wheels. 
 
4. 
In synchronous tooling/decking areas, the stabilizer/cam rail shall be 
adjusted to assure that in-process vehicle master locators are true to the 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 256 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
tooling centerline, are level and symmetrical with tooling located guides, 
blocks, alignment pins, etc. and are aligned as required to eliminate 
potential carrier perch disengagement do to manual influence during the 
marriage/decking processes. The stabilizer/cam rail shall be level at the 
lower track flanges to within +/- 0.063 inch (1.5 mm) over s 40 foot (12.2 
meters) linear track distance. 
9.12.27.6 
Control System Requirements: 
 
1. 
Design of the control panel furnished with the system will be of sufficient 
capacity to integrate and monitor function, including end of travel controls, 
required for both the conveyor and tool vendor installed decking/marriage 
conveyors, manual bridge cranes, articulating arms and associated manual 
processes and tools required to complete the underbody marriage and 
platform located component installation processes. 
 
2. 
End of travel field hardware which stops function of the conveyor will be 
furnished in sufficient quantity and per equipment layout requirements to 
include workstation located strobe lights and remote reset pushbutton 
stations, etc. as identified in GVOSS Section 9.6 Conveyor Electrical 
Controls Design. 
 
3. 
Design of controls system furnished for the synchronous decking process 
shall provide for a remote operator station located adjacent to the primary 
underbody marriage operation which will provide production/maintenance 
personnel with a means to hold and/or jog the conveyor in event of a 
conveyor or marriage tool miss-location fault. 
9.12.28 
Testing and Acceptance 
9.12.28.1 
All conveyor testing and acceptance shall be in accordance with Section 01005. 
9.12.28.2 
Prior to acceptance by the Owner and during the facility launch period, the 
Contractor is to provide to the Owner the results of chain tension testing.  This 
testing shall be accomplished utilizing tension links and recording media to 
indicate actual tensions at final installation. The testing will evaluate the conveyor 
based on loaded, gap, and strip sequences. Excessive tensions or slack 
conditions noted during the tests shall be corrected at no cost to the Owner.

9.13 
INVERTED POWER AND FREE CONVEYORS 
9.13.1 
General 
9.13.1.1 
Specifications for Inverted Power and Free (IP&F) Conveyors shall be the same 
as GVOSS Section 9.12 Overhead Power and Free Conveyors except as noted 
in the following paragraphs. 
9.13.2 
Superstructure 
9.13.2.1 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.12 Overhead Power and Free Conveyors and Section 
9.26 Conveyor Support Steel and Platforms for specifications referring to this 
subject. 
9.13.3 
Conveyor Chain 
9.13.3.1 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.12 Overhead Power and Free Conveyors for items 
referring to this subject. 
9.13.4 
Free Trolleys 
9.13.4.1 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.12 Overhead Power and Free Conveyors for items 
referring to this subject. 
9.13.5 
Power Track 
9.13.5.1 
Adjustable legs shall be provided to compensate for expected building 
settlements or variations. 
9.13.5.2 
Power track shall be either two (2) structural 4-inch channels at 5.4 pounds per 
foot or formed from ¼ inch (6 mm) thick plate. 
9.13.5.3 
The power track design shall incorporate vertical conveyor chain containments.  
For channel tracks a 2” x 1½” x ¼” angle is to be welded to the top flanges of 
each track, full length. For formed track a 2 inch (50 mm) vertical leg formed up 
from the top horizontal flange shall be acceptable. 
9.13.5.4 
All power only track joints shall be assembled with a bolted connection.  This 
shall be accomplished through angle sections attached to each end of the power 
only track.  Connections shall be made in accordance with Paragraph 9.12.2. 
9.13.5.5 
The power rail shall be designed with an “open” bottom design configuration to 
ensure that miscellaneous fasteners and other small components and production 
parts used in the assembly operations do not “jam” with the chain trolleys. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 258 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.13.6 
Conveyor Track 
9.13.6.1 
Adjustable legs shall be provided to compensate for expected building 
settlements or variations. 
9.13.6.2 
The IP&F conveyor free track shall be constructed of the following members: 
 
Conveyor System 
Free Track Member Type 
 
4 inch 
Two (2) 4” channels at 5.4 lbs./ft. 
6 inch 
Two (2) 6” MC channels at 12 lbs./ft. 
 
or formed track utilizing 3/8” (10 mm) thick plate 
9.13.6.3 
The free track design shall incorporate a ¾ inch (19 mm) minimum vertical side 
guide roller contact surface.  For channel tracks a ¾” (19 mm) thick flat bar is to 
be welded to the top flange of each track, full length. For formed track a ¾ inch 
(19 mm) vertical leg formed up from the top horizontal flange shall be acceptable. 
9.13.6.4 
Conveyor track yokes shall be spaced at a maximum of 5’-0” (1524 mm) centers 
for straight track sections and 2’-0” (610 mm) for horizontal turns and vertical 
curves. 
9.13.6.5 
The actual yoke spacing shall be determined based on maximum imposed loads 
for each section of track or workstation. 
9.13.6.6 
Conveyor track support legs shall be provided on centers which will limit 
conveyor free track deflection to a maximum of 1/16 inch (1.5 mm). 
9.13.6.7 
All power and free track joints, free track joints, and power track joints shall be 
assembled by means of modular bolted construction with track yokes installed on 
each end of the track section. 
 
1. 
Both the power and free track sections shall be cut square and flush with 
the outboard edges of the track yokes. 
 
2. 
Assembly of all track sections shall be by means of bolting two (2) outboard 
yokes together from adjacent track sections. The assembled track joint gap 
shall not exceed 1/16 inch (1.5 mm). 
 
3. 
The two (2) adjoining yokes at the track joint shall be bolted assembly 
utilizing high strength ASTM F3125/F3125M bolts, flat washers, and steel 
insert locknuts. The threaded portion of the bolt must not be in the shear 
plane of the track joint. Proper alignment of adjoining track sections must be 
maintained. 
 
7. 
No welded track joint connections will be allowed. 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 259 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.13.7 
Track Seal 
9.13.7.1 
All conveyor straight track runs and horizontal turns which are installed below the 
plant personnel walking surface – i.e., plant floor, operator platform, etc. – or any 
locations where production operations are performed regardless of the conveyor 
crossovers shall be equipped with a track seal. 
9.13.7.2 
The king pin opening shall be held to a maximum of 2 ¼ inches (57 mm).  Any 
opening which exceeds this dimension shall be identified in writing to the Owner.  
Written approval for any deviation must be given by the Owner. 
9.13.7.3 
The track seal material shall conform to Mol Belting Company track seal 
specification TS55-Y3. Three-point 2 (3.2) mil Volta or Moly Seal material shall 
be acceptable. If any other material is intended to be used it must be approved in 
writing from the Owner. Track seal color shall be OSHA safety yellow. 
9.13.7.4 
The track seal shall be installed to the top surface of the stabilizer track, cover 
plate, etc.  The seal material shall be sandwiched between the stabilizer track, 
cover plate, etc. and a continuous running retainer bar. 
9.13.7.5 
The closure seal retaining plates and all track seal structures shall be reinforced 
to suit aisle location(s) as well as loading requirement(s) for the individual work 
areas or particular track locations. 
9.13.7.6 
The retainer bar and track seal shall be fastened to the stabilizer track, cover 
plate, etc. utilizing flat head socket cap screws. 
9.13.7.7 
Fastener centers shall be determined such that no shifting of the track seal will 
occur during operation. In no case shall the fastener centers be greater than 10 
inches (254 mm). 
9.13.7.8 
The track seal when installed shall have a 1 inch (25 mm) overlap at the center to 
completely close off the conveyor track opening. 
9.13.7.9 
All closure seals shall be treated with “Moly Kote”.  The coatings shall be applied 
after all track sections are completely installed and cleaned of all contamination, 
and prior to the initial debug period. 
9.13.7.10 
All plating adjacent to the track seal, on both sides, shall be painted black with 
yellow diagonal striping to highlight the potential pinch point. The maximum width 
of this painted zone shall be 3’-0” (914 mm). 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 260 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.13.8 
Stabilizer Track   
9.13.8.1 
Stabilizer track shall be provided in manual and automated production areas. 
9.13.8.2 
The stabilizer track shall be fabricated from structural steel members with flared 
ends. 
9.13.9 
Traction Wheel Turns 
9.13.9.1 
A ½” x ¾” minimum Sure-Alloy, or Owner approved equivalent, reinforcement bar 
shall be welded to the top flanges of both free tracks, flush with the inside edges 
for the full length of the horizontal curve. A design analysis will determine the 
proper configuration of the reinforcement bar stock required. 
9.13.10 
Roller Turns  
9.13.10.1 
A ½” x 3/4” minimum Sure-Alloy, or Owner approved equivalent, reinforcement 
bar shall be welded to the top flanges of both free tracks, flush with the inside 
edges for the full length of the horizontal curve. A design analysis will determine 
the proper configuration of the reinforcement bar stock required. This 
reinforcement bar is not required where the Contractor track is a rolled section 
and the bar is an integral part of the form. 
9.13.11 
Vertical Curves 
9.13.11.1 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.12 Overhead Power and Free Conveyors for items 
referring to this subject except as indicated below. 
9.13.11.2 
A reinforcement bar shall be welded to the top flanges of both free tracks, flush 
with the toes for the full length of the vertical curve. A design analysis will 
determine the proper configuration of the reinforcement bar stock required. 
9.13.11.3 
When a vertical curve section passes through the floor plating or other work 
surface, the areas adjacent to the transition point(s) shall be painted with two (2) 
coats of tiger-stripe paint – i.e., yellow and black “hash-mark” pattern – in order to 
highlight the transition area. The pattern shall be three 3 feet (914 mm) wide on 
each side of the transition area. 
9.13.12 
Expansion Joints 
9.13.12.1 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.12 Overhead Power and Free Conveyors for items 
referring to this subject. 
9.13.13 
Track Switches 
9.13.13.1 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.12 Overhead Power and Free Conveyors for items 
referring to this subject except as indicated below. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 261 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.13.13.2 
A track switch assembly shall consist of a pair of precision cut Sure-Alloy or 1045 
hardened steel tongues. The tongues shall be attached to a vertical pivoting shaft 
which is to be aligned with two (2) thrust bearings and framing. This assembly is 
to be an integral part of the power and free track assembly. 
9.13.13.3 
The underside of the lower tongue tip shall be supported by tongue receptacles 
and shall have lead-ins incorporated into the design for smooth tongue 
engagement. The tongue receptacles shall be attached to the bottom flange of 
the free track. The tongue receptacle shall provide a minimum of 1 inch (25 mm) 
bearing surface for the tongue. 
9.13.13.4 
Switch Seals: 
 
1. 
All track switches which are installed below the plant personnel walking 
surface – e.g., the plant floor, operator platform, etc. – shall be equipped 
with a switch tongue seal cover. This seal cover shall be installed at an 
elevation which is at the underside of the walking surface (cover plate, etc.). 
 
2. 
The seal cover shall be an integral part of the track switch assembly and 
operate with the switching of the tongue. In addition, it is to be spring loaded 
to only allow passage of the carrier king pin.  Both paths of the switch 
opening for king pin passage will be closed at all times except during king 
pin passage. 
 
3. 
All plating adjacent to the track switch seal, on both sides shall be tiger-
stripe painted – i.e., black with yellow diagonal striping – to highlight the 
potential pinch point.  The minimum width of this painted zone shall be 3’-0” 
(914 mm).

9.13.26 
Load Bars and Tow Bars 
9.13.26.1 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.12 Overhead Power and Free Conveyors for items 
referring to this subject. 
9.13.27 
Part Carrier/Hook 
9.13.27.1 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.12 Overhead Power and Free Conveyors for items 
referring to this subject except as follows. 
9.13.27.2 
When required, carriers shall incorporate stabilizer wheels in addition to trolley 
wheels, to maintain carrier “level” through sections of the conveyor system. 
9.13.27.3 
The stabilizer wheel design shall incorporate full width guide track “sweep” to 
prevent fasteners, parts used in assembly, of other small components from 
“jamming” with the carrier stabilizer wheels. Care shall be taken to eliminate any 
operator safety and/or equipment safety concerns. 
9.13.27.4 
Sure-alloy rails shall be installed for the stabilizer wheels to contact. Rails shall 
be replaceable, and shims shall be used to ensure rails are true and level. 
9.13.28 
Miscellaneous Components 
9.13.28.1 
Carriers shall have permanent tags “Caution - Do Not Step” affixed to each end 
of the carrier, in a location appropriate to highlight track seal openings. 
9.13.28.2 
When specified, spring clips shall be included on each carrier for attaching 
“broadcast” sheets, or other production aids. 
9.13.28.3 
When specified, carriers shall incorporate “parts trays” to aid in the assembly 
process. 
9.13.28.4 
When specified, carriers shall incorporate an easily removable containment pan 
to capture vehicle fluid run-off. The pan shall be complete with screen cover to 
lap the pan profile, and a ½” (13 mm) welded coupling with drain plug at the 
bottom of the pan. All pan edges shall be rolled or otherwise smooth. 
9.13.29 
Pit Requirements 
9.13.29.1 
Pits, cover plates, and all related equipment shall be formed and installed in 
accordance with GVOSS Section 9.27 Conveyor Pits, Footings, Trenches and 
Related Pit Steel. 
9.13.29.2 
When conveyor drives and/or take-ups are located in pits below the plant floor 
elevation, the Contractor shall provide removable support beams and cover 
plates to suit removal of the drive and reducer. 
9.13.29.3 
Cover plates over drive areas shall be complete with lugs to suit forklift removal 
of the plates.

 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.13.29.4 
Removable plating shall be sized so that maximum weight of individual pieces is 
no greater than 175 pounds (80 kg). 
9.13.29.5 
Pit Located Drives and Take-ups: 
 
1. 
Drives and take-ups bases shall be anchored to embedded beams. 
 
9.13.29.6 
Pit Located Devices: 
 
1. 
Stops, positioners, etc. located below the plant floor shall have removable 
cover plate sections located above the device(s). 
 
2. 
Cover plates shall be 3/8” (9.5 mm) thick minimum, with angle steel 
reinforcement, complete with disappearing handles at each plate section. 
 
3. 
For solenoids located below plant floor elevation, grating sections shall be 
installed in the pit plating above each device. These shall be small, 
removable sections which will provide a means to visibly verify solenoid 
electrical operation. In addition, these shall be removable, and provide a 
means to access the manual actuators of the solenoid(s). The grating shall 
be secured in a recessed angle frame.
