9.17.5 
Paint Repair Flat Top 
9.17.5.1 
Supports and grating shall be provided by the conveyor Contractor.  The grating 
in booth shall be 3/16" x 1¼" bearing bars with banded ends 1¼" x 1/8", 
minimum. The size of the grating opening shall be 1-3/8" x 4" with the bearing 
bars installed perpendicular to the conveyor. This minimum size is a guideline; 
however, the Contractor shall design to meet carrying capacity and deflection 
requirements. 
9.17.6 
Water Test (Wet) Flat Top Applications 
9.17.6.1 
The entire conveyor structure for wet applications shall be constructed of hot 
dipped galvanized steel.  Bar grating inside the booth shall be galvanized.  Cover 
plate may be mild steel. 
9.17.6.2 
The chain assembly shall meet the requirements specified in section 9.17.7, 
including the following additional design criteria: 
a. The chain pins (roller axles), all bushings, all bearings, inboard rollers, 
hardware to be stainless steel.   
b. Outboard rollers to be UHMW with 440C hardened stainless steel bushings 
(if loading allows) and 304 stainless steel washers.  
c. Inboard rollers (contacts the sprocket) shall be 416 hardened stainless steel 
with 416 hardened stainless-steel bushings.  
d. The wheel bushing shall be type 440C stainless steel, hardened to 55 to 60 
Rockwell C and the outside diameter finished to 16 RMS minimum. 
e. Cotter pins are to be 302 stainless steel. 
f. 
Axles to be 416 hardened stainless steel. 
g. All other chain steel, components, structure, welds, top plates and channels 
on the top plate to be galvanized steel.  Rainbow Cleats - which are provided 
only as maintenance replacement on existing/legacy Dual Strand and Single 
Strand Flat-Top conveyors – must be epoxy painted Ford safety yellow 
(cleats only must be yellow). 
h. All top plate channels to be treated with a permanent textured surface similar 
to Algrip, MEBAC, or Slipnot prior to galvanizing – painted or adhesive 
textured surfaces are not acceptable. 
i. 
Material certification papers for 440C stainless steel shall be submitted to 
Ford prior to processing. 
9.17.6.3 
The pit floor shall slope at each end towards a retention pit.  Drain pits from the 
beginning of the car wash through the water test inspection repair and blow off 
shall be provided.  The conveyor Contractor is responsible for providing holes per 
engineering drawing(s) in the grating for spray nozzles on bottom side of the unit 
(114ZF-2567). 
9.17.6.4 
The grillage steel for wet environment applications shall be hot dip galvanized. 
After welding the head, drive assembly and tail frames to the grillage steel, the 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 304 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
galvanized coating shall be repaired by applying a zinc-rich paint; i.e. cold 
galvanizing. Proper care shall be taken when preparing the galvanized steel 
surfaces for welding, during the welding process and when repairing the 
galvanized coating following the field welds. 
9.17.7 
Flat Top Chain General 
9.17.7.1 
The Supplier shall unconditionally guarantee the chain for a period of one year 
against manufacturing defects and workmanship.  Additionally, where applicable, 
this guarantee shall apply to water test and/or car wash applications which utilize 
Ford material standard M-5B227 F. and M-5B228 F. cleaning materials in 
solution.  Chain installed in a wet or corrosive environment shall be as specified 
in section 9.17.6.2. 
9.17.7.2 
The chain shall have a minimum ultimate strength of 104,000 pounds for dual-
chain construction and 53,000 pounds for single-chain construction. 
9.17.7.3 
The capacity of the 4-inch UHMW flanged roller shall be 1500 pounds minimum 
per roller.  The capacity of steel flanged wheels shall be 2,000 pounds minimum 
each under operating conditions. 
9.17.7.4 
The construction of the flat top chain shall conform to the following fixed 
dimensions (all dimensions are inches): 
 
 
 
 
            Figure No. 9.17.7.4 – Flat Top Chain Fixed Dimensions 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 305 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.17.7.5 
The assembled chain tolerance shall be within plus 0.25", minus 0" or plus 6 mm, 
minus 0 mm in 7 pitches or 10.5 feet. 
9.17.7.6 
The assembly joints shall be paint marked so they are easily located in case of 
field issues. 
9.17.7.7 
Use dual chain construction on common axles. 
9.17.8 
Flat Top Chain Top Plate 
9.17.8.1 
For new conveyors, the top plates shall be minimally 3/8" thick x 17-5/8" long and 
constructed of ASTM A36 Hot Rolled Steel. (Where ½" thick plate is required, 
due to unsymmetrical loading or other project specific requirements, the plate 
shall be ½" x 17½" long.) The top plates shall have 2" x 1" x 1/8" milled channels 
welded to the top plates (with matching finger plate guards as specified in section 
9.17.2 where the chain transfers into the floor). Formed channels are not 
allowed. The gap between channels shall not exceed 1 1/4”. For modifications to 
existing systems, the top plates shall match the existing flat plate. 
9.17.8.2 
The walking surface on the top of the chain, whether flat plate or the tops of the 
escalator channel bars, shall be treated with a SlipNot, MEBAC, Ongrip (must be 
approved by Ford Mechanical and Safety Engineers before use), or Algrip 
permanent slip resistant application.  Painted or adhesive slip resistant coatings 
are not acceptable. 
9.17.8.3 
Cleats shall be provided only on existing/legacy Flat-Top conveyor chain 
replacement projects where the current in-plant process requires the driver to 
use the cleats to set the vehicle spacing. (For new installations, the spacing will 
be set using controls as described in GVOSS section 9.17.1.16.) The cleats on 
the new replacement chain shall be bent, round bar, “rainbows” in accordance 
with 3ZF-32103, sheet 18. The outer two cleats on a chain slat are to have a 
plate welded on the inside to prevent an operator from putting their foot inside the 
cleat. All cleats to be epoxy painted yellow. 
9.17.8.4 
The top plate shall be square to 90 degrees plus or minus ¼ degree for a total 
variation not to exceed plus or minus 1/16” or 1.5mm across the diagonal of the 
top plate. 
9.17.8.5 
The top plate shall be welded to the chain sidebars with the centerline of the axle 
shaft being 3/16" (5 mm), plus or minus 1/32" (0.7 mm), to the front edge.  A 
minimum 36 inches (914 mm) of ¼" (6 mm) fillet weld (min.) shall be applied per 
each set of chain links per top plate for dual-chain assembly.  A minimum 18 
inches (457 mm) of ¼" (6 mm) fillet weld (min.) shall be applied per each set of 
chain links per top plate for single-chain assembly.  The fillet welds shall be 
uniformly distributed in 3 inch (75 mm) long fillets and alternately staggered on 
the sides of each sidebar link for each strand of chain. Staggering application is 
not required if welds are applied robotically. However, a FEA is required to verify 
welds are of equal or better strength/quality as the staggering process.   

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 306 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.17.8.6 
Top plates shall be flat within plus or minus 1/16" (1.5 mm) after welding.  Plates 
with channels shall also be level to within plus or minus 1/16" (1.5 mm). 
9.17.8.7 
Top plates shall be square and in-line with chain centerline within plus or minus 
1/16" (1.5 mm). 
9.17.8.8 
Break all edges and remove all burrs, sharp corners, etc. 
9.17.8.9 
Where required to match existing, Flat-Top chain on chain replacement 
projects only, Single Strand layout configuration Flat Tops shall have cleats 
installed on the top plates. Single Strand configuration Flat Tops shall not be 
used for new conveyor installations. There shall be no sharp edges on any 
exposed surface of the metal. 
9.17.8.10 
The side bars shall be 3/8" x 4" C1040 or Ford approved equivalent medium 
carbon hot rolled steel, construction with offset minimized for reduced side-to-
side clearance between linkages, with holes punched and shaved or burnished 
(process shall be approved by Ford) for a forced fit of 0.003" minimum 
interference for 1-3/8" chain bushings. Holes for the 1" axles shall possess a 
minimum of an 85 percent minimum bearing area and shall have a slip fit on the 
shaft diameter and slip fit on the machined surface to a tolerance of 1.001 to 
1.005 and all burrs, sharp corners, metal displacement, etc., suitably removed. 
9.17.9 
Flat Top Chain Flanged Wheel 
9.17.9.1 
The UHMW (Ultra High Molecular Weight) polyethylene flanged wheel shall be 4" 
or 100 mm in diameter on the rolling face with 1½" or 38 mm minimum width 
having a flange of 5" or 125 mm in diameter which shall be a minimum of ½" at 
the base and 3/8" at the outside.  All hub surfaces to be machined smooth to a 
maximum of 125 RMS.  Material certification papers for the UHMW polyethylene 
(4.5-6 million molecular weight) shall be submitted to the Owner prior to 
machining. 
9.17.9.2 
Sealed cartridge style 4”- or 100-mm diameter flanged wheels shall be 
constructed of a 1045 HR shell that is to be induction hardened to 50 to 55 Rc 
with a depth of case to be .125" on the tread diameter.  The inner component 
group shall be a dual compliment single row deep groove precision ball bearing 
and shall be mechanically retained within the wheel housing.  Roller bushing is 
contained within cartridge. 
9.17.9.3 
Lube style 4" (100 mm) diameter flanged wheels shall be constructed of a 1045 
HR shell that is to be induction hardened to 50 to 55 Rc with a depth of case to 
be .060" on the tread diameter.  The inner component group shall be a minimum 
of one MR 20 sealed roller bearing or Owner approved equivalent.  The roller 
bushing shall be ground to 16RMS and heat treated to 55 – 60 Rc with a lube 
hole to the center of the bearing. A potential material change will be required if 58 
– 62 Rc hardness is desired. 
9.17.10 
Removed 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 307 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.17.11 
Flat Top Chain Axle Shaft 
9.17.11.1 
The axle shaft shall be constructed from 416 stainless steel hardened to 38 to 48 
RC for wet applications and from 1144 CD stress proof (A311) steel for dry 
applications. The axle shaft shall be designed to lock within one of the outer side 
bar links and a slip-fit as per Section 9.17.9.1 with a tolerance of 1.000 – 0.995 
shaft diameter in the remaining side bar links. 
9.17.11.2 
The nominal major diameter shall be 1" or 25 mm.  The shaft outside diameter 
shall be prepared by centerless grinding to a 32 RMS surface finish. 
9.17.11.3 
Legacy - Flat Top Chain Axle Shaft 
9.17.11.3.1 The axle shaft shall include lubricant passages for dry applications. The lubricant 
passages within the axle shaft shall permit entrance of lubricant at the midpoint 
location of the wheels as well as chain roller through the chain bushing. The 
passage diameter shall not exceed 3/16" or 5mm. Lubricant passages are not 
required for wet applications. The shaft shall be fitted with a grease groove as 
shown on Branch Drawing 3ZF-9386. 
9.17.11.3.2 The shaft shall be fitted with a grease groove as shown on Branch Drawing 
3ZF9386 for dry applications. 
9.17.11.3.3 The design shall provide for a ¼" or 6mm diameter hole at each end for 
fasteners, cotter pin (zinc plated for dry applications and 302-304 stainless steel 
for wet applications), which shall be positively locked to hold the bushing and 
wheel configuration secure. 
9.17.11.3.4 The shaft shall be fitted at each end with a threaded Alemite grease fitting. The 
grease fitting is not required for wet applications. 
9.17.12 
Sprocket Chain Bushing and Roller 
9.17.12.1 
The steel 1-3/8" or 35 mm outside diameter chain bushing shall be locked to one 
of the inside side bar links only and the mechanical lock shall be incorporated on 
one end of bushing only. The bushing shall be constructed from 1026 DOM 
tubing for dry applications and 416 stainless steel for wet applications. It shall be 
finished to permit a minimum of a 0.003" (0.076 mm) interference fit with the side 
bar holes. 
9.17.12.2 
The steel sprocket chain roller shall be 1-7/8" or 48 mm outside diameter by 1-
7/8" long and shall be constructed from 1026 DOM tubing for dry applications 
and 416 stainless steel for wet applications. Dimensional clearance shall be 
provided for a lubrication film between the roller and the bushing. 
9.17.12.3 
The bushing and roller shall be hardened to a depth of 0.0025" – 0.040" 
minimum at 55 to 60 Rockwell "C" for dry applications and 38 to 48 Rockwell "C" 
for wet applications. 
9.17.12.4 
The flange washer shall be low carbon steel. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 308 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.17.13 
Flat Top Chain Lubrication 
9.17.13.1 
The lubricant shall be Ford Material Specification MC-1C164-A or of Ford 
approved equal. The cavities shall be totally filled after completion of all 
assembly operations and purging assured by a show of lubricant outside of 
wheels and between sidebars and sprocket roller.  Chain manufacturer shall 
apply necessary lubricant with chain order for all field connections and total 
lubrication of chain after installation prior to any break-in operation. 
 
9.17.14 
Flat Top Chain Shipping Instructions 
9.17.14.1 
Chain assemblies shall be covered with rust preventive prior to shipping, and 
after lubrication. 
9.17.14.2 
All chain shall be palletized. 
9.17.14.3 
Each pallet load shall weigh 4,500 pounds maximum with 7 pitch / 10’-6” chain 
lengths stacked flat and separated with hard wood 2x4's.  
9.17.15 
Flat Top Chain Assembly Drawings 
9.17.15.1 
When deviating from Branch Drawing 3ZF-32103, chain assembly drawings shall 
be supplied for review and approval by the Ford. 
9.17.15.2 
When deviating from Branch Drawing 3ZF-32103, bids received without proposal 
drawings will not be accepted by the Owner. 
9.17.15.3 
Upon completion of install, it is required for the contractor to supply all project 
related shop drawings. These complete drawing sets shall be uploaded into EIS. 
The project is not deemed complete until all drawings have been procured and 
approved by Ford regardless of protag sign-off. 
9.17.16 
Replacement Chain 
9.17.16.1 
Replacement chain shall conform to this specification, except for dimensional 
requirements. 
9.17.16.2 
Chain for extensions to existing conveyors shall match existing chain.  Bearings 
and dimensions must be identical to existing conveyor chain. 
9.17.16.3 
Contractor shall be responsible for obtaining correct dimensional and operation 
requirements. 
9.17.17

Chain manufacturer shall supply with each order to installer, all necessary and 
required tools, such as pullers, mandrels, locators, fixtures, grease gun and any 
other required tools and/or tools necessary to assure total reliability and warranty 
considerations.

 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.18 
SLAT CONVEYORS 
9.18.1 
General 
9.18.1.1 
All new slat conveyor installations shall be a 10’ wide, single-strand layout 
configuration with checker-plate slat chain as defined within this specification. 
9.18.1.2 
All sharp edges that could come into contact with a tire or wheel, including the 
plated edges of platforms and the edges of angles, shall be removed or covered 
by a split, smooth, round tube or pipe. 
9.18.1.3 
All welds shall be ground smooth and all weld spatter shall be removed on any 
surface that could possibly contact a tire or wheel. 
9.18.1.4 
All bolted connections shall utilize a minimum of two bolts. All slotted connections 
must be blocked or welded after final field adjustments have been made. 
9.18.1.5 
All pillow blocks and flange bearings must be blocked following final alignment. 
9.18.1.6 
The drive motor, reducer, coupling, coupling guard, and driven device shall be 
mounted on a common adjustable drive base. 
9.18.1.7 
All conveyor sprockets shall be steel with flame hardened teeth and shall be of a 
solid hub design with one keyway and two double set screws, one over the 
keyway and one at 90 degrees. The sprockets shall be designed for 8 tooth 
effective and 32 tooth actual. 
9.18.1.8 
Provide straight track section on return side of chain under tension in the head or 
tail pit for the addition of a lubricator or greaser as specified in the project 
detailed equipment description. 
9.18.1.9 
The head and tail frame of the conveyor shall be located such that all vehicle 
models manufactured by the plant can be driven on to the conveyor in a straight-
on orientation after the vehicle makes a continuous turn towards the conveyor 
(three-point turns are not acceptable). 
9.18.1.10 
The take-up must be designed so that independent operation of the two sides of 
the take-up is not possible. This is done so that it is impossible to adjust only one 
side of the take-up and create a twisting force in the conveyor slats 
9.18.2 
End-Plate Assembly 
9.18.2.1 
End plates, or guards, shall be provided at the head and tail ends where the 
chain transfers into the floor and shall be painted yellow. 
9.18.2.2 
The new flat top end-plate design shall be sufficient to support the weight of the 
vehicle during transfer to/from the flat top conveyor. Deflection in the end-plate 
shall be limited such that the details do not contact the slat chain during transfer 
in a dynamic or static condition. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 311 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.18.2.3 
For slat conveyor installations, the end plate assembly design shall incorporate a 
sliding design that will allow small parts to pass below the plate but will cause the 
conveyor to stop if a larger object is caught at the end of the conveyor. 
Additionally, the hinged end plate design shall include the following: 
1. 
Designed such that the conveyor will shut down if the end plate slides 1" (25 
mm) from the original position. 
9.18.2.4 
All sharp edges that could contact a tire or wheel, including the plated edges of 
platforms and the edges of angles, shall be removed or covered by a split, 
smooth, round tube or pipe. 
9.18.3 
Slat Chain General 
9.18.3.1 
The supplier shall unconditionally guarantee the chain for a period of one year 
against manufacturing defects and workmanship. 
9.18.3.2 
The chain shall have a minimum ultimate strength of 53,000 pounds. 
9.18.3.3 
The assembled chain tolerance shall be within plus 0.25", minus 0" (plus 6 mm, 
minus 0 mm) in 30 pitches or 15 feet. 
9.18.4 
Slat Chain Electrical General 
9.18.4.1 
Ultrasonic sensors and photo-eyes will be installed at the start of the conveyor to 
determine proper unit spacing. 
9.18.4.2 
A red/green ‘traffic light’ to be installed toward the entry end of the conveyor to 
provide visual cues to operators loading the conveyor so that proper unit spacing 
can be achieved easily during operation. 
9.18.4.3 
Ultrasonic sensors are to be installed at the exit end of the conveyor to stop the 
conveyor if a unit was not driven clear of the conveyor by an attending operator 
9.18.4.4 
A completely designed and assembled control panel with human-interface 
controls and a programming port that is ready for installation on location by 
another contractor.  The control panel must include a ‘lock-out’ point that will 
enable maintenance personnel to safely lock-out the conveyor before performing 
any maintenance 
9.18.4.5 
Run/Stop buttons are to be provided at both the drive frame and take-up frame.  
The Run/Stop buttons should be located in an easy-to-reach location for 
maintenance personnel to operate when required 
9.18.4.6 
A lockable local disconnect for the drive motor must be included. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 312 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.18.5 
Slat Chain Top Plate 
9.18.5.1 
For new conveyors, the top plates shall be minimally 1/4" thick and constructed 
of C1020 Hot Rolled Steel. The top plates shall have a checker plate surface 
texture or similar permanent non-slip surface.  Painted on or glued-on friction 
surfaces are not acceptable. 
9.18.5.2 
For modifications to existing systems, the top plates shall match the existing 
slats. 
9.18.5.3 
When required to match existing, "Algrip" non-slip steel plate, or Owner approved 
equivalent, shall be used for wet conveyor applications. Algrip plate is 
manufactured by Safe Walk, Inc., in Leola, Pennsylvania and is distributed in 
Detroit by Brown Campbell (313-891-2390). 
9.18.5.4 
A yellow stripe is to be painted across the width of every slat (location to be 
determined) such that a ‘continuous’ guide stripe will be formed when the 
conveyor is fully assembled during installation 
9.18.5.5 
Top plates shall be flat within plus or minus 1/16" (1.5 mm) after installation.   
9.18.5.6 
Top plates shall be square and in-line with chain centerline within plus or minus 
1/16" (1.5 mm). 
9.18.5.7 
Break all edges and remove all burrs, sharp corners, etc. 
9.18.5.8 
Each slat section will be attached to the chain strands via interference-thread 
nuts that have been permanently secured to the underside of the supporting 
chain surfaces to facilitate installation and removal of slat sections. 
9.18.6 
Slat Chain Rollers 
9.18.6.1 
The chain flanged rollers will each contain frictionless (i.e. ball bearing) bearings 
to reduce overall chain pull and potential surging. 
9.18.6.2 
All slat rollers are to be fitted with a standard-sized grease fitting (‘zerk’ fitting) so 
that the rollers can be greased with an automatic greasing unit 
9.18.7 
Flat Top Chain Lubrication 
9.18.7.1 
The lubricant shall be Dubois MPG-2, Ford Material Specification MC-1C164-A 
or Owner approved equal.  The cavities shall be totally filled after completion of 
all assembly operations and purging assured by a show of lubricant outside of 
wheels and between sidebars and sprocket roller.  Chain manufacturer shall 
apply necessary lubricant with chain order for all field connections and total 
lubrication of chain after installation prior to any break-in operation. 
9.18.8 
Flat Top Chain Shipping Instructions 
9.18.8.1 
Chain assemblies shall be covered with rust preventive prior to shipping, and 
after lubrication. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025

 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.18.8.2 
All chain shall be palletized. 
9.18.8.3 
Each pallet load shall weigh 4,500 pounds maximum with 9'-0" chain lengths 
stacked flat and separated with hard wood 2x4's.  
9.18.9 
Slat Chain Assembly Drawings 
9.18.9.1 
When deviating from Branch Drawing 3ZF-9386, chain assembly drawings shall 
be supplied for review and approval by the Owner. 
9.18.9.2 
When deviating from Brach Drawing 3ZF-9386, bids received without proposal 
drawings will not be accepted by the Owner. 
9.18.10

9.18.10.1 
Replacement chain shall conform to this specification, except for dimensional 
requirements. 
9.18.10.2 
Chain for extensions to existing conveyors shall match existing chain.  Bearings 
and dimensions must be identical to existing conveyor chain. 
9.18.10.3 
Contractor shall be responsible for obtaining correct dimensional and operation 
requirements.

required tools, such as pullers, mandrels, locators, fixtures, grease gun and any 
other required tools and/or tools necessary to assure total reliability and warranty 
considerations. 
9.18.11.2 
All special tools shall be accounted for and delivered to the Plant Engineering 
Manager of the affected Plant as Ford Motor Company property for maintenance 
and service purposes. 
9.18.12

9.18.12.1

placement of precision component parts is strictly forbidden.

9.19 
 CHAIN DRIVEN LIVE ROLLER (CDLR) & RELATED CONVEYOR 
9.19.1 
General 
9.19.1.1 
CDLR Conveyors are effectively a roller bed made up of individually driven rollers 
wrapped in Ultrex sleeves that allow the beds to transfer convey and accumulate 
components. Typical examples are uses in tire & wheel conveyors and palletized 
seat conveyor systems, but other applications may apply. 
1. Slip Sleeve CDLR conveyors have  
rollers wrapped in Ultrex 
sleeves, or  Ford 
approved 
equivalent, 
that 
allow 
 
accumulation. 
2. Regular CDLR conveyors have rollers without sleeves that do 
not allow accumulation. Regular CDLR conveyor can have added 
texture to aid in holding the components in intended spacing. This 
Section is to be used in conjunction with the following 
specifications.  This document shall supersede any conflicts 
between this specification and those listed below. 
9.19.1.2 
1. 
GVOSS Section 3.2 Typical Machine Components 
2. 
GVOSS Section 3.3 Motors, Gearmotors and Speed Reducers 
3. 
GVOSS Section 9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors 
4. 
GVOSS Section 9.5 Conveyor Mechanical Engineering 
5. 
GVOSS Section 9.6 Conveyor Controls Engineering 
6. 
GVOSS Section 9.25 Lubrication for Conveyors 
7. 
GVOSS Section 9.26 Conveyor Support Steel and Platforms 
8. 
GVOSS Section 9.27 Conveyor Pits, Footings, Trenches and Related Pit 
Steel 
9.19.1.3 
The roller width shall be determined by the application for which it is intended. 
9.19.1.4 
The maximum working capacity of individual CDLR sections shall be determined 
by the individual application for which it is intended. 
9.19.1.5 
The design load weight shall be calculated based on the heaviest part weight 
such the system will be capable of transferring and supporting the worst-case 
load for the application. i.e., complete system fill with heaviest part weight. 
9.19.1.6 
All potential scratch surfaces must be covered with non-marring, non-scratching 
covers to eliminate potential for damage. 
9.19.1.7 
The CDLR conveyor rollers shall be constructed from a minimum of 2 1\2” 
outside diameter (with machined bearing pockets) x 11ga. wall tubing, grease 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 315 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
packed bearings, 11/16” pin retained hex axle, (2) 60A15 sprockets with heat-
treated teeth, Sleeves. Supplier to provide materials review and approval 
documentation to the Owner at the design review meeting. 
9.19.1.8 
Roller center distances shall be designed such that the following is met: 
1. Rollers to be on 6” nominal centers  
for most applications.  
2. Roller chain shall not contain offset  
links.   
3. Any type of part recognition (RFID,  
Prox, etc.) shall be able to 
function at  
required locations throughout the  layout.  
4. Stops within the layout shall be able  to 
actuate 
successfully 
without  
interference with surrounding rollers.  
5. Stops shall be able to hold back the  product as per cycle time 
requirements 
9.19.1.9 
All CDLR to utilize semi-precision bearings on drive rollers. 
9.19.1.10 
All chain sprockets to have hardened teeth (58/60RC). 
9.19.1.11 
All T&W conveyor and related equipment above 6’, floor mounted and/or truss 
hung, shall have tire hold-downs. They shall be constructed for easy removal via 
bolted connection with a maximum length of 11’. 
9.19.1.12 
All CDLR conveyor and related equipment where applicable that have the chain 
drive and/or chain lift mechanism within chain-lift transfers located underneath 
shall have removable drip pan via bolted connection with maximum length of 4’. 
This only applies if drip pan cannot be welded directly to the mezzanine. 
9.19.1.13 
All CDLR conveyors and related/surrounding equipment shall have a gap of 1” 
minimum during the design phase to allow for adjustability – that is, no conveyor 
section shall be in direct contact with one another unless otherwise required. 
9.19.1.14 
Manual removal station(s) must be provided for a convenient means of extracting 
empty pallets in the event of a system breakdown or other unforeseen 
occurrences at every operator unload station. 
9.19.1.15 
Manual insertion station(s) must be provided for a convenient means of inserting 
loaded pallets in the event of a system breakdown or other unforeseen 
occurrences at every operator unload station. 
9.19.1.16 
All manual stations must incorporate pallet length adjustable UHMW 
Polyethylene tapered side guide bars with steel plate backer opposite the 
operator.

STRAIGHT 
9.19.2.1 
The

9.19.3 
ROLLER TURN 
9.19.3.1 
All Roller Turn CDLR conveyor shall meet the requirements found within section 
9.19.1 in addition to the specifications included herein. 
9.19.3.2 
All Roller Turns shall be slip-sleeve, unless otherwise required for the application. 
9.19.3.3 
All Roller Turns shall be a minimum of 5’ radius. Radii shall be designed to meet 
the application for which it is intended.  
9.19.3.4 
All Roller Turns shall have segmented slip-sleeves that allow the product to 
successfully move about the turn. If the application is not to be accumulating, 
then tapered rollers are required. product. 
9.19.4 
CHAIN LIFT 
9.19.4.1 
All Chain-Lift CDLR conveyor shall meet the requirements found within section 
9.19.1 in addition to the specifications included herein. 
9.19.4.2 
The following Teamcenter link gives NextGen rev 3 standards – vendors must 
have access to Teamcenter to use this: 
https://www.teamcenter2pdcloc1.ford.com/tc/launchapp?-attach=true&-
s=226TCSession&-
o=zYF5bE0RBhGXHAAAAAAAAAAAAAA&servername=PDCLOC1 
9.19.4.3 
The integrated roller chain elevation change shall run faster than the CDLR that 
is integrated into the same roller table. The actual speed shall be designed to 
meet the application’s design rate. 
9.19.4.4 
A stop shall be integrated into the functionality of the Chain-Lift table. The stop 
shall meet the requirements found within section 9.19.9. Appropriate type of stop 
must be determined for the application. 
9.19.5 
TURNTABLE 
9.19.5.1 
All Turn Table CDLR conveyor shall meet the requirements found within section 
9.19.1 in addition to the specifications included herein. 
9.19.5.2 
A stop shall be integrated into the functionality of the Turn Table. The stop shall 
meet the requirements found within section 9.19.9, if applicable. Appropriate type 
of stop must be determined for the application; however, the stop will likely be a 
hard-stop – meaning it will be motionless. Most Turn Table stops are integrated 
into the Turn Table’s roller frame. This would be an acceptable design. 
9.19.6 
DRAWBRIDGE 
9.19.6.1 
All drawbridge conveyors shall meet the requirements found within section 9.19.1 
in addition to the specifications included herein. 
9.19.6.2 
All Drawbridges shall be slip-sleeve.

Revision Date: June 8, 2025

9.19.6.3 
All Drawbridges shall be accompanied by dock trailer-tire guides, de-springer 
guides, and a dock-lock for proper alignment during truck-trailer docking 
operation. Dock-lock is provided by others. All items listed shall be interlocked 
with the truck-trailer. 
9.19.7 
CONTINUOUS VERTICLE CONVEYOR (CVC), Related* 
9.19.7.1 
The number of carriages shall be maximized to meet the required cycle time for 
the application and fit within the footprint of the overall stroke of the chain. 
9.19.7.2 
Stations immediately upstream and downstream of a CVC shall be Slip-Sleeve 
CDLR. 
9.19.7.3 
The three CDLR rollers immediately upstream and downstream of a CVC shall 
be a non-slip material and durometer coated to suit the application, full length of 
roller, and to be slightly larger than the outside diameter of the adjacent Ultrex 
sleeves. 
9.19.7.4 
A safety locking mechanism must be designed to control potentially stored 
gravity. 
9.19.8 
GRAVITY SILO, Related* 
*Although gravity type conveyor is not CDLR, it is used within CDLR applications 
often and will be captured herein. 
9.19.8.1 
Silos are typically used within T&W applications and shall be designed such that 
the following is met: 
1. Dual stringer channel support structure. Must be provided at  a 
maximum of 45-degree intervals to properly support each wrap. 
Contractor to determine the channel size by the application for 
which it is intended. 
2. Must be floor mounted – tree supported not allowed.  
3. Inside ladder usable from floor to highest level with cage.  
4. Warning level detection shall be provided for low tire & wheel    
stock. 
9.19.9 
STOPS 
9.19.9.1 
Wedge Style Escapements for pallet application shall be/have: 
1. Air cylinder externally side mounted.  
2. Side mounted Arms. 
3. Wedge to match exact angle of chamfer on pallet.  
4. Flange mounted ball bearings for arm rotation.

Revision Date: June 8, 2025

5. Guarding for rotating equipment.  
6. Ability to detect both open and closed positions.The CDLR 
manufacturer shall supply with each order to the INSTALLER, all 
necessary and required tools, such as pullers, mandrels, locators, 
fixtures, grease gun, and any other required tools necessary to 
assure total reliability and warranty considerations. 
9.19.9.2 
Roller Style Escapements for T&W application shall be/have:  
1. Air cylinder mounted overhead. 
2. Heavy duty.  
3. Side mounted arms. 
4. Roller nest hold-back arms with minimum of 3 rollers each.  
5. Flange mounted ball bearings for arm rotation.  
6. Connecting links need to be adjustable. 
7. Guarding for rotating equipment. 
8. Entire frame must be removable.

escapement – not before  the 
escapement, 
so 
that 
the

9.19.9.3 
Shot Pins to be air actuated. Typically paired with CDLR conveyor type, T&W 
application. There shall be guarding for all moving equipment. The Shot Pins 
shall also have the ability to detect both up and down positions. 
9.19.9.4 
Rotary Blade (Flipper) Stop to be air actuated. Typically paired with CDLR 
conveyor type, seat pallet application. The Flipper shall also have the ability to 
detect both up and down positions. 
9.19.9.5 
Inverted Rotary Blade (Flipper) Stop to be air actuated. Typically paired with 
modular plastic chain/belt conveyor. The Inverted Flipper shall also have the 
ability to detect both up and down positions.

9.19.9.6 
Vision Systems are considered a potential stop section within conveyors but are 
not owned by conveyors. It is the responsibility of the awarded contractor to meet 
the following requirements related to Vision System but nothing more: 
1. Delivery conveyor through vision system is to be accumulating 
modular plastic chain straight conveyor for the purpose of more 
precise positioning of the T&W assemblies. 
2. Inverted Rotary Blade (Flipper) to be  used to stop components 
in a rhythmic pattern to meet cycle time requirements. 
3. Conveyor shall locate components such that tolerance   
requirements per application can be met.  
4. Component count shall be shown on  local panel view for buffer 
awareness. 
9.19.10 
GUIDES 
9.19.10.1 
In-line delivery conveyor shall be sized 4” wider than intended component width 
so that side guides can be adjusted. 
9.19.10.2 
Flow rail to be type 4 (Litton P10 or Ford approved equivalent) 1 15/16” dia dual 
skate rollers on 3” centers with offset. The mounting frame shall be adjustable for 
minus 0” to plus 2” between rollers, each side.   
9.19.10.3 
Guide Bars shall be UHMW Polyethylene plastic with beveled edges. Guide bars 
shall be used in all locations that product has potential for unwanted contact, 
including but not limited to T&W UHMW shelving that must be provided for the 
entire length of the operator load zone. 
9.19.11 
CONVEYOR FRAMEWORK 
9.19.11.1 
The supporting structure shall be fabricated steel construction, welded and/or 
bolted assembly, designed to safely support the intended live load (20 lbs. per 
square foot, minimum). 
9.19.11.2 
All structure in contact with any product shall be free of sharp edges. All sharp 
edges must be radiused and all potential scratch surfaces must be covered with 
non-marring, non-scratching covers, to eliminate potential damage. Welds shall 
be ground flat with spatter removed to prevent premature wear and/or damage to 
the conveyor components and/or conveyed product. 
9.19.11.3 
Provide a minimum of 24" walkway maintenance clearance around all new 
equipment, drives, escapements, etc. 
9.19.11.4 
Where conveyor is overhead, all drives and escapements must have 
maintenance access platforms with minimum 30" clearance to equipment. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 320 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.19.11.5 
Provide “chicken ladder” service walkway and handrail access with spring return 
gate at each incline/decline belt conveyor. If incline/decline does not go to floor 
level a short 3 to 4’ ladder at bottom is required. 
9.19.11.6 
Side frames to be C6x10.5 structural steel channel, minimum. 
9.19.11.7 
Spreaders to be 2-1/2" x 2-1/2" x 3/16" angle iron. 
9.19.11.8 
Legs to be 4" channel iron by 5.4# with an adjustable foot with plus or minus 1 ½ 
top of rollers. 
9.19.11.9 
Roller to roller chain guards to be 12-gauge sheet metal. 
9.19.12 
DRIVE MECHANISM 
9.19.12.1 
The CDLR conveyor drive shall consist of a VFD controlled motor. Where hollow 
shaft gearmotors are used, they shall be complete with a standard torque arm 
arrangement as provided by the gearmotor supplier. Torque overload protection 
shall be incorporated utilizing electrical prox/limit switch detection. 
9.19.12.2 
All individual pieces of equipment shall be self-contained – meaning it must have 
its own drive. Parasitically driven sections of conveyor must have a justifiable 
reason for use and be approved by Ford VOME Engineer. 
9.19.12.3 
Drives to be upsized from GVOSS Section 3.3.3.3 requirement to 2.0 service 
factor – AGMA heavy duty belt conveyor. 
9.19.12.4 
All CDLR conveyor types shall be electrical helical bevel gearmotor driven. 
9.19.12.5 
Drives shall be located in the center of the individual conveyor span to distribute 
the chain pull load.3 
9.19.12.6 
All new controls to comply with the latest controls spec: 
• For systems following Next-Gen see  GVOSS Section 8 
• For systems following GVOSS see Section 14 
• Processors and HMIs as required. 
9.19.12.7 
Auto shutoff timers to be incorporated on all conveyors. 
9.19.13 
LUBRICATION 
9.19.13.1 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.25 for lubrication requirements. 
9.19.13.2 
Grease manifold necessary when zerk fitting is not accessible. 
9.19.14 
GROUNDING 
9.19.14.1 
The CDLR conveyor frame shall be grounded/bonded in compliance with 
GVOSS section 8.3.3.12, including standards drawing 70ZF- 20013207. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014

Revision Date: June 8, 2025

9.19.15 
PITS 
9.19.15.1 
All floor mounted conveyors to be designed to mount to existing plant finished 
floor level. Pits are typically not required in a T&W and/or palletized conveyor 
system. 
9.19.16 
REPLACEMENT COMPONENTS 
9.19.16.1 
Replacement components shall conform to this specification. 
9.19.16.2 
The CDLR for future conveyor extensions shall match the existing CDLR 
components. 
9.19.16.3 
The bearings, shafts, and supporting structure shall be identical to the existing 
conveyor. 
9.19.16.4 
The Contractor shall be responsible for obtaining the correct dimensional and 
operational requirements.

9.19.17.3 
The CDLR manufacturer shall supply clear, concise, explicit and pictorial data, 
procedures and processes for assembly of the maintenance replacement parts.

9.22 
MOVING SIDEWALKS 
9.22.1 
General 
9.22.1.1 
Moving Sidewalks – a.k.a. People Movers – are Modular Plastic Belting (MPB) or 
Modular Hybrid Belting (MHB) conveyor systems installed adjacent to or 
integrated into a production conveyance system for the purpose of establishing a 
stand-on work surface to transport production line personnel at a speed that is 
nominally synchronized with that of the work piece on the production conveyor. 
9.22.1.2 
This Section is to be used in conjunction with the following specifications.  This 
document shall supersede any conflicts between this specification and those 
listed below. 
 
1. 
GVOSS Section 3.2 Typical Machine Components 
 
2. 
GVOSS Section 3.3 Motors, Gearmotors and Speed Reducers 
 
3. 
GVOSS Section 9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors 
 
4. 
GVOSS Section 9.5 Conveyor Mechanical Engineering 
 
5. 
GVOSS Section 9.6 Conveyor Controls Engineering 
 
6. 
GVOSS Section 9.25 Lubrication for Conveyors 
 
7. 
GVOSS Section 9.26 Conveyor Support Steel and Platforms 
 
8. 
GVOSS Section 9.27 Conveyor Pits, Trenches and Related Pit Steel 
9.22.1.3 
The Moving Sidewalk MPB width and length shall be determined by the 
application for which it is intended. 
9.22.1.4 
The maximum working capacity of the Moving Sidewalk shall be determined by 
the individual application for which it is intended. 
9.22.1.5 
The Contractor shall provide MSDS sheets for all plastics supplied in the 
construction of the Moving Sidewalk conveyor. 
9.22.1.6 
Approved Suppliers for Modular Plastic Belting are: (1) Uni-Chain, (2) Intralox 
and (3) Rexnord. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 325 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.22.2 
Modular Belt 
9.22.2.1 
The Moving Sidewalk conveyor belting shall be constructed from injection 
molded Nylon for non-corrosive applications and Acetal or Polypropylene for 
corrosive applications. The MPB/MHB Supplier shall be consulted as to proper 
material selection for the specific application. The Contractor shall provide the 
MPB Supplier materials review and approval documentation to the Owner at the 
design review meeting. 
9.22.2.2 
The MPB/MHB shall be assembled using abrasion resistant nylon rods, 
polypropylene, polyester or stainless-steel rods. The Contractor shall provide the 
MPB/MHB Supplier review and approval documentation to the Owner at the 
design review meeting. 
9.22.2.3 
The MPB/MHB shall be "anti-static" by design. i.e., the belts shall utilize material 
which prevents static electricity buildup. The belts shall have a maximum surface 
resistivity 106 ohms per square as measured per ASTM D257. 
9.22.2.4 
The MPB/MHB shall have a "raised rib non-skid" style surface for safety 
purposes. 
9.22.2.5 
The MPB/MHB color shall be black with yellow edges. 
9.22.3 
Modular Belt Carry-Way 
9.22.3.1 
The modular belt Carry-Way shall be constructed from 1/4" (6 mm) thick 
minimum UHMW-PE (Ultra High Molecular Weight Polyethylene) sheeting placed 
under the modular belt as a wear surface. The UHMW-PE wear surface shall 
extend the entire width and for the entire length of the Moving Sidewalk. 
9.22.3.2 
The UHMW-PE wear surface and the conveyor shall be grounded to dissipate 
any generated static electricity. The UHMW-PE material shall be new non-
recycled material. It should have a high molecular weight on the order of 6 
million. The material shall be anti-static with a maximum surface resistivity of 106 
ohms per square as measured per ASTM D257, comparable to the conveyor 
belting. 
9.22.3.3 
The UHMW-PE wear surface sheet shall be fastened at one end only and shall 
be allowed to expand thermally along its full length. Each individual sheet shall 
never come in contact with the previous or following sheets. A gap of sufficient 
size shall run between the sheets, not to exceed 2 inches (50 mm) to allow for 
this thermal expansion. 
9.22.3.4 
The gaps between the UHMW-PE wear sheets shall be at a sufficient angle to 
prevent the modular belt from catching any corners. 
 
1. 
The forward and trailing edges between Individual sheets should be angled 
in a chevron pattern such that the gap does not allow a single chain row or 
pitch to be unsupported as it travels across the gaps. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 326 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
2. 
The typical sheet pattern is a parallelogram shape leading/trailing edge at 
30-45 degrees to direction of modular belt travel. 
 
3. 
The feeding edges of all wear sheets shall have a minimum 2mm x 45-
degree chamfer. 
9.22.3.5 
The UHMW-PE wear surface shall be continuously supported by a structural 
framework. The Carry-Way shall not deflect more than ¼” (6 mm) over a 10’-0 
(3050 mm) span. The supporting structure shall be designed to be below the 
UHMW-PE wear sheets to increase ergonomic function of the sidewalk system. 
9.22.3.6 
The wear surface support material shall be designed sufficient for the operating 
conditions. i.e., stainless steel shall be used in areas such as liquid fill areas. 
9.22.4 
Belt Return-Way 
9.22.4.1 
The Return-Way assembly shall include catenary rollers constructed from steel 
tube with heavy duty sealed bearings on both ends to provide smooth operation. 
9.22.4.2 
The Return-Way rollers shall be a minimum diameter of 3X the belt pitch, or a 
minimum 5-inch (125 mm) diameter, whichever is greatest. Larger diameters are 
acceptable. 
9.22.4.3 
The catenary roller spacing between rollers shall vary to prevent harmonic 
belt/roller engagement. 
9.22.4.4 
The catenary return roller spacing shall be as approved by the modular belt 
Supplier consistent for the height constraints of the particular conveyor system 
application. The Contractor shall provide the modular belt Supplier catenary 
review and approval documentation to the Owner at the design review meeting. 
9.22.4.5 
For Moving Sidewalk conveyors less than 300 feet (91 meters) long, belt length 
expansion and proper belt/drive sprocket engagement shall be controlled utilizing 
catenary sag of the belt on the Return-Way. The amount of catenary sag 
between sets of return rollers shall be between 1 inch (25 mm) and 4 inches (100 
mm). 
9.22.4.6 
For Moving Sidewalk conveyors greater than 300 feet (91 meters) length, a 6-
inch (150 mm) diameter intermediate gravity-weighted take-up roller shall be 
utilized to insure proper drive sprocket engagement. The weighted take-up roller 
shall be designed to generate 20 lb.’s (9 kg) per foot of conveyor belt width back 
tension on the drive. 
9.22.4.7 
The Contractor is responsible to work with the modular belt Supplier to make the 
proper take-up selection. The Contractor shall provide the modular belt Supplier 
take-up review and approval documentation to the Owner at the design review 
meeting. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 327 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.22.5 
Guides 
9.22.5.1 
The modular belt shall include side guides along the Carry-Way to prevent it from 
being forced laterally left or right causing damage to the belt. 
 
1. 
The Carry-Way side guides shall be from UHMW blocks located adjacent to 
the wear sheets on both sides to form a “guide channel” the full length of the 
moving sidewalk conveyor. 
 
2. 
The side guides shall be the thickness of the belting and 4 to 6 inches 
(100mm to 150mm) wide with length to suit the head, tail and intermediate 
track section lengths. 
 
3. 
The side guides blocks shall be slotted to provide side to side adjustment to 
allow proper clearances for thermal expansion of the belt and easy tool cart 
access. 
 
4. 
The side guides shall be beveled at transitions to provide smooth belt 
transfer from section to section. 
9.22.5.2 
The belt shall be edge guided along the Return-Way to prevent it from hitting any 
structure that may damage the belt. 
 
1. 
Guidance of the modular belt shall be accomplished utilizing UHMW or steel 
(1045 or equivalent) guide channels. 
 
2. 
The guide channels shall be installed the full length of the moving sidewalk 
on both sides of the sidewalk. 
 
3. 
Guides shall be 0.25" (6 mm) thick x 1.25" (31 mm) wide, minimum, with 5-
inch (125 mm) gaps between guide sections, maximum. 
 
4. 
Sufficient gaps shall be provided on each side of the modular belt to allow 
for thermal expansion. 
9.22.6 
Structure 
9.22.6.1 
The supporting structure shall be fabricated steel construction, welded and/or 
bolted assembly, designed to safely support the intended live load (20 lb.’s per 
square foot, minimum). 
9.22.6.2 
All bolted connections shall utilize a minimum of two (2) bolts. All slotted 
connections must be blocked or welded after final field adjustments have been 
made. 
9.22.6.3 
All pillow blocks and bearings must be blocked following final alignment.  

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 328 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.22.6.4 
All structure in contact with the MPB shall be free of sharp edges. Welds shall be 
ground flat with spatter removed to prevent premature wear and/or damage to 
the MPB. 
9.22.6.5 
Dead plate covers and/or finger plate covers shall be provided at the head and 
tail ends of the Moving Sidewalk conveyor where the MPB transfers into the floor. 
These covers shall be painted Safety Yellow. 
9.22.6.6 
Guards intended to prevent a pinch point or to prevent material from falling 
through this gap and accumulating under the sidewalk shall be provided on the 
sides of the Moving Sidewalk conveyor. These guards may be constructed from 
either UHMW or steel material and shall be reviewed at the initial design review 
meeting. 
9.22.6.7 
The supporting frame and/or structure should be appropriately grounded as per 
the specifications included herein. 
9.22.7 
Drive Mechanism 
9.22.7.1 
The main (and standby) drive shall consist of a VFD controlled motor 
incorporating a hollow shaft mounted gearmotor complete with integral brake and 
torque arm arrangement. Torque overload protection shall be incorporated 
utilizing electrical prox/limit switch detection. 
9.22.7.2 
The Contractor shall work with the modular belt supplier to determine if a back-
tension pulse dampening gearmotor is required.  
 
1. 
Where a pulse dampening gearmotor is required, the motor shall be located 
on the conveyor tail shaft. 
 
2. 
The gearmotor shall be VFD controlled and connected to the tail shaft using 
a gear or jaw type coupling assembly with minimal backlash. 
 
3. 
The gearmotor shall operate at a slightly slower speed than the main drive 
to maintain constant belt tension at all times. 
9.22.7.3 
The main (and standby) drive, coupling, coupling guard, and driven device shall 
be mounted on a common drive base. 
9.22.8 
Shafts, Sprockets and Rollers 
9.22.8.1 
The Moving Sidewalk conveyor sprockets shall be constructed from stainless 
steel or polymer material suitable for the specific application. 
 
1. 
Two-piece split sprockets shall be used in lieu of solid sprockets. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 329 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
2. 
The largest sprocket possible, consistent with space constraints, shall be 
used to reduce velocity pulsation due to chordal action. In no case shall a 
sprocket with fewer than 12 teeth be used. 
 
3. 
When multiple driving sprockets are used, only the center sprockets on both 
the head and tail ends are to be fixed in place. All other sprockets shall 
"float" on the shaft for self-aligning purposes. 
 
4. 
The sprocket material, quantity and spacing shall be determined by the 
modular belt manufacturer. The Contractor shall provide the modular belt 
Supplier sprocket application review and approval documentation to the 
Owner at the design review meeting. 
9.22.8.2 
The head and tail shafts be a square cross section – either 1 ½ inch (40 mm), 2 
½ inches (62 mm) or 3 ½ inch (88 mm) diameter, as required to control shaft 
deflection to acceptable limits – and manufactured from 1018 carbon steel or 
303/304 stainless steel hardened to 38 to 42 RC. 
9.22.8.3 
The first roller adjacent to the drive sprocket – a.k.a. the snub roller – shall be 
located in such a manner to ensure 180 degrees (min.) to 210 degrees (max.) 
drive sprocket engagement. 
9.22.8.4 
All head and tail shaft bearings shall be fitted with grease fittings. 
9.22.9 
Bearings 
9.22.9.1 
The wheel bushing shall be type 440C stainless steel, hardened to 55 to 60 
Rockwell C and the outside diameter finished to 16 RMS or better. 
9.22.9.2 
Material certification papers for the 304 stainless steel shall be submitted to the 
Owner prior to processing. 
9.22.10 
Catch Tank (Cleanout) 
9.22.10.1 
A catch tank is to be fabricated and installed under the head end of the sidewalk 
to collect any fasteners, materials, or other objects that fall onto the sidewalk. 
9.22.10.2 
The catch tank is to be easily removed from its working position so it can be 
cleaned out by plant personnel. 
9.22.11 
Lubrication 
9.22.11.1 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.25 for lubrication requirements. 
9.22.12 
Grounding 
An internal grounding device such as a grounding brush shall be utilized to insure 
the elimination of static electricity discharge from the moving belt. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 330 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.22.13 
Pits 
9.22.13.1 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.27 for applicable conveyor pit specifications. 
9.22.13.2 
Pits are required only if the top surface of the moving sidewalk is lower than 1'-7" 
(483 mm) above floor level.  
9.22.13.3 
The standard Moving Sidewalk conveyor pit shall be 1'- 7" (483 mm) below the 
top surface of the walkway for belt return clearance purposes. Shallow pits may 
be used in special applications. 
9.22.14 
Multi-Level Sidewalks 
9.22.14.1 
Height variations between levels on the sidewalk are to be accomplished using a 
sufficient number of "hold down tabs" to ensure the MPB does not create a gap 
between itself and the UHMW wear sheets. 
9.22.14.2 
The strength of "hold down tabs" is to be determined per application and the 
design data to be reviewed and approved by the Owner at the design review 
meeting. 
9.22.15 
Replacement Components 
9.22.15.1 
Replacement sidewalk components shall conform to this specification. 
9.22.15.2 
The modular belt for existing Moving Sidewalk conveyor extensions shall match 
the existing modular belt. 
9.22.15.3 
The bearings, shafts, UHMW and supporting structure shall be identical to the 
existing moving sidewalk. 
9.22.15.4 
The Contractor shall be responsible for obtaining the correct dimensional and 
operational requirements. 
9.22.16 
Field Installation Tooling 
9.22.16.1 
The modular belt manufacturer shall supply with each order to the INSTALLER, 
all necessary and required tools, such as pullers, mandrels, locators, fixtures, 
grease gun, and any other required tools necessary to assure total reliability and 
warranty considerations. 
9.22.16.2 
All special tools shall be accounted for and delivered to the Plant Engineering 
Manager of the affected plant as Ford Motor Company property for maintenance 
and service purposes. 
9.22.16.3 
The modular belt manufacturer shall supply clear, concise, explicit and pictorial 
data, procedures and processes for assembly of the modular belt. 
9.22.16.4 
Impact placement of precision modular belt component parts is strictly forbidden. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 331 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.23 
MODULAR ACCUMULATION BELT CONVEYORS 
9.23.1 
General 
9.23.1.1 
Modular Accumulation Belt Conveyors – AKA Plastic Chain Conveyors – are 
Modular Accumulation Belting (MAB) conveyor systems that convey and 
accumulate components. Typical examples are uses in tire conveyors and 
palletized seat conveyor systems. 
9.23.1.2 
This Section is to be used in conjunction with the following specifications. This 
document shall supersede any conflicts between this specification and those 
listed below. 
1. 
GVOSS Section 3.2 Typical Machine Components 
2. 
GVOSS Section 3.3 Motors, Gearmotors and Speed Reducers 
3. 
GVOSS Section 9.4 Safety Requirements for Conveyors 
4. 
GVOSS Section 9.5 Conveyor Mechanical Engineering 
5. 
GVOSS Section 9.6 Conveyor Controls Engineering 
6. 
GVOSS Section 9.25 Lubrication for Conveyors 
7. 
GVOSS Section 9.26 Conveyor Support Steel and Platforms 
8. 
GVOSS Section 9.27 Conveyor Pits, Footings, Trenches and Related Pit 
Steel 
9.23.1.3 
The plastic chain width and length shall be determined by the application for 
which it is intended. 
9.23.1.4 
The maximum working capacity of the MAB shall be determined by the individual 
application for which it is intended. 
9.23.1.5 
The contractor shall provide MSDS sheets for all plastics supplied in the 
construction of the MAB conveyor. 
9.23.1.6 
Approved suppliers for Modular Plastic Belting are: (1) Uni-Chain, (2) Intralox and 
(3) Rexnord. 
9.23.2 
Modular Accumulation Belt 
9.23.2.1 
The Modular Accumulation conveyor belting shall be constructed from injection 
molded Nylon for non-corrosive applications and Acetal or Polypropylene for 
corrosive applications. The MAB Supplier shall be consulted as to proper 
material selection for the specific application. The Contractor shall provide the 
MAB Supplier materials review and approval documentation to the Owner at the 
design review meeting. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 332 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.23.2.2 
The MAB shall be assembled using abrasion resistant nylon rods, polypropylene, 
polyester or stainless-steel rods. The Contractor shall provide the MAB Supplier 
review and approval documentation to the Owner at the design review meeting. 
9.23.2.3 
The MAB shall be "anti-static" by design. i.e., the belts shall utilize material which 
prevents static electricity buildup. The belts shall have a maximum surface 
resistivity 106 ohms per square as measured per ASTM D257. 
9.23.3 
Modular Belt Carry-Way 
9.23.3.1 
Carry-way shall be constructed of parallel array similar to Intralox flat finger-joint 
UHMW 
wear-strips, 
installed 
to 
manufacturer’s 
recommendations 
to 
accommodate thermal expansion. 
9.23.3.2 
The UHMW-PE wear surface and the conveyor shall be grounded to dissipate 
any generated static electricity. The UHMW-PE material shall be new non-
recycled material. It should have a high molecular weight on the order of 6 
million. The material shall be anti-static with a maximum surface resistivity of 106 
ohms per square as measured per ASTM D257, comparable to the conveyor 
belting. 
9.23.3.3 
The UHMW-PE wear surface sheet shall be fastened at one end only and shall 
be allowed to expand thermally along its full length. 
9.23.3.4 
The UHMW-PE wear surface shall be continuously supported by a framework. 
The Carry-Way shall not deflect more than 1/4" (6 mm) over a 10’-0 (3050 mm) 
span.  
9.23.3.5 
The wear surface support material shall be designed sufficient for the operating 
conditions. i.e., stainless steel shall be used in such as liquid fill areas. 
9.23.4 
Belt Return-Way 
9.23.4.1 
The Return-Way assembly shall include catenary rollers constructed from steel 
tube with heavy duty sealed bearings on both ends to provide smooth operation. 
9.23.4.2 
The Return-Way rollers shall be a minimum diameter of 3X the belt pitch, or a 
minimum 5-inch (125 mm) diameter, whichever is greatest. Larger diameters are 
acceptable. 
9.23.4.3 
The catenary roller spacing between rollers shall vary to prevent harmonic 
belt/roller engagement. 
9.23.4.4 
The catenary return roller spacing shall be as approved by the modular belt 
supplier consistent for the height constraints of the particular conveyor system 
application. The contractor shall provide the modular belt Supplier catenary 
review and approval documentation to the Owner at the design review meeting.

Revision Date: June 8, 2025

EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.23.4.5 
For modular accumulation conveyors less than 300 feet (91 meters) long, belt 
length expansion and proper belt/drive sprocket engagement shall be controlled 
utilizing catenary sag of the belt on the Return-Way. The amount of catenary sag 
between sets of return rollers shall be between 1 inch (25 mm) and 4 inches (100 
mm). 
9.23.4.6 
For modular accumulation conveyors greater than 300 feet (91 meters) length, a 
6-inch (150 mm) diameter intermediate gravity weighted take-up roller shall be 
utilized to insure proper drive sprocket engagement. The weighted take-up roller 
shall be designed to generate 20 lbs. (9 kg) per foot of conveyor belt width back 
tension on the drive. 
9.23.4.7 
The contractor is responsible to work with the modular belt supplier to make the 
proper take-up selection. The contractor shall provide the modular belt Supplier 
take-up review and approval documentation to the Owner at the design review 
meeting. 
9.23.4.8 
The catenary take-up shall include viewports in the frame and a visual factory 
label for preventive maintenance purposes. i.e., a red/yellow/green marker to 
identify acceptable or non-acceptable ranges of chain sag and schedule 
maintenance activity. The location of the catenary shall be based on the 
application. i.e., head end, tail end or intermediate location. 
9.23.5 
Guides 
9.23.5.1 
The modular belt shall include side guides along the Carry-Way to prevent it from 
being forced laterally left or right causing damage to the belt. 
1. 
The Carry-Way side guides shall be from UHMW blocks located adjacent to 
the wear strips on both sides to form a “guide channel”. 
2. 
The side guides shall be the thickness of the belting with length to suit the 
head, tail and intermediate track section lengths. 
3. 
The side guides shall be beveled at transitions to provide smooth belt 
transfer from section to section. 
9.23.5.2 
The belt shall be edge guided along the Return-Way to prevent it from hitting any 
structure that may damage the belt. 
1. 
Guidance of the modular belt shall be accomplished utilizing UHMW or steel 
(1045 or equivalent) guide channels. 
9.23.6 
Structure 
9.23.6.1 
The supporting structure shall be fabricated steel construction, welded and/or 
bolted assembly, designed to safely support the intended live load (20 lbs. per 
square foot, minimum). 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 334 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.23.6.2 
All bolted connections shall utilize a minimum of two (2) bolts. All slotted 
connections must be blocked or welded after final field adjustments have been 
made. 
9.23.6.3 
All pillow blocks and bearings must be blocked following final alignment.  
9.23.6.4 
All structure in contact with the MAB shall be free of sharp edges. Welds shall be 
ground flat with spatter removed to prevent premature wear and/or damage to 
the MAB. 
9.23.6.5 
The supporting frame and/or structure should be appropriately grounded as per 
the specifications included herein. 
9.23.6.6 
Provide a minimum of 24" walkway clearance around all new equipment, drives, 
escapements, etc. 
9.23.6.7 
Where conveyor is overhead, all drives and escapements must have 
maintenance access platform with minimum 30" clearance to equipment. 
9.23.7 
Drive Mechanism 
9.23.7.1 
The main (and stand-by) drive shall consist of a VFD controlled motor 
incorporating a hallow shaft mounted gearmotor complete with integral brake and 
torque arm arrangement. Torque overload protection shall be incorporated 
utilizing electrical prox/limit switch detection. 
9.23.7.2 
All new controls to comply with the latest controls spec: 
• For systems following Next-Gen see GVOSS Section 8. 
• For systems following GVOSS see GVOSS Section 14. 
• Processors and HMIs as required. 
9.23.7.3 
Auto shutoff timers to be incorporated on all conveyors. 
9.23.8 
Shafts, Sprockets and Rollers 
9.23.8.1 
The MAB conveyor sprockets shall be constructed from stainless steel or 
polymer material suitable for the specific application. 
1. 
The largest sprocket possible, consistent with space constraints, shall be 
used to reduce velocity pulsation due to chordal action. In no case shall a 
sprocket with fewer than 12 teeth be used. 
2. 
When multiple driving sprockets are used, only the center sprockets on 
both the head and tail ends are to be fixed in place. All other sprockets shall 
"float" on the shaft for self-aligning purposes. 
3. 
The sprocket material, quantity and spacing shall be determined by the 
modular belt manufacturer. The Contractor shall provide the modular belt 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 335 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
Supplier sprocket application review and approval documentation to the Owner at 
the design review meeting. 
9.23.8.2 
The head and tail shafts be a square cross section – either 1-1/2-inch (40 mm), 
2-1/2 inches (62 mm) or 3-1/2-inch (88 mm) diameter, as required to control shaft 
deflection to acceptable limits – and manufactured from 1045 carbon steel or 
303/304 stainless steel hardened to 38 to 42 RC. 
9.23.8.3 
The first roller adjacent to the drive sprocket – a.k.a. the snub roller – shall be 
located in such a manner to ensure 180 degrees (min.) to 210 degrees (max.) 
drive sprocket engagement. 
9.23.8.4 
All head and tail shaft bearings shall be fitted with grease fittings. 
9.23.9 
Lubrication 
9.23.9.1 
Refer to GVOSS Section 9.25 for lubrication requirements. 
9.23.10 
Grounding 
An internal grounding device such as a grounding brush shall be utilized to insure 
the elimination of static electricity discharge from the modular belt. 
9.23.11 
Pits 
9.23.11.1 
All floor mounted conveyors to be designed to mount to existing plant finished 
floor level.  No pits to be required. 
9.23.12 
Replacement Components 
9.23.12.1 
Replacement components shall conform to this specification. 
9.23.12.2 
The modular belt for future conveyor extensions shall match the existing modular 
belt. 
9.23.12.3 
The bearings, shafts, UHMW and supporting structure shall be identical to the 
existing conveyor. 
9.23.12.4 
The Contractor shall be responsible for obtaining the correct dimensional and 
operational requirements. 
9.23.13

9.23.13.1 
The modular belt manufacturer shall supply with each order to the INSTALLER, 
all necessary and required tools, such as pullers, mandrels, locators, fixtures, 
grease gun, and any other required tools necessary to assure total reliability and 
warranty considerations. 
9.23.13.2 
All special tools shall be accounted for and delivered to the Plant Engineering 
Manager of the affected plant as Ford Motor Company property for maintenance 
and service purposes.
