9.26.11 
Height Restriction Warning Bars 
9.26.11.1 
Where conveyors and related equipment are installed 16’-0 (4880 mm) or less 
over aisles, a height restriction warning bar – a.k.a. Bong Bar – shall be installed 
on both sides for each approaching direction. 
9.26.11.2 
The warning bar shall be painted Safety Yellow. 
9.26.11.3 
The warning bars shall be installed horizontally 10’-0 (3048 mm), min., from the 
overhead obstruction at each end of the obstruction. e.g., overhead falling parts 
guards, platforms, mezzanines, process control boards, etc. 
9.26.11.4 
The warning bar shall be located vertically 6 inches (150 mm) lower than the 
lowest point of the overhead obstruction. 
9.26.11.5 
The warning bar shall be constructed from 3-inch diameter Schedule 40 steel 
pipe. The minimum length of the bar shall be 2’-0 (600 mm) greater than the aisle 
width. i.e., the bar is to extend 1’-0 (300 mm) extension on both sides of the aisle. 
9.26.11.6 
The warning bar shall be suspended from the overhead support by two (2) 3/8” 
Herc-Alloy 800 chains, or Owner approved equivalent, one (1) chain each end. 
9.26.11.7 
The primary support chain shall connect to the overhead support by looping 
around the support, incorporating a standard Herc-Alloy Hammerlok and Oblong 
Master Link to complete the loop. The chain shall be covered with a standard 
convoluted sleeving material where it loops around the overhead support. 
9.26.11.8 
The primary support chain shall connect to the warning bar by incorporating a 
standard Herc-Alloy Hammerlok fastened to a shoulder type eyebolt. The eyebolt 
shall use an Anco locknut to fasten to the warning bar. 
9.26.11.9 
Secondary supports shall be provided in compliance with FAS08-145 Secondary 
Restraints for Overhead Equipment. The secondary restraints shall be 
constructed from two (2) 1/4” steel wire ropes, one (1) each end. 
9.26.11.10 
The secondary supports shall connect to the overhead support by looping around 
the support, incorporating standard G-450 Crosby Wire Rope clips to complete 
the loop. The wire rope shall be covered with a standard convoluted sleeving 
material where it loops around the overhead support. 
9.26.11.11 
The secondary support shall connect to the warning bar by incorporating G-450 
Crosby Wire Rope Clips and a Standard Wire Rope Thimble assembled to a 
shoulder type eyebolt. The eyebolt shall use an Anco locknut to fasten to the 
warning bar. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 371 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.27 
CONVEYOR PITS, TRENCHES AND RELATED PIT STEEL 
9.27.1 
General 
9.27.1.1 
The Contractor shall be responsible for the design, detail, procurement, 
fabrication and installation of all conveyor related concrete pits, footings, 
trenches and pit steel for the units identified in the project bid specification. This 
work shall include all structural reinforced concrete for pit floor, walls and 
foundations, including forming, reinforcing, finishing, curing, protecting and 
jointing, etc. 
9.27.1.2 
All conveyor related concrete layouts and pit steel design drawings and 
calculations shall be reviewed and approved by a registered professional 
structural engineer with knowledge of automotive operation prior to submitting for 
approval by the Owner. 
9.27.1.3 
The cover plate, support steel and curb angle at the aisle-way crossings shall be 
designed by a registered professional structural engineer and submitted to the 
Owner for approval. Refer to FCS Section 01700 for additional design criteria. 
9.27.1.4 
The Contractor shall provide concrete pit, footing and miscellaneous pit steel 
layouts and details for approval prior to fabrication or installation of these items. 
9.27.1.5 
The existing concrete support system, including subgrade, shall be analyzed for 
proper stress, soil bearing and load capacity with all existing and additional loads 
applied. The final concrete and reinforcing system design shall be in compliance 
with the FCS Section 03 30 00 Cast-in-Place Concrete. 
9.27.1.6 
All drive and take-up pits subject to accumulation of water or other liquids shall 
be provided with a sump pit located below the access door. A sump pump will be 
required under certain conditions when indicated in the project bid documents. 
The minimum sump pit size shall be 2'-0" x 2'-0" x 1'-0" deep (610 mm x 610 mm 
x 305 mm deep) with a metal grated cover. 
9.27.1.7 
All large pits with cover plating work pits, Inverted P&F and Flat-Top drive/take-
up pits, Side-/Stroke-Arm High-Lift table pits, etc. shall be provided with LED 
lighting fixtures per FCS Section 26 51 00, 110v service outlet(s), compressed 
air, fire protection and ventilation for confined space areas, as required. These 
pits shall include Unistrut embedded in the walls for mounting services at 10'-0" 
(3048 mm) on center. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 372 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.27.1.8 
A service trench, 12" wide x 12" deep (305 mm wide x 305 mm deep) with cover 
plate flush with the floor, shall be provided from the nearest building column to 
the pit to provide passage for utilities. The Contractor shall remove the existing 
column concrete within the isolation joint to the web of the column. The removal 
of the “diamond” shall be performed utilizing a chipping hammer in such a way as 
to not damage the existing anchor bolts. The utility trench shall be covered with 
cover plate to support the intended floor loads in compliance with FCS 01700 
Facility Design Criteria section 9.0 Design Loads. 
9.27.1.9 
The Contractor shall provide all material and Labor required for all pits, trenches, 
sumps, cleanouts, etc., for Cross-Transfer aisle crossings, Shuttle Cart aisle 
crossings, Skillet pits, IP&F pits, Flay-Top pits, work pits, etc. 
9.27.1.10 
Certain existing plant roads, pavements and floor areas will be made available to 
the Contractor for the shipment of material and equipment, upon approval of the 
Owner. Design capacities of the roads, pavements and floor areas shall not be 
exceeded by any vehicle at any time. Only rubber-tired vehicles will be permitted 
on concrete and bituminous pavements. The Contractor shall repair, at no 
additional cost to the Owner, any damages to existing plant roads, pavements 
and floor areas due to abuse or overloading by the Contractor. 
9.27.2 
Excavation 
9.27.2.1 
All excavation procedures shall conform to applicable federal, state and local 
codes and requirements. 
9.27.2.2 
All excavation work required for conveyor related pits, footings, and trenches etc. 
shall be completed in compliance with the requirements specified in FCS Section 
31 00 00 Earthwork section 3.3 Excavation. 
9.27.2.3 
All excavations shall be kept free from water at all times, including excavations 
having bottom elevations below the water table, up to such time that concrete 
has attained its initial set. The Contractor shall provide pumps, well points, etc., 
as necessary to remove the water. Pumping water into the plant sanitary or storm 
sewer system shall not be permitted. 
9.27.2.4 
The excavation shall be of sufficient size to permit construction and removal of 
the forms and to inspect the work and as soil conditions permit. 
9.27.2.5 
The excavations shall be made in such a way that the adjacent areas of the plant 
building shall be maintained for trucking activities. The Contractor shall furnish 
and install all sheeting, piling, shoring, etc., as required to hold such areas as 
needed to complete the excavation work. 
9.27.3 
Concrete 
9.27.3.1 
For concrete design, placing, finishing, flatness/levelness tolerances, curing, 
protection and patching, the requirements identified in FCS 03 30 00 Cast-in-
Place Concrete shall apply. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 373 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.27.3.2 
The reinforced concrete steel reinforcing design and installation shall be 
completed in compliance with the requirements identified in FCS 03 30 00 Cast-
in-Place Concrete. 
9.27.4 
Pit Steel 
9.27.4.1 
All pit steel including curb angles and cover plates shall be considered part of the 
new pit. 
9.27.4.2 
All floor plates over pits must be installed flush with floor level. 
9.27.4.3 
All cover plate over new pits, or new cover plate over old pits, containing 
operating equipment shall be furnished with a minimum of two (2) lift handles, or 
holes, which will set flush with the top of the plate when released. 
9.27.4.4 
One hinged aluminum hatch cover plate approximately 2'-0" x 2'-0" (610mm x 
610mm) shall be provided over the pit access ladder. 
9.27.4.5 
The maximum weight of any floor plate section shall not exceed 250 pounds 
(114kg). 
9.27.4.6 
Pit cover plates and structural supports for pit cover plates in areas where fork 
trucks are not present shall be designed for a 2500 pound (1140kg) minimum 
concentrated load. 
9.27.4.7 
In areas of aisle-way crossing and areas where fork trucks can operate, the 
cover plate and steel beams supporting he cover plate shall be designed and 
installed to comprehend the floor loads identified in FCS 01700 Facility Design 
Criteria section 9.0 Design Loads. 
9.27.4.8 
The cover plate splices shall always be located on a structural support member. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 374 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.28 
REQUIREMENTS FOR REUSED CONVEYORS AGV/AGCs 
9.28.1 
General 
9.28.1.1 
Where identified in the project specification, conveyors AGV/AGCs and related 
equipment shall be reused in compliance with the requirements identified within 
this section and with all applicable Ford Motor Company health and safety 
standards and GVOSS specifications.  
9.28.1.2 
All refurbished/relocated/remanufactured (R3) conveyors AGV/AGCs and related 
equipment shall be upgraded to meet the latest safety specifications. (Ref. 
GVOSS 2.10.1.11) 
9.28.1.3 
All equipment identified to be reworked and/or relocated shall be cleaned and 
stripped of all grease, sludge, rust and other debris and shall be re-painted to 
provide a “like new” appearance. 
9.28.1.4 
All reused components such as motors, reducers, brakes, bearings, chains, 
sprockets, cylinders, stops, shot pins etc., shall be tested for proper functionality. 
The cost of repairs and/or replacement parts shall be submitted to the VO 
Implementation Supervisor, or designated representative, using the standard 
FAR process prior to performing any rework or purchasing replacement parts. 
9.28.1.5 
All R3 conveyors and related equipment shall be lubricated per the manufacturers 
instruction whether it is provided as new, relocated or refurbished. i.e., bearings, 
chain, motors, gearboxes, etc. 
9.28.1.6 
Where the existing Power Roll conveyor, Two-Strand head and tail sections, Lift 
Tables, Turntables, etc. for R3 conveyors do not have a central lube system 
installed, central lube blocks, fittings and all necessary tubing, etc. shall be 
installed in compliance with the Ford conveyor standards. Where lube blocks 
exist, the Contractor shall purge all lines and verify proper operation. 
9.28.1.7 
All reused chain shall be lubricated before operating. This includes rivet less 
OHM/P&F conveyor chain, power transmission chains, drop lifter lift chains, and 
CC5 conveyor chains, etc. 
9.28.1.8 
All reused trolley conveyor carrier trolley wheels and side guide wheels, 
OHM/P&F chain trolley wheels, drop lifter cam followers, and all reused conveyor 
pillow-block and flange bearings, etc. shall be manually greased and wiped clean 
of excess grease before operating the conveyor. 
9.28.1.9 
All existing labeling shall be removed from the equipment if no longer applicable, 
and new labeling shall be affixed to the conveyor as required per the current 
standard specification release level. 
9.28.1.10 
Where removal and/or relocation of conveyors AGV/AGCs and related 
equipment from existing facilities are included in the scope of work noted on the 
project bid specifications, the Tier1/AGV/AGC/Contractor shall provide all labor, 
material, tools and equipment required for the complete removal, refurbishment, 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 375 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
storing, scrapping and dismantling of the complete conveyor and related facilities 
specified. 
9.28.1.11 
The Tier1/AGV/AGC/Contractor shall locate, identify, tag and be responsible for 
the removal, refurbishment and relocation of all conveyors AGV/AGCs and 
related components, in entirety, as required to provide the project bid 
specification intent. The Tier1/AGV/AGC/Contractor shall be responsible to 
transfer 
the 
removed/relocated 
components 
to 
the 
designated 
storage/staging/loading area as described in the project bid specification. 
9.28.1.12 
All conveyors and related equipment to be removed and relocated to another 
location shall be tagged (using the Reused Conveyor Equipment Instruction Tag 
form) to identify the originating plant, originating plant location, conveyor type and 
OEM, conveyor and/or component description, proposed plant and proposed 
plant location (if known). Each piece of equipment shall have an individual tag 
and, where applicable, shall be identified as a sub-component of an overall 
conveyor system. The tag shall also have an individual identification number to 
be used for tracking as well as the name and phone number of the individual that 
tagged the item. Tags shall be protected by plastic sleeves and affixed, or "tie 
wrapped", to the equipment for shipping. 
9.28.1.13 
The Contractor shall provide a maintenance manual for all relocated and/or 
refurbished conveyors in compliance with the applicable maintenance 
specifications for new equipment of like type. 
9.28.2 
Intent 
9.28.2.1 
The intention of this specification is to identify the minimum requirements 
necessary for removal and installation of R3 conveyor systems, conveyor 
components and related equipment. 
9.28.2.2 
This section is intended to define the minimal work that must be completed to 
utilize R3 conveyors and related equipment, either in a new location in the 
current facility or relocated to a new plant facility. This document does not apply 
to existing equipment to be reused in the existing location. 
9.28.2.3 
This document is not intended to supersede the GVOSS but is intended to 
complement those specifications. The Contractor is required to identify any 
discrepancies between this section, the applicable GVOSS standard conveyor 
sections and the project bid specification during the proposal development phase 
of the project. 
9.28.2.4 
GVOSS Specification Section 9.29 identifies the minimal requirements and 
methods of inspection, measurement and/or determination of the condition of 
conveyors and equipment relative to the applicability to reuse and/or 
remanufacture and shall be included as part of this specification. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 376 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.28.2.5 
This specification is not intended to replace the detailed scope of work specific to 
the individual project and/or bid documents. The responsible Ford Project 
Engineer must review the equipment that is proposed to be relocated and/or 
refurbished and, using this standard as a guide, determine the applicability for 
reuse and state such in the contract bid documents. i.e., the extent of repairs or 
upgrades/modifications required to bring the equipment up to usable condition. 
9.28.2.6 
After award of contract, the Contractor shall be required to verify the functionality 
of the various R3 conveyors and conveyor components as identified within this 
document and the project specifications. Where components are not identified for 
repair in the contract documents but are discovered to be non-functional or 
appear to be worn beyond the point of continued use, the Contractor shall be 
required to identify such components to the Owner and provide the cost for repair 
or replacement using the standard FAR process or as identified in the project 
specification hard points for Owner consideration. 
9.28.3 
Definitions 
9.28.3.1 
Existing conveyors and related equipment may be reused and/or refurbished in 
the current plant at the existing location, in the existing facility at a new location, 
or they may be reused and/or refurbished and transferred to another plant 
location to use as spare parts, for plant projects and new model programs. 
Therefore, for the purpose of this section, the following definitions shall apply: 
 
1. 
A Reused Conveyor is defined as a conveyor or related component that is 
to be reused in the existing location in the current state. No work shall be 
required to be performed on these type conveyors by the Contractor, unless 
specified in the detailed equipment descriptions or other areas of the 
purchase order documents. 
 
2. 
A Refurbished Conveyor is defined as a conveyor or related component that 
shall have minor rework only and is to be reused in the existing location. 
 
3. 
A Relocated Conveyor is defined as a conveyor or related component that is 
to be relocated within the existing facility or to a different assembly plant 
location. These type conveyors may be relocated in the existing condition or 
may require some refurbishment. 
 
4. 
A Remanufactured Conveyor is defined as a conveyor that is subject to 
major rework – i.e., stripped to the frame and reassembled using all new 
purchased components – and is to be relocated within the existing facility or 
to a different assembly plant location. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 377 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.28.4 
Equipment Warranty 
9.28.4.1 
Although previous warranties may apply, reused conveyors and conveyor 
components (that have not been refurbished) shall not be subjected to additional 
warranty claims when used in the current-state and existing location as part of a 
modified system layout. 
9.28.4.2 
For R3 conveyors and related equipment, the Ford Motor Company Standard 
Terms and Conditions shall apply only to the work provided as part of the specific 
project. i.e., refurbished components, replacement controls devices, control logic, 
installation quality, etc. 
9.28.5 
Structural Systems 
9.28.5.1 
Structural Supports and Related Systems: 
 
1. 
Header steel shall be new unless reused in the existing location, provided 
the final design is in compliance with applicable GVOSS specifications. 
 
2. 
Reuse and/or relocation of existing truss clamps shall be strictly prohibited. 
All truss clamps shall be new for all R3 conveyors and related equipment. 
 
3. 
Reuse of structural steel fasteners (F3125/F3125M bolts and washers, nuts, 
etc.) shall be strictly prohibited. All fasteners for structural support and 
related applications shall be new as specified in applicable GVOSS 
specifications. 
9.28.5.2 
Platforms and Related Systems: 
 
1. 
Existing drive, take-up and repair spur platforms shall be reused where 
specified in the project bid specification. All R3 conveyor drive, take-up and 
repair spur platforms shall be repaired or upgraded to comply with 
applicable GVOSS specifications. 
 
2. 
Existing service walks and access ladders shall be reused where specified 
in the project bid specification. All R3 service walks and access ladders shall 
be repaired or upgraded to be in compliance with applicable GVOSS 
specifications. 
9.28.5.3 
Drip Pans and Basket Guard: 
 
1. 
Existing drip pans and basket guards shall be reused where specified in the 
project specification. All R3 drip pans and basket guards shall be repaired or 
upgraded to comply with the applicable GVOSS specification and Ford 
Global Manufacturing Standard CGX1. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 378 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.28.6 
Mechanical Systems 
9.28.6.1 
All machinery guards included in the current release level of Branch Drawings 
shall be included in the R3 conveyors.  
9.28.6.2 
Gravity energy control devices must be upgraded to the current standard. 
9.28.6.3 
Motors: 
 
1. 
Where reuse of existing motors is specified for refurbished and relocated 
conveyors, the motor frame shall be brushed and/or wiped clean and the air 
passages shall be blown and/or vacuumed to ensure adequate air flow to 
cool the motor during operation.  
 
2. 
All remanufactured conveyors shall be provided with new motors for the 
duty cycle identified in the project specification and in compliance with the 
applicable Conveyor Specifications. 
9.28.6.4 
Brakes: 
 
1. 
Where reuse of existing brakes is specified for refurbished and relocated 
conveyors, the brake shall be disassembled so that all friction material dust 
can be removed from the interior surfaces. The dust shall be removed by 
brushing and/or vacuuming in compliance with the OEM service and 
maintenance instructions. 
 
2. 
Where reuse of existing brakes is specified for refurbished and relocated 
conveyors, the brake shall be disassembled so that the friction surfaces can 
be inspected for excessive wear and proper operating parameters. e.g., 
rotor thickness and air gap. Where excessive wear or other non-compliance 
items are noted, the offending parts shall be replaced, and parameters shall 
be adjusted to be in compliance with the OEM service and maintenance 
instructions. Only the original OEM parts shall be used for repairs and 
replacements. 
 
3. 
All remanufactured conveyors shall be provided with new brakes for the 
duty cycle identified in the project specification and in compliance with the 
applicable Conveyor Specifications. 
9.28.6.5 
Gearboxes: 
 
1. 
All gearboxes to be reused on remanufactured conveyor systems for new 
model or plant projects, or to be reused as spare parts at the current or 
other Ford Motor Company plant locations, shall be remanufactured by an 
approved Supplier in compliance with this specification. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 379 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
2. 
Minimally, where reuse of existing gearboxes is specified for refurbished 
and relocated conveyors, the gearbox shall be drained of oil and inspected 
for signs of excessive gear wear. e.g., metal shavings in the base pan. The 
exterior casting shall be brushed and/or wiped clean and the gearbox filled 
with a lubricant compatible with that currently used in the plant and in 
compliance with the OEM specifications. 
 
3. 
The Ford Project Engineer shall be notified where visible leaks and/or 
excessive wear is observed following inspection, the offending gearbox shall 
be tagged "Do Not Use; Project Name; Plant Name; Conveyor Company 
Name; Inspector Name; Date" and removed from the conveyor area for 
disposition as directed by the Ford Project Engineer. 
 
4. 
All gearboxes specified for remanufacture shall include, as a minimum, the 
refurbishment process requirements outlined in Gearbox Remanufacture 
Requirements Check Sheet. 
 
5. 
Each remanufactured gearbox casting shall be stamped with a separate 
traceable identification number which shall minimally contain: 
A. 
The name of the company performing the refurbishment 
B. 
Month and year work performed, and 
C. 
The job and/or sequence number. 
 
6. 
The remanufactured gearbox Supplier shall create and maintain a database 
history of each refurbished gearbox assembly to include as a minimum: 
A. 
The gearbox identification number 
B. 
A description of the gearbox. e.g., OEM, part no., rated Torque and 
HP, output RPM 
C. 
A description of the work performed 
D. 
The date work was performed 
E. 
The plant and system identification (name, location, department) 
F. 
Purchase Order number 
G. 
Checking/gauging data 
H. 
Applicable R & M data 
9.28.6.6 
Power Transmission Components: 
 
1. 
All roller-chain and sprockets on R3 conveyors shall be visually inspected 
for excessive wear, proper alignment and tension to verify suitability for 
continued service. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 380 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
A. 
All chain and sprockets not identified for replacement in the Contract 
scope of work and found to have excessive wear shall be brought to 
the attention of the Ford Project Engineer using the standard FAR 
process. If approved, the suspect part(s) shall be replaced with new 
components in compliance with the applicable Ford Motor Company 
specifications and applicable industry standards. 
B. 
Any misalignment shall be corrected and verified by the Contractor. 
 
2. 
All power transmission belts and sheaves on R3 conveyors shall be visually 
inspected for excessive wear, proper alignment and tension to verify 
suitability for continued service. 
A. 
All belts and sheaves not identified for replacement in the Contract 
scope of work and found to have excessive wear shall be brought to 
the attention of the Ford Project Engineer using the standard FAR 
process. If approved, the suspect part(s) shall be replaced with new 
components in compliance with the applicable Ford Motor Company 
specifications and applicable industry standards. 
B. 
Any misalignment shall be corrected and verified by the Contractor. 
 
3. 
Shaft couplings on R3 conveyors shall be inspected for wear as defined by 
the OEM's maintenance criteria. 
A. 
Defective part(s) shall be brought to the attention of the Ford Project 
Engineer using the standard FAR process. If approved, the suspect 
part(s) shall be replaced as described in the OEM's inspection and 
maintenance instructions. For example, the backlash between the 
coupling cams and the flexible spider on flexible jaw type couplings 
must be checked with a feeler gage to determine the backlash is within 
the recommended limits. The flexible spider must be changed if the 
backlash is not within the limit of wear as defined in the coupling 
specification. Other coupling types shall be similarly inspected and 
reworked as necessary. 
9.28.6.7 
Bearings: 
 
1. 
Minimally, all precision bearings (ball, roller, tapered, linear slide, etc.) 
installed on conveyors identified for refurbishment and/or relocation shall be 
purged and re-lubed with grease compatible with that currently used in the 
plant and in compliance with the OEM specifications. 
 
2. 
All precision bearings assembled on remanufactured conveyors shall be 
new in compliance with the applicable conveyor standards and 
specifications. Replacement bearings shall meet or exceed the original 
bearing specifications. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 381 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
3. 
Where identified in the project specification, special duty bearings – e.g., 
identified for reuse on remanufactured conveyors shall be visually inspected 
for signs of excessive wear. Any offending bearings shall be noted and 
reported to the plant representative and/or the Ford Project Engineer. 
 
4. 
Sleeve bearings – e.g., white metal, sintered metal and bronze bearings – 
identified for reuse on remanufactured conveyors or conveyor components 
shall be replaced unless otherwise approved by the respective Ford Division 
Conveyor Engineering Supervisor. 
9.28.6.8 
Threaded Fasteners: 
 
1. 
Refurbished and relocated conveyors shall reuse existing threaded 
fasteners in their current state only. Threaded fasteners shall be replaced 
with new if disassembly of an item is required due to a refurbishment action. 
 
2. 
Threaded fasteners subjected to pre-stress as part of the assembly and/or 
installation process shall not be reused on refurbished or remanufactured 
conveyors. Previously used fasteners shall be replaced with new. Where the 
original equipment design level is different than the current Ford Branch 
Standard release, the discrepancy shall be brought to the attention of the 
Ford Project Engineer and the fasteners shall be upgraded to the current 
Branch Standard design level unless directed otherwise. 
 
3. 
Anchor bolts shall not be reused on relocated or remanufactured conveyors. 
All anchor bolts shall be new as specified by the applicable conveyor 
specifications and standards. 
9.28.6.9 
Cotter Pins and Snap Rings: 
 
1. 
Refurbished and relocated conveyors shall reuse existing cotter pins and 
snap rings in their current state only. Cotter pins and snap rings shall be 
replaced with new if disassembly of an item that utilizes one of these 
fasteners is required due to a relocation and/or refurbishment action. 
 
2. 
All remanufactured conveyor applications shall be provided with new split 
pins, cotter pins and snap rings. Where the original equipment design level 
is different than the current Ford Branch Standard release, the discrepancy 
shall be brought to the attention of the Ford Project Engineer and the 
fasteners shall be upgraded to the current Branch Standard design level 
unless directed otherwise.  

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 382 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
3. 
Where reuse of split pins, cotter pins and snap rings is allowed, a random 
sample of these pins/rings shall be inspected to ensure the metal is not 
defective due to strain related failure. The minimum sample size shall be six 
of each type. 
9.28.7 
Electrical Power and Control Systems 
9.28.7.1 
Electrical control components shall be reused where identified in the project 
specification. However, all R3 conveyor electrical control components shall be in 
compliance with applicable sections of the GVOSS, or as specified in the detailed 
equipment descriptions and purchase order documents. 
9.28.7.2 
All electrical control components identified for refurbishment and/or relocation to 
a new assembly plant location must be compatible with the control scheme 
currently utilized at the final plant location. 
9.28.7.3 
Control Panels: 
 
1. 
Control panel enclosures shall be reused as identified in the project bid 
specification. Reused enclosures shall be provided with new back plate as 
driven by the project requirements. 
 
2. 
Reused control panels shall include revised/upgraded programming 
terminals and software as required for compliance with the applicable Ford 
specifications and industry software standards. 
 
3. 
All R3 conveyors utilizing existing VFD firmware shall be upgraded to the 
applicable manufacturer specification. 
 
4. 
Reuse of PLC equipment – e.g., I/O cards, racks and processors – shall be 
acceptable only as approved by the respective Ford Division Conveyor 
Engineering Supervisor. 
 
5. 
Where reuse of existing control panels is specified for refurbished and 
relocated conveyors, the Contractor shall add Ethernet capability for 
machine monitoring if this functionality is not present, in compliance with the 
applicable Ford standards. 
9.28.7.4 
Field Devices: 
 
1. 
All E-stops for R3 conveyors shall be replaced and upgraded as necessary 
for compliance with the applicable Ford standards. 
 
2. 
All safety gate switches and safety pin switches for R3 conveyors shall be 
replaced and upgraded as necessary for compliance with the applicable 
Ford standards. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 383 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
3. 
All field control devices – e.g., pancake proxes, barrel proxes, limit switches, 
etc. – for R3 conveyors shall be replaced with new devices as necessary for 
compliance with the applicable Ford standards. Reuse of existing control 
devices is acceptable only as approved by the respective Ford Division 
Conveyor Engineering Supervisor. 
 
4. 
Motor disconnect switches shall be reused for R3 conveyors and related 
systems where motor disconnects are mounted to the existing conveyor as 
part of a complete conveyor module. Otherwise, all motor disconnect 
switches provided for R3 conveyors shall be new as specified in the 
applicable Ford conveyor standards. 
 
5. 
Junction boxes shall be reused for R3 conveyors and related systems 
subject to the following criteria: 
A. 
Junction boxes shall be reused where mounted to the existing 
conveyor as part of a complete conveyor module. 
B. 
Run stop pushbuttons, associated lights and wiring shall be 
reused/relocated as an individual component or module. 
C. 
QAS pushbuttons and associated lights shall be reused/relocated as 
an individual component or module. 
D. 
Auto on/off pushbuttons for valves shall be reused/relocated as an 
individual component or module. 
9.28.7.5 
Field Wiring and Cables: 
 
1. 
The existing field wiring shall be reused where included as part of a 
complete conveyor or component module. e.g., between the disconnect 
switch and the motor, between run/stop and indicator light, etc. 
 
2. 
Reuse of existing wiring for prox switches shall be allowed only where the 
prox switch is mounted as part of a complete conveyor module. Otherwise, 
reuse of field wiring for prox switches, limit switches, safety gates and safety 
pin switches is prohibited unless approved by the respective Ford Division 
Conveyor Engineering Supervisor. 
 
3. 
Where existing wiring is to be reused, the Contractor shall check all 
connections to ensure the fasteners have adequate tightness for proper 
operation and prox cables shall be inspected for damage. The Ford Project 
Engineer shall be notified of any wiring, cables and/or devices determined to 
be faulty. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 384 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.28.8 
Pneumatic Systems 
9.28.8.1 
Unless otherwise noted in the project specification, where reuse of existing 
pneumatic cylinders is specified for refurbished and relocated conveyors, the 
cylinder shall be disassembled so that the inside diameter of the cylinder and rod 
can be cleaned and inspected for evidence of scoring. Any cylinders found to 
have excessive scoring shall be tagged "Do Not Use; Project Name; Plant Name; 
Conveyor Company Name; Inspector Name; Date" and removed from the 
conveyor area for disposition as directed by the Ford Project Engineer. 
9.28.8.2 
The reused cylinder end seals, rod and piston seals shall be replaced with new in 
compliance with the applicable OEM service and maintenance instructions. Only 
the original OEM parts shall be used for repairs and replacements. 
9.28.8.3 
Where the cylinder is cushioned, the existing cushion seals shall be removed and 
replaced with new cushion seals in compliance with the applicable OEM service 
and maintenance instructions. Only the original OEM parts shall be used for 
repairs and replacements. 
9.28.8.4 
The cylinder shall be lubricated and reassembled as identified in the OEM 
service and maintenance instructions. 
9.28.8.5 
Where reuse of valve boards is specified for refurbished and relocated 
conveyors, the particulate filter and absorber elements shall be replaced as a 
minimum. Additionally, the Contractor shall verify operation of the regulator, 
lubricator, remote flow-controls, lock-out, re-classifier, and control valves. The 
Ford Project Engineer shall be notified of any devices determined to be faulty. 
Only the original OEM parts shall be used for repairs. 
9.28.8.6 
All flex hose and piping to/from valve boards shall be new for all R3 conveyors 
and components, unless included as part of a complete conveyor module. e.g., 
Modular Roller Flight Head Frame with integrally mounted valve board piped to 
the end stop. 
9.28.8.7 
All remanufactured conveyors shall be provided with new air, valve boards, and 
remote flow controls, designed for the duty cycle identified in the project 
specification and in compliance with the applicable Ford Conveyor Specifications. 
9.28.9 
Hydraulic Power and Control Systems 
9.28.9.1 
Unless otherwise noted in the project specification, where reuse of existing 
hydraulic cylinders is specified for refurbished and relocated conveyors, the 
cylinder shall be disassembled so that the inside diameter of the cylinder and rod 
can be cleaned and inspected for evidence of scoring. Any cylinders found to 
have excessive scoring shall be tagged "Do Not Use; Project Name; Plant Name; 
Conveyor Company Name; Inspector Name; Date" and removed from the 
conveyor area for disposition as directed by the Ford Project Engineer. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 385 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.28.9.2 
The reused cylinder end seals, rod and piston seals shall be replaced with new in 
compliance with the applicable OEM service and maintenance instructions. Only 
the original OEM parts shall be used for repairs and replacements. 
9.28.9.3 
Where reuse of an existing hydraulic pump is specified for refurbished and 
relocated conveyors, the pump shall be inspected for internal leakage measured 
as a percentage of theoretical flow available (volumetric efficiency). If the 
volumetric efficiency measured is below the level considered acceptable for the 
application, the pump shall be overhauled in compliance with the OEM service 
and maintenance instructions. Only the original OEM parts shall be used for 
repairs and replacements. 
9.28.9.4 
The operation of the hydraulic system control components – reservoir, pressure 
regulators, flow controls and control valves, etc. – shall be verified for 
functionality and to ensure no leakage. The Ford Project Engineer shall be 
notified of any devices determined to be faulty. Only the original OEM parts shall 
be used for repairs. 
9.28.9.5 
All tubing/piping shall be new for all R3 conveyors and components, unless 
included as part of a complete conveyor/component module. 
9.28.9.6 
All remanufactured conveyors shall be provided with new hydraulic actuators and 
flow controls designed for the duty cycle identified in the project specification and 
in compliance with the applicable Ford Conveyor Specifications. 
9.28.10 
Pallet and Skid Floor Conveyors  
9.28.10.1 
Power Roll Bed (PRB) Conveyors: 
 
1. 
Where tube steel type power roll PRBs are to be reused, the tube steel 
power rolls shall be inspected for wear. Power rolls observed to have 
excessive wear (greater than 1/8" degradation of the diameter) shall be 
replaced for all refurbished and relocated conveyors. All remanufactured 
conveyors shall be complete with new power rolls, axles and bearings. 
 
2. 
Where polyurethane type power roll type PRBs are to be reused, the 
polyurethane power rolls shall be inspected for wear. Power rolls observed 
to have excessive wear – e.g., pitting, delaminating or greater than 1/8" 
overall diameter degradation – shall be replaced for all refurbished and 
relocated conveyors. All remanufactured conveyors shall be complete with 
new power rolls, axles and bearings. 
 
3. 
The pallet/skid guides for R3 conveyors shall be inspected for excessive 
wear and/or damage. Excessively worn or damaged skid guides shall be 
replaced as specified in the project specification.  

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 386 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.28.10.2 
Center- and End-Pivot Turntables: 
 
1. 
For the PRB components specification, refer to GVOSS Section 9.28.10.1. 
 
2. 
The rotation drive shall be inspected and refurbished as specified within this 
document. 
 
3. 
The UHMW pivot bearing shall be replaced on all R3 conveyors. 
 
4. 
The drive and idler wheels shall be visually inspected to ensure the integrity 
of the polyurethane. i.e., no surface pitting or "pock marks", no 
delaminating, etc. Offending parts shall be identified and reported to the 
Ford Project Engineer. 
 
5. 
For Kaydon bearing style Turntables to be refurbished or relocated, the 
Contractor shall inspect the pivot bearing for wear. Additionally, the 
Contractor shall re-lubricate the bearing and gear in accordance with the 
OEM recommendations, check the seals to ensure they are intact and 
seated properly, and replace/retighten the mounting bolts. 
 
6. 
The floor mounted shock absorbers shall be inspected for wear and 
replaced as required. 
 
7. 
The gravity actuated over-travel devices shall be inspected for wear and 
replaced as required. 
9.28.10.3 
CC5 Chain Two-Strand, Roller-Flight and Cross-Transfer Conveyors: 
 
1. 
The CC-5A/B chain shall be measured for wear. The CC-5 chain may be 
reused if the overall wear does not exceed 2% elongation, unless approved 
by the Ford Conveyor Engineering Supervisor. In all cases, chains with 3% 
elongation or more shall be prohibited from reuse. Additionally, the mating 
sprockets for any chain with 3% elongation or more shall not be reused. 
 
2. 
When reuse/relocation of the existing drive assembly is required, the 
Contractor shall inspect the motor, gearbox and coupling for continued 
service feasibility as outlined within this specification. 
 
3. 
When modifying an existing drive train, the Contractor shall furnish and 
install components sized to develop the identical service factor, or better, of 
the original components. In all cases, the applied service factor for all drive 
components shall be 1.3 minimum. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 387 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
4. 
Exit and entrance power rolls, including Ultrex sleeves where applicable, 
shall be inspected for wear. Power rolls observed to have excessive wear 
(roller diameter degradation greater than 1/8") shall be replaced for all 
refurbished and relocated conveyors. 
 
5. 
The Sure-Alloy wear bar shall be measured for wear. The wear bar may be 
reused if the overall wear does not exceed 1/16" as measured at the wear 
surface, unless otherwise approved by the respective Ford Division 
Conveyor Engineering Supervisor. 
 
6. 
Two-strand conveyor side guide rollers with less than 2¾" face width shall 
not be reused. All conveyor track for R3 conveyors shall be complete with 
2¾" wide side guide rollers and corresponding checker plate. The track 
guarding and framework shall be modified as required to accept the taller 
roller without creating any pinch points or other operational concerns. 
 
7. 
If not present in the original equipment track sections, debris clean-out holes 
shall be incorporated into the track sections with catch pan, per the latest 
released branch drawing level. 
9.28.10.4 
Buffer-Belt Accumulating Conveyors: 
 
1. 
Where relocation of buffer belt conveyors is approved, all trolley assembly 
mounting fasteners shall be inspected for proper torque. Additionally, the 
trolleys shall be affixed to the belt using a "double nut" arrangement. 
 
2. 
Where relocation of buffer belt conveyors is approved, the buffer belt stop 
assemblies shall be inspected for stress fractures on the stop block 
mounting brackets. Offending parts shall be identified and reported to the 
Ford Project Engineer. 
 
3. 
Where relocation of buffer belt conveyors is approved, the idler rolls shall be 
inspected to ensure free rotation. Offending parts shall be identified and 
reported to the Ford Project Engineer. 
9.28.10.5 
Pallet Cross-Belt Conveyors: 
 
1. 
The drive shall be inspected and refurbished as specified within this 
document. 
 
2. 
The take-up components shall be inspected for wear and replaced as 
required. All take-up springs shall be replaced on all R3 cross-belt 
conveyors.

3. 
The conveyance belts shall be replaced on all R3 cross-belt conveyors in 
compliance with applicable sections of the GVOSS. 
 
4. 
The stainless-steel wear bar shall be replaced with the 20% glass 
impregnated Teflon slider bed in compliance with applicable sections of the 
GVOSS. 
9.28.10.6 
Lift- and Hold Tables: 
 
1. 
For the PRB components specification, refer to GVOSS Section 9.28.10.1. 
 
2. 
All lift and guide cam-followers shall be replaced for all R3 Lift- and Hold-
Tables that show visible wear and/or have been in service greater than 10 
years unless approved by the respective Ford Division Conveyor 
Engineering Supervisor. 
 
3. 
All R3 shall have the gravity lock pins repaired or upgraded to comply with 
the applicable Ford Motor Company standards and applicable sections of 
the GVOSS. 
9.28.10.7 
Transfer Car: 
 
1. 
The drive gearmotor and transmission shall be inspected and refurbished as 
specified within this document. 
 
2. 
The drive and idler wheels shall be visually inspected to ensure the integrity 
of the polyurethane. i.e., no surface pitting or "pock marks", no 
delaminating, etc. Offending parts shall be identified and reported to the 
Ford Project Engineer. 
 
3. 
The floor mounted shock absorbers shall be inspected for wear and 
replaced as required. 
9.28.11 
Vertical Drop Lifters 
9.28.11.1 
The carriage, counterweight frame and associated lift belt connections shall be 
analyzed utilizing FEA to determine feasibility for reuse based on the proposed 
duty cycle. 
9.28.11.2 
The mast structure, carriage, drive and idler drums and shafts for all VDLs that 
have been in service for greater than 10 years and are identified for 
refurbishment and/or relocation shall be subjected to non-destructive testing – 
i.e., ultra-sound, Magnaflux, or dye penetrant, etc. – to verify the structural 
integrity of these components and validate their suitability for reuse. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 389 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.28.11.3 
Mast Structure: 
 
1. 
The mast structure and all drive and idler drum support systems shall be 
analyzed to verify feasibility for reuse based on the proposed duty cycle. 
 
2. 
The floor support system at the proposed location shall be analyzed to verify 
feasibility for reuse based on the proposed duty cycle and the allowable 
floor loading. 
 
3. 
VDL designs that incorporate an integrally mounted wear bar as part of the 
mast structure shall be inspected for wear. Mast mounted wear bars found 
to have excessive wear shall be replaced with new parts in compliance with 
the applicable Ford Conveyor Specification Section. 
9.28.11.4 
Lift Drive, Belt and Drum: 
 
1. 
The feasibility of reusing the existing lifting motor and related components 
shall be analyzed based on a minimum 1.5 design service factor. 
 
2. 
The lifting motor drive circuitry shall utilize a "closed loop" feedback control 
system in compliance with the applicable Ford Conveyor Specification 
Section. 
 
3. 
The drive shaft and drum for all R3 VDLs shall be required to incorporate the 
following details:  
A. 
The main support bearings shall be a double row spherical bearing. 
B. 
The gearbox to drum shaft connection shall utilize a shift able jaw 
coupling. 
C. 
Multiple drive drums shall be selected as "matched" pairs 
D. 
The drum shall mount to the shaft using a "Ringfedder" type bushing. 
 
4. 
The lifting belt for all R3 VDLs shall be new as required by the project 
specifications and in compliance with the applicable Ford Conveyor 
Specification Section. 
9.28.11.5 
Carriage Design and Guidance Components: 
 
1. 
The load bearing, and non-load bearing guide wheels shall be visually 
inspected for wear and, where applicable, to ensure the integrity of the 
camroller or polyurethane riding surface material. i.e., no surface pitting or 
"pock marks", no delaminating, etc. Offending parts shall be identified and 
reported to the Ford Project Engineer.

2. 
Where applicable, all the machined carriage guides shall be re-aligned/re-
installed using new fasteners in compliance with the Ford Conveyor 
Standards. Reuse of existing guide rail mounting fasteners shall not be 
allowed. 
9.28.11.6 
Counterweight Design and Guidance Components: 
 
1. 
The counterweight frame capacity shall be analyzed to verify feasibility for 
reuse based on the proposed duty cycle. 
 
2. 
The load bearing, and non-load bearing guide wheels shall be visually 
inspected for wear and to ensure the integrity of the camroller or 
polyurethane riding surface material. i.e., no surface pitting or "pock marks", 
no delaminating, etc. Offending parts shall be identified and reported to the 
Ford Project Engineer.  
9.28.11.7 
Gravity Control Components: 
 
1. 
All R3 VDLs shall have all the gravity control lock pins/sprockets repaired or 
upgraded to comply with the applicable Ford Motor Company standards and 
applicable sections of the GVOSS. 
9.28.12 
OHM and P&F Conveyors 
9.28.12.1 
OHM and P&F Conveyor Rivet less Chain Assemblies: 
 
1. 
A remanufactured trolley conveyor chain assembly shall be defined as any 
combination of new and used chain, chain trolleys and/or chain attachments 
assembled together for the purpose of conveying product in an OHM or P&F 
conveyor application. 
 
2. 
All chain trolleys, whether new or rebuilt, shall conform to Ford 
Manufacturing Standards CX-2 and all other applicable specifications. 
Additionally, all reused chain shall be tested for load as specified in 
applicable sections of the GVOSS. 
 
3. 
Rivet less chain may be reused where the overall elongation does not 
exceed 2½ % elongation. Rivet less chain assemblies with greater than 2½ 
% elongation may be used when approved by the respective Ford Division 
Conveyor Engineering Supervisor. Chain with 3% or greater elongation shall 
not be reused. This can be measured as approximately 10'-3" over 30 links 
for 4" chain, and 10'-3½" over 20 links for 6" chain. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 391 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
4. 
Remanufactured OHM conveyor chain shall be assembled from used side 
links in combination with new center links and pins. Previously used center 
links and chain pins shall not be reused on refurbished chain assemblies. 
 
5. 
Remanufactured P&F conveyor chain shall be assembled from used side 
links in combination with new center links, chain pins and pusher dogs. 
Previously used center links, chain pins and pusher dogs shall not be 
reused on refurbished chain assemblies. 
 
6. 
The new center link and pin material composition shall match the side link 
material composition – i.e., MT15, 8642, Frost Smooth Link, etc. – for which 
they are assembled. 
 
7. 
The remanufactured chain assembly may utilize reused I-attachments only. 
The reuse of B-attachments for remanufactured trolley conveyor chain is 
prohibited. 
 
8. 
The remanufactured conveyor chain assembly shall utilize new fasteners, in 
compliance with applicable sections of the GVOSS, for all load carrying 
connections at trolley attachments, load bars, etc. Previously used fasteners 
shall not be reused in the assembly of remanufactured trolley conveyor 
chain. 
 
9. 
All chain trolleys used on remanufactured trolley conveyor chain assemblies 
shall be new or remanufactured from: 
A. 
Frost, Inc. 
B. 
Saginaw Products Corporation 
9.28.12.2 
OHM and P&F Conveyor Drive Assemblies: 
 
1. 
Only rotary floating caterpillar type drives as specified in applicable sections 
of the GVOSS shall be used for remanufactured trolley chain conveyor 
systems. Motors shall be refurbished as defined within this specification. 
 
2. 
Brakes shall be refurbished as defined within this specification. 
 
3. 
All reducers shall be refurbished as identified in this specification. All 
remanufactured gearboxes shall be reassembled to the floating drive frame 
utilizing new fasteners. 
 
4. 
All remanufactured drive assemblies shall utilize cogged, or "no-slip", design 
belts to couple the motor, brake and reducer. Any previously used drives 
that have V-type belts shall be reworked to provide the cogged belt design 
in compliance with applicable sections of the GVOSS. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 392 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
5. 
The Contractor shall inspect the roller nest assembly and adjustable back-
up bar to ensure they are functioning properly and do not have any sign of 
unusual wear. The offending components shall be repaired or replaced as 
necessary to provide a fully functional system. 
 
6. 
The Contractor shall inspect the inner- and outer-frame cam rollers to 
ensure they are functioning properly and do not have any sign of unusual 
wear. The offending components shall be repaired or replaced as necessary 
to provide a fully functional system. 
 
7. 
The used caterpillar chain may be reused on the remanufactured drive if 
previously applied in one direction only. The reused caterpillar chain shall 
be installed on the remanufactured drive such that the previously worn side 
of the pusher dog becomes the unused side. The cat chain shall be 
replaced with a new assembly if the previously used cat chain has already 
been reversed such that both sides of the pusher dogs have been utilized. 
 
8. 
The output drive sprocket and caterpillar chain take-up sprocket shall be 
inspected for wear. If unusual or excessive wear is observed, the sprockets 
shall be replaced. The drive sprocket mounting bolts (drilled into the reducer 
output shaft) shall be replaced and safety wired as specified in applicable 
sections of the GVOSS. 
 
9. 
The adjustable compression springs used to provide the required pre-load 
and overload capacity shall be new as shown in Branch Drawing 3ZF-
20705. 
 
10. The remanufactured drive assembly shall be equipped with a new overload 
limit switch which will stop the conveyor as required in applicable sections of 
the GVOSS. 
 
11. The remanufactured drive assembly shall be equipped with a new proximity 
switches used to monitor the caterpillar chain engaged/disengaged 
condition. 
 
12. The Contractor shall confirm that the caterpillar chain lubrication system is 
functioning properly. The offending components shall be repaired or 
replaced as necessary to provide a fully functional system. 
 
13. The Contractor shall confirm that the grease fittings and lubrication system 
for the back-up rollers is functioning properly. The offending components 
shall be repaired or replaced as necessary to provide a fully functional 
system. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 393 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
14. All remanufactured drive assemblies shall include automatic lubricators with 
pre-mounted heads to lubricate the caterpillar chain rollers and dogs. 
Previously used lubricators are acceptable; however, the tubing and nozzles 
shall be new, and the functionality of the assembly and related devices must 
be confirmed. Other offending components shall be repaired or replaced as 
applicable. 
9.28.12.3 
OHM and P&F Conveyor Take-Up Assemblies: 
 
1. 
The take-up safety chains shall be new for all R3 trolley conveyor systems. 
Reuse of take-up safety chains shall be strictly prohibited. 
 
2. 
The air cylinder shall be refurbished as defined within this specification. The 
take-up cylinder size and function shall be verified to be in compliance with 
the proposed application. 
 
3. 
All remanufactured take-ups shall have air cylinders piped as shown on the 
branch drawings. Valve boards and mounted flow components shall be new 
unless otherwise specified. 
 
4. 
The Contractor shall inspect the carriage rollers to ensure they are 
functioning properly and do not have any sign of unusual wear. The 
offending components shall be repaired/replaced or greased as necessary 
to provide a properly functioning system. 
 
5. 
The Contractor shall replace the take-up slides. Improper slide orientation 
shall be corrected to suit the specific project parameters. The R3 take-up 
travel distance shall be compatible with the proposed chain pitch design. 
 
6. 
The remanufactured take-up shall be equipped with new limit switches and 
a cam operated actuator mounted to the cylinder rod end as indicated in 
applicable sections of the GVOSS. 
 
7. 
Take-up cylinder size and function shall be verified for the new 
installation/application. 
 
8. 
Take-up frame and slides must be verified to be compatible with the new 
chain pitch. 
9.28.12.4 
OHM and P&F Power Only Conveyor Track: 
 
1. 
Overhead Monorail and P&F power only track shall be inspected for wear 
prior to designation for reuse and may be reused only with prior approval by 
the respective Ford Division Conveyor Engineering Supervisor. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 394 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
2. 
Overhead Monorail and P&F power only roller turns shall not be reused 
unless approved by the respective Ford Division Conveyor Engineering 
Supervisor. Where roller turns are approved for reuse, the segment rollers 
shall be replaced, including mounting hardware. 
 
3. 
Traction Wheel Turns shall not be reused unless approved by the respective 
Ford Division Conveyor Engineering Supervisor. Where Traction Wheel 
Turns are approved for reuse, the Contractor shall inspect the tapered roller 
bearing and provide preventive maintenance as described in the OEM 
service and maintenance instructions: i.e., check seals, verify that the 
grease fitting is functioning properly, purge and re-grease, etc. The 
refurbished traction wheel shall be assembled to the hub by means of new 
fasteners as defined in applicable sections of the GVOSS. 
 
4. 
Overhead Monorail and P&F power only vertical curves shall be not be 
relocated. 
 
5. 
Overhead Monorail and P&F power only expansion joints shall not be 
relocated. 
9.28.12.5 
P&F Conveyor Track: 
 
1. 
Power & Free conveyor straight track sections shall be inspected for wear 
prior to designation for reuse. Track sections that exhibit more than 20% 
wear of the running surfaces shall not be reused. The R3 straight track 
sections designated for reuse shall be reassembled in the final location 
utilizing new fasteners as defined in applicable sections of the GVOSS. 
 
2. 
Power & Free conveyor roller turns shall not be reused unless approved by 
the respective Ford Division Conveyor Engineering Supervisor. Where roller 
turns are approved for reuse, the segment rollers shall be replaced, 
including mounting hardware. 
 
3. 
Power & Free conveyor track switches shall not be reused unless approved 
by the respective Ford Division Conveyor Engineering Supervisor. Where 
track switches are approved for reuse, the pneumatic actuator shall be 
inspected and refurbished as defined in this specification document. 
 
4. 
Power & Free conveyor vertical curves shall not be reused. 
 
5. 
Power & Free conveyor expansion joints shall not be reused. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 395 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
6. 
Power & Free conveyor stop assemblies may be reused. The actuator stop 
blade and other related moving components shall be inspected for wear. 
The offending parts shall be repaired or replaced as necessary to provide a 
fully functional stop assembly. 
 
7. 
Power & Free conveyor carrier transfer/pusher assemblies may be reused. 
The actuator, pusher dog and other related moving components shall be 
inspected for wear. The offending parts shall be repaired or replaced as 
necessary to provide a fully functional stop assembly. The proximity 
switches used to control pusher dog carriage positions and transfer 
operation shall be replaced with new proximity switches to the latest 
standard specification release level. Where the existing devices are a 
different release level, they shall be brought to the attention of the Ford 
Project Engineer 
 
8. 
Power & Free conveyor carrier positioner assemblies may be reused. The 
actuator, pusher dog and other related moving components shall be 
inspected for wear. The offending parts shall be repaired or replaced as 
necessary to provide a fully functional stop assembly. The proximity 
switches used to control pusher dog carriage positions and transfer 
operation shall be replaced with new proximity switches to the latest 
standard specification release level. Where the existing devices are a 
different release level, they shall be brought to the attention of the Ford 
Project Engineer 
 
9. 
All R3 Inverted P&F conveyor track shall include new track seal in all 
production zones and as directed in the project specification and in 
compliance with applicable sections of the GVOSS. Reuse of inverted P&F 
track seal is prohibited. 
9.28.12.6 
OHM and P&F Conveyor Carrier Components: 
 
1. 
Remanufactured free trolleys may be utilized when approved by the 
respective Ford Division Conveyor Engineering Supervisor. 
 
2. 
All remanufactured free trolleys shall comply with the allowable tolerances 
as specified by the original equipment manufacturer. The rebuild Supplier 
shall be responsible to provide documentation regarding OEM design 
tolerances, replacement component selection, and specification compliance 
validation procedures as part of the buy-off/approval process. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 396 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
3. 
The free trolley remanufacturing process shall include, as a minimum, the 
following operations as part of the refurbishment process: 
A. 
Disassemble free trolley and mechanically/chemically clean all 
components. 
B. 
All fasteners and pivot pins will be new SAE grade 5 minimum. 
C. 
Inspect all trolley components for excessive wear and cracks. Any 
components exhibiting a crack or excessive wear shall be discarded. A 
dye penetrant process shall be used to determine if cracks are present 
in the trolley body. 
D. 
Side plates with worn and/or elongated king pin mounting holes and 
tripper pins shall be bored and bushed to restore the side plate to the 
original design intent. 
E. 
New load wheels and axles shall be assembled to trolley body, in 
compliance with Specification Section 14540, and torqued to the OEM 
specifications. 
F. 
New horizontal guide rollers and axles assembled, including new 
grease fittings, shall be assembled to trolley body, in compliance with 
applicable sections of the GVOSS, and torqued to the OEM 
specifications. 
G. 
New pivot pins, bushings and bearings shall be assembled within the 
trolley body, in compliance with applicable sections of the GVOSS, and 
per the original OEM specifications. 
H. 
All previously used fasteners – i.e., screws, bolts and nuts – shall be 
replaced with new fasteners and torqued to the OEM specifications. 
Discard all bolts, nuts, cotter pins, washers, load wheels, horizontal 
guide rollers, pivot pins and axles. 
I. 
All remanufactured trolleys shall be checked prior to shipment using a 
precision fixture to ensure proper engagement and clearances with the 
chain pusher dogs. The results shall be documented and included in 
the trolley history. 
J. 
All trolleys parts shall be lubricated in compliance with the applicable 
specification. 
 
4. 
Each remanufactured trolley body shall be stamped with a separate 
traceable number which shall contain the following information: 
A. 
Name of the company performing the refurbishment. Where company 
names are large, the name may be truncated and shall clearly identify 
the company.  
B. 
Month and year work performed 
C. 
Job and Sequence number 
D. 
The trolley re-manufacturer shall create and maintain a history of each 
refurbished trolley assembly to include as a minimum: 
E. 
The new trolley Identification number 
F. 
The previous re-manufacturer's trolley identification number (if 
applicable) 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 397 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
G. 
A description of the trolley. e.g., Lead, Load, or Trailing 
H. 
A description and date of the work performed 
I. 
The assembly plant and system identification (name, location, 
department) 
J. 
Purchase Order number 
K. 
Checking/gauging and R&M data 
 
5. 
Tow bars shall be inspected to ensure proper actuation of the shock 
absorber. Defective shock absorbers shall be replaced as directed by the 
project specification. The tow bars shall be re-calibrated per the 
manufacturer's recommendations and process to accept the new loads. 
 
6. 
All R3 trolley conveyor carrier kingpins shall be constructed new, in 
compliance with GVOSS Section 9.4 and as defined by the specific project 
specification. Reuse of carrier kingpins is not allowed. 
 
7. 
All R3 trolley conveyor carrier secondary safety restraints shall be 
constructed new, in compliance with GVOSS Section 9.4 and as defined by 
the specific project specification. Reuse of trolley conveyor carrier 
secondary safety restraints is prohibited. 
 
8. 
All carriers identified for reuse shall comply with the requirements of 
GVOSS Section 9.4, except that non-destructive testing – i.e., ultra-sound 
or Magnaflux. – shall be completed on 20% of proposed reused carrier 
frames. 
9.28.12.7 
Part Carriers Hang Chains: 
1. 
Part hang chains – i.e., Phosphate/E-Coat, Engine, Frames, etc. – shall be 
new in compliance with the original design intent. Reuse of part hang chains 
shall be strictly prohibited. 
9.28.12.8 
Automatic Lubrication Systems: 
 
1. 
All pivoting, sliding and rotating components on R3 conveyor assemblies 
shall include automatic lubricators with pre-mounted heads to lubricate the 
caterpillar chain rollers and dogs (as previously stated). In addition, 
automatic oil lubricators shall be provided for chain pins and all pivoting, 
sliding and rotating members of the free trolleys. Automatic Greasers shall 
be provided for the power trolleys, free trolley load wheels, and free trolley 
side guide rollers. 
 
2. 
Previously used lubricators are acceptable; however, all plastic delivery 
lines shall be replaced with metal lines. The functionality of these devices 
must be confirmed. Offending components shall be repaired or replaced as 
applicable. 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 398 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
9.28.13 
Flat-Top Conveyors 
9.28.13.1 
Where identified in the project specification, Flat Top conveyors shall be reused 
in compliance with the requirements identified within this section and with 
applicable sections of the GVOSS. 
9.28.13.2 
Where the existing Flat Top conveyor does not have a central lube, system 
installed at the head and tail frames, central lube blocks, fittings and all 
necessary tubing, etc. shall be installed in compliance with Ford conveyor 
standards. 
9.28.13.3 
The machinery guarding strategy shall be modified to include perimeter fencing 
with Gate Plug access in lieu of individual (large, custom fit) machinery guards 
for the drive chains, etc. 
9.28.13.4 
Flat Top Chain: 
 
1. 
Flat Top chain with less than 1½% elongation may be reused and/or 
refurbished. Any Flat Top chain that is planned to be refurbished that has 
greater than 1½% elongation shall be subject to review and approval by the 
Ford Conveyor Engineering Supervisor. 
 
2. 
For modifications to existing systems, the top plates shall match the existing 
flat plate. Chain for extensions to existing conveyors shall match existing 
chain. Bearings and dimensions must be identical to existing conveyor 
chain. The Contractor shall be responsible for obtaining correct dimensional 
and operation requirements. 
 
3. 
The flanged wheels shall be inspected for wear and replaced as necessary. 
 
4. 
The axles and wheel bushing shall be inspected for wear and replaced as 
necessary. 
 
5. 
The lubricant passages within the axle shaft shall and the grease fitting shall 
be inspected for functionality. Any shafts and fittings not compliant shall be 
replaced or repaired as necessary. 
 
6. 
The steel chain roller shall be inspected for wear and replaced as 
necessary. 
9.28.13.5 
Flat Top Head and Drive Assembly: 
 
1. 
The motor shall be refurbished as defined within this section. 
 
2. 
The gearbox shall be refurbished as defined within this section. 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Issue Date: JAN. 10, 2014 
SECTION 9 – FACILITY CONVEYORS 
Revision Date: June 8, 2025 
 
Page 399 of 414 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
 Printed copies are uncontrolled 
3. 
All components of the head frame drive shaft, including but not limited to the 
shaft, the main bearings, shaft key seats, keys, formsprag, couplings, shear 
pin, compensating sprocket and chain sprockets shall be inspected for 
wear. All offending components shall be identified and repaired or replaced 
as required in compliance with applicable sections of the GVOSS. 
 
4. 
All components of the head frame including, but not limited to, the 
framework, bearing supports, catenary roller surfaces, and support legs 
shall be visually inspected for signs of unusual wear or fatigue fracture. All 
offending components shall be identified and repaired or replaced as 
required. 
 
5. 
Minimally, the head frame and drive assembly bearings shall be inspected 
as identified above and repacked with grease in compliance with the OEM 
recommendations. 
 
6. 
Existing finger details and tire guides shall be replaced with new parts as 
identified in Specification Section applicable sections of the GVOSS. 
9.28.13.6 
Flat Top Take-up Assembly 
 
1. 
All components of the tail frame, including but not limited to, the framework, 
bearing supports, bearing slides, roller surfaces, and support legs shall be 
visually inspected for signs of unusual wear or fatigue fracture. All offending 
components shall be identified and repaired or replaced as required. 
 
2. 
Minimally, the tail frame assembly bearings shall be inspected as identified 
above and repacked with grease in compliance with the OEM 
recommendations. 
 
3. 
Existing finger details and tire guides shall be replaced with new parts as 
identified in applicable sections of the GVOSS. 
9.28.13.7 
Flat Top Track Sections 
 
1. 
Inspect and replace as necessary the ball rail and return tracks. Replace the 
ball rail when the measured wear exceeds ¼". 
9.28.14

9.28.14.1 
All bridge cranes and related equipment shall be subject to a complete 
engineering review and health assessment inspection prior reuse/relocation to 
determine feasibility for continued use. 
9.28.14.2 
The equipment provided by the Owner shall be qualified as acceptable for the 
proposed application by the Contractor and original equipment Supplier.

The Contractor quotation shall include all services required to have the

9.28.14.4 
The Contractor quotation shall include all new components required to rebuild,

cast or malleable attachment or connecting devices is not acceptable.
