printout(s) and (PDF’s) with their controls deliverable per line or sub assembly. 
14.3.1.9.3

14.3.1.9.4 
The Tier 1 Integrator shall also make and install all tags noted in Ford standard 
global panel design(s) as integrator, installer or others to make tags. 
14.3.1.9.5 
The full stand-alone controls design of the Ford standard global panel design(s) 
shall not be merged into the controls application design file(s), only the 
connections, references and integrator, installer or others to make tags from 
14.3.1.11.3 and 14.3.1.11.4. 
14.3.1.10 
Tier 1 Integrators shall use Ford supplied Light Screen Worksheet to determine 
safe distance. Tier 1 Integrators shall also follow any local safety requirements. 
14.3.1.11 
Tier 1 Integrators shall use Ford supplied Ethernet calculator to determine 
processor and Ethernet network capacity. 
14.3.1.11.1 Calculation accuracy and functions shall be the responsibility of the installer /

14.3.1.11.2 Ethernet network capacity shall be compliant with the limitations of the Ford 
supplied Ethernet calculator at all phases until final plant buyoff. 
14.3.1.11.3 Connection total shall be 80% or less for new designs and 90% or less for kits. 
14.3.1.11.4 Bandwidth shall be 80% capacity or less for new designs and 90% or less for 
kits. 
 
14.3.1.12 
The Tier 1 Integrators are responsible to update all hardwire designs for revisions 
and/or fixes as part of their base quote. 
14.3.1.13 
Tier 1 Integrators shall provide a water flow switch wired to PLC to indicate that 
any chiller system for any welding or laser application is flowing properly. A flow 
switch is required for each water circuit, if chiller has multiple circuits and each 
shall have its own water flow switch. 
14.3.1.14 
Any robot loading or unloading a press/hemmer/roll hemmer shall have a robot 
cube type (global) clear zone safety interlock to the press/hemmer for motion 
clear when the robot is clear of the press/hemmer tower. See also GVOSS 
Section 13 Robots. 
14.3.1.15 
All cables shall be verified for proper sizing.  
14.3.1.16 
All hoses shall be verified for proper sizing.  
14.3.1.17 
Addition cable and hose requirements. 
14.3.1.17.1 Cable and hose bend radius shall not be below the manufacturer’s minimum 
bend radius. 
14.3.1.17.2 Cable and hose twist angles shall not be more than the manufacturer’s maximum 
twist angle. 
14.3.1.18 
Tier 1 Integrators shall use Ford supplied Weld PDP calculator to determine the 
Weld PDP capacity and the max quantity of branch circuits. 
14.3.1.18.1 Calculation accuracy and functions shall be the responsibility of the installer / 
integrator and reviewed by Ford VOME Controls. 
14.3.1.18.2 The Weld PDP capacity shall be compliant with the Weld PDP calculator at all 
phases until final plant buyoff. 
14.3.1.18.3 The Weld PDP calculated current (A) plus the welding type % shall be less than 
or equal to 80% of the plant drop for new PDP designs and 90% or less for in 
plant kits. It is required to provide a minimum of 20% spare for new PDP designs 
and 10% for in plant kits.

14.3.1.19 
Tier 1 Integrators shall use Ford supplied Control PDP calculator to determine 
the Control PDP capacity and the max quantity of branch circuits. 
14.3.1.19.1 Calculation accuracy and functions shall be the responsibility of the installer / 
integrator and reviewed by Ford VOME Controls. 
14.3.1.19.2 The Control PDP capacity shall be compliant with the Control PDP calculator at 
all phases until final plant buyoff. 
14.3.1.19.3 The Control PDP calculated current (A) and the inrush (A) % shall be less than or 
equal to 80% of the plant drop for new PDP designs and 90% or less for in plant 
kits. It is required to provide a minimum of 20% spare for new PDP designs and 
10% for in plant kits.

identified in the Program SOW. Exceptions shall require a deviation prior to 
design, testing and implementation. 
14.3.1.20.2 Tier 1 Integrators shall consult with the Drive and equipment suppliers for proper 
usage, design and sizing. 
14.3.1.20.2.1 For Rockwell PowerFlex 70, 520 and 750 series drive applications see Rockwell 
Drive Specification. 
14.3.1.20.3 Tier 1 Integrators shall also follow any additional local safety requirements.

14.3.2 
Software Application – Logic and HMI 
Please reference the latest GVOSS Programming Guide in Teamcenter. 
14.3.2.1 
All designs shall utilize the Ford Global tag naming convention. 
14.3.2.2 
All designs shall utilize the Ford Global I.P. address requirements. 
14.3.2.3 
The only alias tags that are allowed are those shown in the GVOSS Logic 
Programming Guide. Any change shall need approval from Ford VOME Controls 
(follow the deviation process). 
14.3.2.4 
Use of a periodic task shall be limited to safety tasks.  Any change requires an 
approved deviation. 
14.3.2.5 
All software and components shall comply with the Ford issued revision level 
matrix for the given plant/program. 
14.3.2.6 
Ford standard UDT's (User Defined Tags) and logic routines shall be used to 
compile the processor program. 
14.3.2.7 
Only the routines designated with "_edit" will be allowed to be modified. Any 
issues with a file which is not identified with "_edit" are to be brought to the Ford 
VOME Controls department attention for resolution. 
14.3.2.8 
The non-safety file construction will be done by copying routines and tags of the 
desired entities and deleting unused programs. These files can then be 
connected to real world inputs/outputs in the "input mapping_edit" and "output 
mapping_edit" routines. The sequence and associated machine will be 
accommodated by completing the "motion_edit" routine for that entity. 
14.3.2.9 
The entire Ford supplied safety task is to be copied into the application 
processor. Delete tags and routines that are not required for the application. The 
safety routines indicated with "_edit" will then need to be modified for the 
application. 
14.3.2.10 
The entire standard HMI routines and tags shall be copied into the application 
processor file. Then delete screen specific routines which do not apply to the 
application. Modify the "_edit" routines to attach to the machine programming 
and turn on or off visibility of entities on the screens as applicable for the 
process. Screen specific tags are part of the HMI UDT are not to be deleted or 
modified, they are just not used. 
14.3.2.11 
The Ford standard systems (such as FIS, PCB, AVS, etc.) tags and routines 
shall be copied into the application file for each asset. The associated "_edit" file 
can then be modified for the appropriate triggers. 
 

 
 
 
 
Global Vehical Operations Structures Standard 
 
Issue Date: Jan. 08, 2021 
SECTION 14 - CONTROLS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
 
 
Page 11 of 34 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
  Printed copies are uncontrolled 
14.3.2.12 
The RPI's of all input and output module configurations shall be configured per 
GVOSS Logic Programming Guide. 
14.3.2.13 
For the HMI: Ford standard screens shall be loaded into the application HMI file.  
14.3.2.14 
HMI local messages are to be completed for both screen indicators and banner 
fault messages appropriate to the application.  
14.3.2.15 
Specialty designs of which there is not a Ford standard routine(s) developed 
(Hemmer, etc.) will require the Tier 1 Integrator to develop under Ford VOME 
Controls department direction. 
14.3.2.16 
Tier 1 Integrators shall utilize Ford Standard Abbreviation list for all messages, 
descriptors and tags. 
14.3.2.17 
Standard and Safety Task Settings: 
14.3.2.17.1 Safety task scan time shall be equal to or less than 20 milliseconds. 
14.3.2.17.2 Safety task period shall be equal to 50 milliseconds. 
14.3.2.17.3 Safety task priority shall be set to 1. 
14.3.2.17.4 Safety task watchdog shall be set to 22 milliseconds. 
14.3.2.17.5 Standard task watchdog shall be equal to 500 milliseconds. 
14.3.2.17.6 For PLCs with system overhead time slice configuration shall be set to 20%.

  Printed copies are uncontrolled 
14.3.3 
Controls Build – General 
All components shall comply with the approved components list identified in the 
Section 2 Approved GVOSS Controls Architecture Components and the Program 
SOW. 
14.3.3.1 
No more than six (6) mid line Ethernet bulkhead type connections from switch to 
switch or switch to device. 
14.3.3.2 
Ethernet connectors shall be D-coded M12 exterior to control enclosure and 
RJ45 within all control enclosures. 
14.3.3.3 
A single Ethernet cable shall be used to connect two devices. Ethernet extension 
connectors are not authorized for use. 
14.3.3.4 
The maximum Ethernet cable length is limited to 85 meters, including patch 
cords (panel internal cord sets). 
14.3.3.5 
Patch cords (panel internal cord sets) shall not exceed 5 meters. 
14.3.3.6 
All cable extensions shall be reviewed and approved by the Ford lead controls 
engineer. If extensions are approved; then it should be mounted on the side of 
the cable tray and labeled. If extension connections are approved to be in the 
cable tray, then the cover shall be marked such that the plant is aware a 
connection is present in the tray. Data/communication cable extensions are not 
allowed. 
14.3.3.7 
Cable tray (floor, fence, overhead, etc.) shall be sized to comply with all project 
site local laws, specifications, electrical codes and fill requirements with at least 
20% reserved fill space per channel. Communication cables (Ethernet, 
Devicenet, etc.), control cables, ground / bond cables and high voltage cables 
shall be separated with dividers. See the latest GVOSS Controls Hardware 
Template for Grounding, Bonding and Cable Tray Installation Examples for 
GVOSS examples. See also GVOSS Section 1 General Requirements and 
Section 2 Design for additional requirements.  
14.3.3.8 
Hook and loop tie wraps shall be used for ALL electrical cables including 
Ethernet cables (see example below). Buckle style shall be prohibited. See the 
project "Approved Components List" for additional information on approved 
manufacturers and models. 
a. For Canada: Hook and loop tie wraps shall only be used on Ethernet and 
Communication cables. 
b. The exceptions to this requirement are FTF Panels, Tier 2 standard products, 
standard panels and the Robot RTU unit and systems that require specific cable 
anchor point securing methods by the manufacturer. i.e. (Cat Track entrance and 
exit points, Service Loops on VDLs, Indexing Slides, Dumps etc.). 

 
 
 
 
Global Vehical Operations Structures Standard 
 
Issue Date: Jan. 08, 2021 
SECTION 14 - CONTROLS

Page 13 of 34 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS

14.3.3.9 
Removed 
14.3.3.10 
I/O Modules 
14.3.3.10.1 The integrators shall calculate the power consumption and quick-connect time 
needed for components to determine the number of modules that can be daisy 
chained. 
14.3.3.10.1.1 No more than five device power and / or ethernet shall be daisy chained. For 
applications with quick change connectors, no more than ten device power and / 
or ethernet shall be daisy chained; applications such as Framing gates / end 
effectors.  
14.3.3.10.2 All I/O shall be electronically short circuit and over current protected. 
14.3.3.10.3 IP6x style I/O modules shall be mounted 10 inches (254 mm) to 66 inches (1676 
mm) above working level. 
14.3.3.10.4 IP6x style I/O modules should be positioned where I/O lights on / off can easily 
be seen by maintenance. 

 
 
 
 
Global Vehical Operations Structures Standard 
 
Issue Date: Jan. 08, 2021 
SECTION 14 - CONTROLS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
 
 
Page 14 of 34 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
  Printed copies are uncontrolled 
14.3.3.11 
All field IP5x and IP6x type devices (i.e., power supplies, ethernet switches, 
routers, safety I/O, standard I/O) shall use protection brackets when components 
or cables or connectors are subject to damage. These are typically not necessary 
for robot end effectors. See example below. See also GVOSS Section 2 Design 
for design(s). 
                      
 
 
14.3.3.12 
Design placement of IP5x and IP6x style devices requires Ford VOME Controls 
approval and the basic layout placement and tags shall be documented as part of 
the Controls Application Package. 
14.3.3.13 
IP5x and IP6x drives shall be mounted as close to the motor as possible. 
14.3.3.13.1 IP5x drives should be mounted just outside the fence near the motor. 
14.3.3.13.2 IP6x drives should be mounted on the station / frame / conveyor inside the cell 
near the motor. 
14.3.3.13.3 IP6x drives shall be mounted 10 inches (254 mm) to 66 inches (1676 mm) above 
working level; unless approved by Ford VOME Controls.  
14.3.3.14 
All power tees and taps (i.e., AC power tee, AC power tap, DC power tee, DC 
power taps, safety tee, DeviceNet tee) shall use protection brackets when 
components or cables or connectors are subject to damage. These are typically 
not necessary for robot end effectors. See GVOSS Section 2 Design for 
design(s). 
14.3.3.14.1 All power tees and taps need to be accessible and not buried in tray etc. 
14.3.3.14.2 DC safety power tees (orange tee) are required for IP6x motion power solutions. 
Example: DC safety power tee (orange tee) required to combine IP6x power 
supply hot power pins with IP6x safety module safety power pins (gate / E-stop); 

 
 
 
 
Global Vehical Operations Structures Standard 
 
Issue Date: Jan. 08, 2021 
SECTION 14 - CONTROLS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
 
 
Page 15 of 34 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
  Printed copies are uncontrolled 
to power station manifolds. 
14.3.3.14.3 All power tees and taps shall be mounted 10 inches (254 mm) to 66 inches (1676 
mm) above working level; unless approved by Ford VOME Controls. 
14.3.3.15 
Grounding and Bonding. 
14.3.3.15.1 Grounding and bonding of electrical systems shall meet any regional and local 
requirements plus applied as shown in the latest GVOSS Controls Hardware 
Template Bonding and Cable Tray Installation Examples. 
14.3.3.15.1.1 System grounding and bonding shall be documented as part of the Controls 
Application Package. 
14.3.3.15.2 Tier 1 Integrator or installer shall install an external protective conductor 
(equipment grounding conductor) for each machine supply circuit to the external 
protective earthing system (grounding electrode system). The conductor shall be 
continuous, suitably protected from physical damage, as short as practicable and 
connected to the building column by exothermic welding or other methods 
approved by local code FNA Equipment grounding conductor examples. The 
Facility Owner (i.e., Ford Land) shall identify the columns of the building metal 
structure that may be used for this purpose, having a resistance of less than 5.0 
Ohm to earth. 
14.3.3.15.3 All materials, surface preparation and labor shall be supplied by the equipment 
installer. 
14.3.3.15.4 Final disposition of contaminants shall be coordinated with the plant in 
accordance with the Industrial Hygiene Standard and OSHA Lead in 
Constructions Standard. 
14.3.3.16 
System integrator shall provide dust-and-water proof covers for all unused 
Ethernet PORTS, I/O PORTS, and POWER PORTS on robot control panels, 
weld control panels, control panels, HMI panels and any IP5x / IP6x type 
modules or devices. This also includes any unused connectors on the robot base 
(Axis 1) and the robot arm (Axis 3). 
14.3.3.17 
System integrator shall provide all necessary DeviceNet (DNET) terminating 
resistors for any given material handling application. Applies only if DeviceNet is 
used and in the project "Approved Components List”. 
14.3.3.18 
Short-Circuit Current rating (SCCR). 
14.3.3.18.1 All industrial control panels shall be clearly marked with panel short circuit current 
rating (SCCR) per NEC / IEC / CEC / OSHA / Project site local laws, 
Specifications, Norms. 
14.3.3.18.2 Control panels shall be designed, fabricated and contain components to at least 
meet the following minimum SCCR requirements:

Issue Date: Jan. 08, 2021 
SECTION 14 - CONTROLS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
 
 
Page 16 of 34

  Printed copies are uncontrolled 
a. Panels requiring 30 amp or 60 amp service drops shall have a minimum SCCR 
of 10kA. 
b. Panels requiring 100 amp or 200 amp service drops shall have a minimum 
SCCR of 20kA. 
c. Panels requiring greater than 200 amp service drops shall have a minimum 
SCCR of 45kA. 
d. Panels requiring Direct Feed substation service drops shall have a minimum 
SCCR of 65kA.  
14.3.3.19 
Controls Global Wire Color Codes (After plant drop / PDP etc.) below:  Buss drop 
cable wire, shall follow regional wire color codes and be shown in the controls 
prints. 
14.3.3.19.1 Power Circuits: 
14.3.3.19.1.1 Black (BK) = 3 Phase AC 380-575 VAC (L1, L2, L3) Ungrounded 
14.3.3.19.1.2 Black with Orange Stripe (BK/OG)= 3 Phase AC 380-575 VAC (L1,L2,L3) 
Ungrounded which remains energized with enclosure disconnect off. 
14.3.3.19.2 Control Circuits: 
14.3.3.19.2.1 Red (RD) = Single Phase AC 110-120/220-240 VAC Ungrounded fed from 
Control Power Disconnect. 
14.3.3.19.2.2 White (WH) = Single Phase AC 110-120/220-240 VAC Grounded fed from 
Control Power Disconnect. 
14.3.3.19.2.3 Orange (OG) = Single Phase AC 110-120/220-240 VAC Ungrounded which 
remains energized with Control Power Disconnect off. 
14.3.3.19.2.4 White with Orange stripe (WH/OG) = Single Phase AC 110-120/220-240 VAC 
Grounded which remains energized with Control Power Disconnect off. 
14.3.3.19.2.5 Blue (Dark) (BU) = +24 VDC ungrounded fed from Control Power Disconnect. 
14.3.3.19.2.6 White with Blue (Dark) stripe (WH/BU) = 0 VDC ungrounded fed from Control 
Power Disconnect. 
14.3.3.19.2.7 Blue (Dark) with Orange stripe (BU/OG) = +24 VDC ungrounded which remains 
energized with Control Power Disconnect off. 
14.3.3.19.2.8 White with Blue (Dark) stripe (WH/BU) = 0 VDC grounded which remains 
energized with Control Power Disconnect off. 
14.3.3.19.2.9 Green with Yellow stripe (GN/YE) = Ground / Bond (PE). 
14.3.3.19.2.10 
Violet (VT) or White (WH) (White is not allowed in Canada) with a middle

14.3.3.20 
Tier 1 Integrators shall provide the stand for each: Control power panel; PLC in a 
box; and large panels not floor or fence mounted.  
14.3.3.21 
Tier 1 Integrators are to supply generic arc flash labels consistent with the 
requirements spelled out in the regional Electrical Code. This shall apply to all 
panels which contain 50 volts and above. 
14.3.3.22 
Tier 1 Integrators shall provide a control power distribution panel custom design 
where the Ford standard does not exactly support the application. Any custom 
designs shall be the responsibility of the installer/integrator to create. All designs 
will then be approved by Ford VOME Controls and documented with a Ford 
standard controls naming / number. 
14.3.3.23 
All applications shall be engineered such that the voltage drop due to cable 
length does not compromise component operation requirements. 24 VDC circuits 
shall not drop below 23 VDC. 
14.3.3.24 
Conduit and Flexible Conduit Runs: 
14.3.3.24.1 Where flexible or rigid conduit enters a box, it shall be attached with double 
locknuts (one inside, one outside) with an insulating bushing at conduit 
termination. 
14.3.3.24.2 Running threads shall not be acceptable for any conduit run. 
14.3.3.24.3 Nonmetallic flexible conduit shall not be allowed; any flexible conduit runs shall 
meet all applicable local codes and shall be metallic. 
14.3.3.24.4 When conduit runs are used, they shall require a locknut and sealing ring on the 
outside and an NRTL (OSHA's Nationally Recognized Testing Laboratory) / CE / 
regional recognized listed grounding locknut and insulated bushing on the inside.  
14.3.3.24.5 All exiting conduit runs shall require a locknut and sealing ring on the outside of 
the panel and a locknut and insulated bushing on the inside. 
14.3.3.25 
Opening hinged electrical enclosures shall require the use of a 7 mm square key 
in accordance with 7E DIN 7417. 

 
 
 
 
Global Vehical Operations Structures Standard 
 
Issue Date: Jan. 08, 2021 
SECTION 14 - CONTROLS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
 
 
Page 20 of 34 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
  Printed copies are uncontrolled 
14.3.4 
Conveyors Between Systems 
14.3.4.1 
Control of conveyor shall be independent of the two systems; stops shall be 
controlled by the zone control where they are located. 
14.3.4.2 
The conveyor shall continue to run if either system is shut down in separate PLC 
type.  
14.3.4.3 
In shared PLC type of conveyor; safeties shall be added to keep the conveyor 
running for when the connected controls system is shut down. 
14.3.4.3.1 
Example of shared PLC conveyor: BCE door line saring a PLC with the drop side 
controlled conveyor. 
14.3.4.3.2 
Example of added safeties to keep conveyor running: Added light screen(s) 
around the drop side conveyor to separate it from the BCE door line cell. 

 
 
 
 
Global Vehical Operations Structures Standard 
 
Issue Date: Jan. 08, 2021 
SECTION 14 - CONTROLS

14.4 
CABLE REQUIREMENTS 
14.4.1 
Approved Cable Manufacturers  
14.4.1.1 
Approved cable manufacturers are listed on the approved components list and 
referenced in the standard hardware drawings; any change shall follow the 
deviation process. 
14.4.2 
Control Cables 
14.4.2.1 
All receptacle wire insulation colors are to comply with wire colors shown in Ford 
standard drawings and shall meet any regional color requirements. Receptacle 
wires shall be tagged with pin number and guidelines outlined in 70ZF-20022808 
Connectors and Cords from Teamcenter. 
14.4.2.2 
All cable assemblies shall be factory manufactured with molded connectors. 
14.4.2.3 
4-pin and 5-pin mini (DC) cords and receptacles shall be 16 AWG / 1.5 mm², 600 
V, UL, CSA & CE approved rated for 10.0 amps. Cord sets shall have connectors 
on both ends, yellow TPE outer jacket, TC-ER/FT4 rated.  
14.4.2.3.1 
3-pin mini (AC) cords and receptacles shall be 16 AWG / 1.5 mm², 600 V, UL, 
CSA & CE approved rated for 10.0 amps. Cord sets shall have connectors on 
both ends, red TPE outer jacket, TC-ER/FT4 rated.  
14.4.2.4 
4-pin and 5-pin mini (DC) tee & tap connectors shall be yellow in color and UL, 
CSA & CE approved rated for 10.0 amps. 
14.4.2.4.1 
3-pin mini (AC) tee & tap connectors shall be red in color and UL, CSA & CE 
approved rated for 10.0 amps. 
14.4.2.5 
4-pin and 5-pin M12 Euro cords and receptacles shall be 18 AWG / 1 mm², 250 
V, UL, CSA & CE approved rated for 4.0 amps. Cord sets shall have connectors 
on both ends, yellow TPE outer jacket, ITC/PLTC/FT4 rated. 
14.4.2.6 
Mini style hi-flex cords shall be 16 AWG / 1.5 mm², 600V UL, CSA & CE 
approved rated for 10.0 amps. 
14.4.2.6.1 
Connectors on both ends, TPE outer jacket, FT1 4 conductor 20 million flexes at 
3 inches (76.20 mm) bend radius, 12 conductor 20 million flexes at 4.5 inches 
(114.30 mm) bend radius. 

 
 
 
 
Global Vehical Operations Structures Standard 
 
Issue Date: Jan. 08, 2021 
SECTION 14 - CONTROLS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
 
 
Page 22 of 34 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
  Printed copies are uncontrolled 
14.4.3 
Devicenet Cables 
14.4.3.1 
DEVICENET SHALL ONLY BE USED if in the project approved components list 
and the design use is approved by Ford VOME Controls. 
14.4.3.2 
Devicenet cables shall be Devicenet thin type, gray TPE outer jacket, 22 AWG / 
0.34 mm², 300 V, manufacturer high flex rated for 10 million flexes, UL, CSA & 
CE approved rated for 5.0 amps, ITC/PLTC/FT4 rated. Devicenet cables shall 
have factory molded mini connectors on both ends and be ODVA compliant. 
14.4.3.3 
Devicenet tee's and terminating resistors shall be gray in color.

 
 
 
 
Global Vehical Operations Structures Standard 
 
Issue Date: Jan. 08, 2021 
SECTION 14 - CONTROLS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
 
 
Page 23 of 34 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
  Printed copies are uncontrolled 
 
14.4.4 
Ethernet Cables 
14.4.4.1 
Each Ethernet assembly shall be factory assembled and tested to comply with 
TIA CAT5E/CAT6A requirements using a Fluke DSP-4000 analyzer. Test to 
include: Continuity, Propagation Delay, Delay Skew, Near End Crosstalk, 
Attenuation, Resistance, Return Loss, Power Sum Near End Crosstalk, Equal 
Level Far End Crosstalk, Power Sum Far End Crosstalk. 
14.4.4.2 
Ethernet cables exterior to enclosures not requiring high flex properties: Minimum 
requirements shall be 24 AWG / 0.25 mm² stranded, 250 Volts listed, 2 pair, 
twisted bonded pairs or equivalent, unshielded, TPE outer jacket, over-molded 
M12 D coded male connector on both ends, Rated Cat5e, CM listed. 
14.4.4.3 
Ethernet cables exterior to enclosures requiring high flex properties: Minimum 
requirements shall be 24 AWG / 0.25 mm² stranded, 250 Volts listed, 2 pair, 
twisted bonded pairs or equivalent, unshielded, TPE outer jacket, over-molded 
M12 D coded male connector on both ends, Rated Cat5e, CM listed.  
Manufacturer rated for 10 million cycles at 20 times the diameter bend radius. 
14.4.4.4 
Removed. 
14.4.4.5 
Ethernet receptacle assemblies installed on the exterior of an enclosure: 
Minimum requirements shall be 24 AWG / 0.25 mm² stranded, 250 V, 2 pair, 
twisted bonded pairs or equivalent, unshielded, TPE outer jacket, M12 D coded 
female receptacle on one end and a RJ45 male connector on the other end, 
Rated Cat5e, CM listed.   
14.4.4.6 
Ethernet cables interior to enclosures: Minimum requirements shall be 24 AWG / 
0.25 mm² stranded, 250 V, 4-pair, twisted bonded pairs or equivalent, 
unshielded, TPE outer jacket, RJ45 male connector on both ends, Rated Cat5e, 
CM listed. 
14.4.4.7 
Ethernet cable outer jacket color requirements:  Blue shall be used for static 
applications for control & I/O networks.  Orange shall be used for identifying High 
Flex rated cables for control & I/O networks.  Orange cables may be used 
instead of blue. 
14.4.4.8 
X-Code Ethernet Cables 
14.4.4.8.1 
X-Code ethernet cables shall only be used for applications that require gigabit 
speeds. 
14.4.4.8.2 
Where devices are manufactured with a X-Code connection and do require 
gigabit speeds, a X-Code to D-Code adapter shall be used.

SECTION 14 - CONTROLS

14.4.4.8.3 
X-Code ethernet cables shall be a minimum of 26 AWG / 0.14mm2, 250V listed, 
4 pair, unshielded twisted pairs, teal TPE outer jacket, over-molded M12 male 
connectors at both ends, rated minimum CAT6A, CM listed. 
14.4.4.8.4 
All X-Code ethernet cables shall be high flex rated, manufacturer rated at 10 
million cycles at 20 times the diameter bend radius.

14.4.5 
Power Cables 
Tier 1 Integrators shall provide data to show complete cross reference with the 
approved sources on the approved components list. 
Following color code shown in the controls drawings and shall meet any regional 
color requirements. 
14.4.5.1 
Welding Cable (weld control load side) to be IEC & NEC compliant, copper 
conductors, 90 Deg C Min., FT4, TC-ER, UL ,CSA & CE approved, TPE/XLPE 
outer jacket, numbered conductors with green/yellow ground static application.  
14.4.5.2 
Power Cable (distribution panel to weld control, robot control, drive panel) to be 
IEC & NEC compliant, copper conductors, 90 Deg C Min., FT4, TC-ER, UL, CSA 
& CE approved, 600V Min., TPE/CPE/TSE/PVC outer jacket, numbered 
conductors with green/yellow ground static application. 
14.4.5.3 
Single phase AC (110-260VAC) Distribution Cable shall be 90 Deg C Min., 600V, 
TPE jacket, TC-ER, UL ,CSA & CE approved bulk cable with the following color 
code shown in the controls drawings and shall meet any regional color 
requirements. 
14.4.5.4 
Items 1 & 2 outer jackets are to be black for static and yellow (pink in FSA) for 
flexing applications.

14.4.6 
Motor Control Cables 
Tier 1 Integrators shall provide data to show complete cross reference with the 
approved sources on the approved components list. 
NOTE: The use of plug-in connections on voltages 50v and above requires a City 
of Chicago exception to the Chicago Electrical Code. Contact Ford VOME 
Controls prior to the start of design.  
14.4.6.1 
Motor Cable (VFD applications; NON Rockwell), IEC & NEC compliant, copper 
conductors, 90 Deg C Min., FT4, TC-ER, UL ,CSA & CE approved, 600V Min, 
Shielded, TPE outer jacket, numbered conductors with green/yellow ground, both 
static and continuous flex applications. Motor only conductors and motor 
conductors with 16AWG / 1.5 mm² signal pair composite.  
14.4.6.2 
Motor Cable (motor starter applications), IEC & NEC compliant, copper 
conductors, 90 Deg C Min., FT4, TC-ER, UL ,CSA & CE approved, 600V Min, 
TPE outer jacket, numbered conductors with green/yellow ground, both static 
and continuous flex applications. 
14.4.6.3 
Motor Cable (Rockwell VFD applications), IEC & NEC compliant, copper 
conductors, 90 Deg C Min., FT4, TC-ER, UL ,CSA & CE approved, 600V Min, 
Shielded, TPE outer jacket, black numbered conductors with green/yellow 
ground, both static and continuous flex applications.  
14.4.6.4 
Items 1-3 outer jacket are to be black for static and yellow (pink in FSA) for 
flexing applications.  
14.4.6.5 
Encoder Cable, IEC & NEC compliant, copper conductors, 90 Deg C Min., FT4, 
TC-ER, UL ,CSA & CE approved, 300V Min, Shielded, TPE outer jacket, 
numbered conductors, conductor colors matching controls prints (and shall meet 
any regional color requirements), both static and continuous flex applications. 
Encoder cable min 18AWG / 1 mm².

14.5 
CHECKLIST 
14.5.1.1 
The Control Logix Design Checklist shall be completed for each Controls 
processor prior to releasing designs to build. 
14.5.1.2 
The Control Logix Build Checklist shall be completed for each Controls processor 
prior to Buy-Off and included in the Buy-Off Book. 
14.5.1.3 
The Logic Comparison Tool shall be used to complete the Control Logix Design 
Checklist and the Control Logix Build Checklist. See the Logic Comparison Tool

14.6 
INTELLIGENT DEVICES AND SOFTWARE TASK UPDATES 
 
14.6.1 
Intelligent Devices Firmware or Software:  
14.6.1.1 
Intelligent devices are those that contain a component with firmware or software. 
These devices shall be approved and maintained by Ford; placed on a “Ford 
Program Software - Hardware Revision Matrix” found in Teamcenter.  
14.6.1.1.1 
Each component supplier is responsible for getting intelligent devices approved 
by VO controls and placed on the “Ford Program Software - Hardware Revision 
Matrix” found in Teamcenter. 
14.6.1.1.2 
Each Integrator is responsible for getting any intelligent device items not listed on 
the “Ford Program Software - Hardware Revision Matrix” approved by VO 
controls and placed on the “Ford Program Software - Hardware Revision Matrix” 
found in Teamcenter. 
14.6.1.1.3 
Each Equipment Supplier is responsible for getting items not listed on the “Ford 
Program Software - Hardware Revision Matrix” approved by VO controls and 
placed on the “Ford Program Software - Hardware Revision Matrix” found in 
Teamcenter. 
14.6.1.1.4 
Each Integrator is responsible for updating all components’ software / firmware, 
as required, to comply with to the “Ford Program Software - Hardware Revision 
Matrix” found in Teamcenter. 
14.6.2 
Intelligent Device Level Verification: 
14.6.2.1.1 
No later than the start of power on, the VO Controls lead will issue the program 
software and firmware revision level requirements via the “Ford Program 
Software - Hardware Revision Matrix. 
14.6.2.1.2 
Upon power on, integrators shall verify and/or update all components to the 
proper level. 
14.6.2.1.3 
Integrator shall maintain a matrix documenting such verification. 
14.6.2.1.4 
Any new devices that are not documented via VO controls lead shall be added to 
the verification matrix and maintained by the Integrator. 
14.6.2.1.5 
Any updates required after power on shall be documented via a direction letter. 
14.6.3 
Program Control Task Update Process  
14.6.3.1 
The program Control Task Update Process shall be as follows: 
14.6.3.1.1 
The Ford VOME Controls standards supervisor will communicate via direction 
letter the initial controls logic template revision level to be loaded from 

 
 
 
 
Global Vehical Operations Structures Standard 
 
Issue Date: Jan. 08, 2021 
SECTION 14 - CONTROLS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
 
 
Page 29 of 34 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
  Printed copies are uncontrolled 
Teamcenter.  Timing:  No later than start of controls software design per the Tier 
1 Integrator timeline. 
14.6.3.1.2 
The Ford VOME Controls standards supervisor will communicate via direction 
letter, task level updates that are required prior to buy-off.  Timing:  No later than 
power on dates per Tier 1 Integrator time line. 
14.6.3.2 
Any task level update identified after power on will be held and implemented after 
the tool ships.  
14.6.3.2.1 
Exceptions: Updates required that affect the safe operation of the tool shall be 
completed before buy-off. 
14.6.3.2.2 
Exceptions: Updates required to facilitate an effective buy-off (including 2500 
cycles). 
14.6.3.3 
All task level updates required after tool shipment will be communicated via a 
single direction letter from the Ford VOME Controls manager.

14.7 
FIS 
14.7.1 
Machine Fault Development and Validation 
NOTE: The "Ford Developed Tools" referenced in this section are available in 
the Teamcenter. 
14.7.1.1 
The Tooling Machine Fault in FIS Development and Validation Flow Chart 
illustrates the process for developing and validating machine faults that will be 
used by FIS. 
14.7.1.2 
The Conveyor System Machine Fault in FIS Development and Validation Flow 
Chart illustrates the process for developing and validating conveyor faults that 
will be used by FIS. 
14.7.1.3 
If not using FTF PLC panels; the MPN Ethernet card or “P3” card will be line 
builder’s responsibility to purchase and install. If using FTF; the Tier 1 Integrators 
are to include the “P3” card option when ordering the processor panel for which 
the tool end user will be a stamping plant and FIS is not available. 
14.7.1.4 
Reference FIS THROUGHPUT CAPABILITY as an illustration of FIS data and 
constructs used for Throughput Capability Analysis.

14.8 
RFID TAGS 
14.8.1.1 
All pallet conveyors and delivery carriers (pallets, any overhead conveyance 
carrier including Facility P/F, Friction, EMS,...conveyors) shall include an RFID 
tag as specified for a given program. All conveyors shall include Tag Readers at 
the insertion points, extraction points and diversion points.  
14.8.1.2 
Tool integrators shall coordinate location of tag on carrier to accommodate a 
maintainable location for the tag reader.  
14.8.1.3 
RFID Tag data and bit level structure shall match the defined project shift register 
/ RFID tag document provided by Ford VOME Controls lead. Any modifications or 
additions shall be reviewed and approved by the Ford VOME Controls engineer 
and documented in the project shift register / RFID tag document.  
14.8.1.4 
Exceptions: Batch build, non-quality tracking and single model conveyors are 
excluded from this requirement if there is no Body Shop Scheduling (AVS) data 
available.

14.9 
PRIMARY, TRANSFORMER, AND SECONDARY CONDUCTORS 
(See Also GVOSS Section 6 Welding and Mechanical Fastening)  
14.9.1 
Welding Primary   
14.9.1.1 
Hard tool weld / manual guns: Transformer connectors (plug and socket type; 
i.e., RobiFix) shall be two-conductor + ground, flexible copper, 600 volts insulated 
with neoprene jacket, sized per welding current and duty cycle requirements; but 
will have a regional minimum of 2 AWG (N.A.) or 35 mm² (all other regions). 
14.9.1.2 
Robot weld / Robot ped weld: See GVOSS Section 13 Robots (Internal Dress 
Package Requirements). 
14.9.2 
Transformers   
14.9.2.1 
Only Ford Motor Company approved MFDC transformers shall be selected. Tier 
1 Integrators shall use for one transformer size for a given plant. 
14.9.2.2 
Transformers and secondary conductors shall be sized to provide the required 
secondary current specified by the nominal design weld schedule at maximum of 
80% of current output capacity as described in GVOSS Section 6. 
14.9.2.3 
Transformers with taps, the tap setting shall have the following settings: 
a. 
Minimum of two tap settings to go. 
b. 
Integral transformer with Hard Link Tap set to Tap 1. 
14.9.2.4 
All hard automation transformer brackets shall be identified with brass or lamicoid 
tags permanently attached and readily visible. Identification of transformer 
number shall be with 25 mm high letters and numbers on a 50 mm high tag. Tags 
shall be appropriately fastened.  See GVOSS Hardware template in Teamcenter. 
14.9.2.5 
Fixture type transformer mounting brackets shall be capable of supporting a 
transformer one size larger as defined below and have slots for ease of 
replacement to minimize downtime. See Ford Standard Drawing 56ZF-25116.  
a. 
Single Secondary B-1-S (50 KVA) with C-1-S (75 KVA) 
b. 
Dual Secondary B-1 (85 KVA) with C-1 (100 KVA) 
14.9.2.6 
Transformers of different voltage ratings shall be controlled separately. 
14.9.2.7 
All Tier 1 Integrators shall use heat shrink tube on any cable lug utilized for 
connection to the weld transformer primary terminals. The Tier 1 Integrators shall 
also verify that all primary connections are fully clear and isolated from grounded 
such as the transformer terminal enclose surfaces. 
 
14.9.3 
Welding Secondary Conductors   

 
 
 
 
Global Vehical Operations Structures Standard 
 
Issue Date: Jan. 08, 2021 
SECTION 14 - CONTROLS 
Revision Date: June 08, 2025 
 
 
 
Page 33 of 34 
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS 
  Printed copies are uncontrolled 
14.9.3.1 
Roll shunts are preferred over air-cooled or water-cooled cables. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
14.9.3.2 
When cables are used the following shall apply: 
a. Secondary cables for fixture guns shall be approved dry-type jumpers unless 
otherwise specified and shall be as short as possible. 
b. Secondary cable lengths shall not be longer than 24 inches (609.60 mm). 
c. All secondary cables shall be tied together with leather straps or other approved 
devices to reduce air gap between jumpers of opposite polarity and prevent 
excessive jumper vibration when carrying current. Plastic tie wraps shall not be 
used. 
d. The routing, length and area in MCM / mm² of welding shunts shall be shown on 
the design.  
e. Welding transformer secondary loops shall be balanced so that the maximum 
current difference between the two secondaries of any one transformer and 
between transformers with similar welding conditions shall not exceed 5% of the 
nominal designed weld current. All secondary welding circuits connected to a 
specific welding system shall be electrically balanced to each other. 
f. 
All single conductor cables passing through metal openings shall pass through in 
pairs (from the same phase opposite polarity) with no magnetic material between 
the cables. 
g. Water-cooled cables shall be piped downstream of the transformer, electrodes, 
etc. in the water circuit. 
h. Minimum size for air cooled welding cables shall be 750 MCM / 400 mm².  
14.9.3.3 
For push/pull applications, loop areas of the upper and lower portions of the 
secondary circuit shall be balanced, gun forces shall be equal, and the same size 
and type of transformer shall be used for both the upper and lower. 
14.9.3.4 
Secondary conductors including air-cooled cables, water-cooled cables, kickless 
cables, leaf shunts and buss bars shall be sized appropriately for the worst case 
continuous current requirement.

14.10 
CONTROLS PRODUCTION NETWORK (CPN) 
14.10.1.1 
The integrator shall reference the Scope of Work (SOW) for a list of devices 
types that shall be connected to the Manufacturing Production Network (MPN). 
14.10.1.2 
The integrator shall coordinate with the Ford Network Planning group, to acquire 
a list of static MPN IP addresses, to use on the MPN.   Note:  These lists are 
specific for each MPN drop. 
14.10.1.3 
The integrator shall design and validate the Controls Production Network (CPN) 
in accordance to GVOSS Programming Guide, and the latest GVOSS Controls 
Hardware Template. 
14.10.1.4 
The completed MPN router Network Address Translation (NAT) table document, 
as defined by the plant Network Planning Group, and a  copy of the router(s) 
configurations shall be stored in the same folder. This applies to the 
Programming Terminal and Teamcenter.  
14.10.1.5 
The integrator shall purchase hardware (router / switches / security devices), 
mount, and install the appropriate cabling for these connections excluding the 
MPN drop.
